source.subject: Next steps: Security runbook & MAP checkpoints for ClearPoint evaluation
messages:
From: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@clearpointbank.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@clearpointbank.com>, Eli Turner <eli.turner@auditpartner.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: ClearPoint evaluation — security gates and timeline

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our security team wants to formalize a short MAP so we can evaluate Redwood for a production pilot. High-level asks from our side: SOC 2 attestation (or timeline to ship evidence), SIG/CAIQ responses, an initial DPA we can redline, and confirmation of data retention/deletion SLAs for caching and logs.

Proposed cadence: kickoff the week of May 9, SIG bulk review the following week, DPA redline by end of week 3, SOC2 evidence review week 4. Can you share a short runbook/MAP with owners on your side and any standard documents you will attach?

Attaching our internal primer to give context.

Thanks,
Anna
-----
ClearPoint Security Primer (attachment): ClearPoint_Security_Primer.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@clearpointbank.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-02T11:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPoint evaluation — security gates and timeline

Anna — thanks for the clear ask and the primer. Attached is a concise MAP + runbook and our baseline artifacts to accelerate your review. High level plan below (happy to iterate):

- Week 0 (May 9): Kickoff call — agenda: scope, sequence lengths, data residency, and acceptance criteria. Owner: Ava (AM).
- Week 1 (May 10–14): SIG/CAIQ Q&A bulk delivery — Owner: Marcus (runtime/security tech lead). We'll provide an initial completed CAIQ spreadsheet and highlighted items for you to focus on.
- Week 2 (May 17–21): DPA draft + procurement coordination — Owner: Naomi (legal ops). We will deliver DPA_draft_v1.docx for redline and propose standard indemnities/retention clauses.
- Week 3 (May 24–28): SOC 2 evidence & operational questions — Owner: Ben (compliance). We will surface a redacted SOC 2 summary and evidence index.
- Week 4 (May 31–Jun 4): Acceptance review and go/no-go decision.

Key security touchpoints to confirm now:
- Data retention for KV cache and ephemeral runtime state (we propose TTL=30 days by default, configurable per-customer).
- Log retention: we retain operational logs for 12 months; customer-managed log exports available on request.
- DPA: we support deletion requests and can add explicit SLA language for deletion and attestations.

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_summary.pdf, DPA_draft_v1.docx, SIG-CAIQ-responses.xlsx

Links: https://docs.redwood.ai/shared/MAP-ClearPoint-2027 (MAP draft)

Next steps: confirm kickoff slot (I suggested Tue May 10 at 10am PT in HubSpot) and let us know if you need a private deployment discussion up front.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0182

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@clearpointbank.com>
Date: 2027-05-03T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPoint evaluation — security gates and timeline

Adding a few tech notes for the SIG call and MAP so we don't get downstream surprises:

- KV cache/data retention: our default TTL is 30 days but we can offer 0-day (no cache) or shorter windows for sensitive workloads; explain tradeoffs to the ClearPoint security team on latency/cost.
- Audit logs: we capture request/response hashes and runtime metadata; payloads are not stored unless explicitly enabled via integrations. Retention is configurable up to 24 months for enterprise.
- Private deployment option: if they need additional network isolation, our Private VPC option supports HSM/KMS integration and can be included in the MAP as an optional milestone.

I'll prepare the CAIQ notes and highlight any control gaps and compensating controls for the SIG review.

Marcus
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime

From: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@clearpointbank.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@clearpointbank.com>, Eli Turner <eli.turner@auditpartner.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-04T13:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPoint evaluation — security gates and timeline

Thanks Ava and Marcus — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifications from our security folks:

- Data deletion SLA: we need a firm contractual SLA for deletion after a termination request (target: 30 days). Can you confirm you will add explicit language to the DPA?
- SOC 2: a redacted summary is fine for now but our auditors will request the evidence index; can Ben confirm availability of the index for the SOC engagement week?

Also requesting that DPA_draft_v1.docx be delivered by end of business Friday (May 7) so our legal team can start redlining.

On Tue, May 2, 2027 at 11:40 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Anna — thanks for the clear ask and the primer. Attached is a concise MAP + runbook and our baseline artifacts to accelerate your review. High level plan below (happy to iterate):
> 
> - Week 0 (May 9): Kickoff call — agenda: scope, sequence lengths, data residency, and acceptance criteria. Owner: Ava (AM).
> - Week 1 (May 10–14): SIG/CAIQ Q&A bulk delivery — Owner: Marcus (runtime/security tech lead). We'll provide an initial completed CAIQ spreadsheet and highlighted items for you to focus on.
> - Week 2 (May 17–21): DPA draft + procurement coordination — Owner: Naomi (legal ops). We will deliver DPA_draft_v1.docx for redline and propose standard indemnities/retention clauses.
> - Week 3 (May 24–28): SOC 2 evidence & operational questions — Owner: Ben (compliance). We will surface a redacted SOC 2 summary and evidence index.
> - Week 4 (May 31–Jun 4): Acceptance review and go/no-go decision.

> Key security touchpoints to confirm now:
> - Data retention for KV cache and ephemeral runtime state (we propose TTL=30 days by default, configurable per-customer).
> - Log retention: we retain operational logs for 12 months; customer-managed log exports available on request.
> - DPA: we support deletion requests and can add explicit SLA language for deletion and attestations.
> 
> Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_summary.pdf, DPA_draft_v1.docx, SIG-CAIQ-responses.xlsx

Appreciate the quick turnaround — if you can confirm Friday delivery for the DPA, we will prioritize review.

Best,
Anna

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Lopez <anna.lopez@clearpointbank.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-06T16:11:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPoint evaluation — security gates and timeline

Anna — confirmed. Naomi and I will finalize the DPA_draft_v1.docx with the explicit 30-day deletion SLA language and send by EOD Friday (May 7). Ben will also upload the SOC 2 evidence index with redactions for review during the SOC engagement week — ETA May 24.

Quick action items:
- Naomi: finalize DPA and push to DocuSign link (https://docusign.example/send/CPB-DPA-2027).
- Marcus: finalize CAIQ highlights and share SIG worksheet.
- Ava: lock kickoff time in HubSpot and share calendar invite.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Compliance Manager | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
Attachments: DPA_draft_v1.docx (attached)

source.subject: Temporary BYOK retention window exception — coordination & product gaps
messages:
From: Andrea Smith <andrea.smith@cardinalsys.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence

Hi Ben,

Hope you’re well — we’re in the middle of a SOC2 readiness window and procurement has flagged a gap in Redwood’s standard BYOK retention behavior for Cardinal Systems. Our external auditors are asking for 30–90 days of retained audit events tied to key usage and KMS wrapping operations (they need exportable evidence for a sample period).

Summary of asks:
- Do you have a supported way to provide per-customer retained audit logs (key wrap/unwrap events) for a defined window?
- If not, can Redwood provide a short-term exception/process to retain and hand back evidence for the period May 1–May 31, 2027?
- Any required paperwork (exceptions form, DPA addendum, or procurement flow) we need to fast-track?

I attached our requirements PDF (cardinal_byok_requirements.pdf) with the auditor checklist. Procurement is pushing for a confirm/no-confirm by EOD today so we can update the audit plan.

Thanks — Andrea
Andrea Smith
Senior Security & Privacy Manager, Cardinal Systems
andrea.smith@cardinalsys.com
Attachment: cardinal_byok_requirements.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence

Folks — forwarding Cardinal’s ask (see below). TL;DR: they need retained audit evidence for key wrap/unwrap and KMS-related events for a one-month window to satisfy an audit. Procurement wants confirmation today.

Questions I need answered quickly:
- Can we produce an evidence bundle (audit log exports + signed attestation) without changing production retention behavior?
- Is there a safe temporary process (manual export + escrow) that legal/infra are comfortable with, or do we need product work / config changes?
- If a product change is required (per-customer retention override), rough estimate of scope and ETA.

Please reply with a one-paragraph recommendation and an owner. I’ll coordinate with Cardinal on any paperwork.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Andrea Smith <andrea.smith@cardinalsys.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>

[original message attached]

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 11:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence

Ben / team — quick technical triage below.

Summary of current state:
- Our BYOK flow (customer-managed keys) never stores plaintext keys; we keep cryptographic metadata and non-sensitive operational logs. The default retention for low-level KMS traces is short (7 days) and not intended for audit handback.
- We can export key-operation events (wrap/unwrap, authorization checks, request metadata) from the runtime logs, but that requires a manual extraction from the ops pipeline.

Recommendation (short-term, low-risk):
1) Manual evidence bundle: ops run an export for the requested date range (May 1–31) including key-operation events and correlated request IDs. We can redact any unrelated PII and provide a signed attestation describing what was extracted. Delivery: 3 business days if we prioritize.
2) Product path (medium-term): add a per-customer retention override + audit-exports feature. This is non-trivial: touches storage retention config, billing, and privacy controls; estimate 4–6 sprints with 2 engineers + infra work. Requires legal/contract review on long-term retention.
3) If procurement requires a formal exception for policy, we should pair the manual export with a short (30–90 day) documented exception and a DocuSign acceptance.

Action items / owners:
- Evidence export (ops): assign to Marcus (3 business days) — includes export + simple attestation template.
- Legal/Privacy: Kimberly to advise on wording and whether we can sign a short exception or need DPA addendum.
- Product: I’ll put a scoped ticket for per-customer retention and drop a note to roadmap — not blocking the audit but required for future cases.

I’ll post the SEC ticket after this thread and link it here.

— Jonas
Jonas Weber
Head of Security Engineering, Redwood Inference
Attachment: evidence_export_template.txt

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Andrea Smith <andrea.smith@cardinalsys.com>, procurement@cardinalsys.com
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 13:38:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence

Andrea — thanks for the fast heads-up and the PDF. Internal triage complete; here’s the agreed path so we can close this for procurement today.

What Redwood will do (short-term, agreed):
- Produce an evidence bundle containing exported key-operation events and correlated request-level logs for May 1–31, 2027. The bundle will be redacted for unrelated PII and accompanied by a signed attestation from Redwood Security describing the scope. (Owner: Marcus Lin; ETA: 3 business days.)

title: Temporary BYOK retention window exception — coordination & product gaps
preview:
From: Andrea Smith <andrea.smith@cardinalsys.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence

Hi Ben,

Hope you’re well — we’re in the middle of a SOC2 readiness window and procurement has flagged a gap in Redwood’s standard BYOK retention behavior for Cardinal Systems. Our external auditors are asking for 30–90 days of retained audit events tied to key usage and KMS wrapping operations (they need exportable evidence for a sample period).

Summary of asks:
- Do you have a supported way to provide per-customer retained audit logs (key wrap/unwrap events) for a defined window?
- If not, can Redwood provide a short-term exception/process to retain and hand back evidence for the period May 1–May 31, 2027?
- Any required paperwork (exceptions form, DPA addendum, or procurement flow) we need to fast-track?

I attached our requirements PDF (cardinal_byok_requirements.pdf) with the auditor checklist. Procurement is pushing for a confirm/no-confirm by EOD today so we can update the audit plan.

Thanks — Andrea
Andrea Smith
Senior Security & Privacy Manager, Cardinal Systems
andrea.smith@cardinalsys.com
Attachment: cardinal_byok_requirements.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence

Folks — forwarding Cardinal’s ask (see below). TL;DR: they need retained audit evidence for key wrap/unwrap and KMS-related events for a one-month window to satisfy an audit. Procurement wants confirmation today.

Questions I need answered quickly:
- Can we produce an evidence bundle (audit log exports + signed attestation) without changing production retention behavior?
- Is there a safe temporary process (manual export + escrow) that legal/infra are comfortable with, or do we need product work / config changes?
- If a product change is required (per-customer retention override), rough estimate of scope and ETA.

Please reply with a one-paragraph recommendation and an owner. I’ll coordinate with Cardinal on any paperwork.

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Andrea Smith <andrea.smith@cardinalsys.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>

[original message attached]


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 11:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Temporary BYOK retention exception request for SOC2 evidence

Ben / team — quick technical triage below.

Summary of current state:
- Our BYOK flow (customer-managed keys) never stores plaintext keys; we keep cryptographic metadata and non-sensitive operational logs. The default retention for low-level KMS traces is short (7 days) and not intended for audit handback.
- We can export key-operation events (wrap/unwrap, authorization checks, request metadata) from the runtime logs, but that requires a manual extraction from the ops pipeline.

Recommendation (short-term, low-risk):
1) Manual evidence bundle: ops run an export for the requested date range (May 1–31) including key-operation events and correlated request IDs. We can redact any unrelated PII and provide a signed attestation describing what was extracted. Delivery: 3 business days if we prioritize.
2) Product path (medium-term): add a per-customer retention override + audit-exports feature. This is non-trivial: touches storage retention config, billing, and privacy controls; estimate 4–6 sprints with 2 engineers + infra work. Requires legal/contract review on long-term retention.
3) If procurement requires a formal exception for policy, we should pair the manual export with a short (30–90 day) documented exception and a DocuSign acceptance.

Action items / owners:
- Evidence export (ops): assign to Marcus (3 business days) — includes export + simple attestation template.
- Legal/Privacy: Kimberly to advise on wording and whether we can sign a short exception or need DPA addendum.
- Product: I’ll put a scoped ticket for per-customer retention and drop a note to roadmap — not blocking the audit but required for future cases.

I’ll post the SEC ticket after this thread and link it here.

— Jonas
Jonas Weber
Head of Security Engineering, Redwood Inference
Attachment: evidence_export_template.txt

- To proceed we need a signed short-term exception: a one-page form confirming Cardinal requests Redwood to extract and retain evidence for the defined window and that Cardinal accepts responsibility for storing that evidence after delivery. We can send via DocuSign.

source.subject: Request: one-off credit forgiveness & sandbox restart for OliveTech POC
messages:
From: Sara Patel <sara@olivetech.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 1 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Trial expired — request for credits to restart POC

Hi Avery,

Quick background: our small engineering team at OliveTech was midway through a feature POC using Redwood last month. The sandbox expired two weeks ago while procurement routed our PO (we hit a weird internal approval hold). The POC is blocked — we need a short burst of credits to finish the eval so we can sign.

Can Redwood grant a one-time restart (enough credit to run the final 10 model runs — roughly 80–120k tokens)? We can sign a short MAP or PO as soon as procurement unblocks, but we need the sandbox reactivated for ~2–3 weeks to complete the tests.

I attached a small usage report (usage_report_may2027.csv) showing the runs left and expected token burn. If there are alternative options (shorter extension + staged restart), we can adapt. Budget is tight — we were close to selecting Redwood for latency/throughput reasons, so any help here is appreciated.

Thanks,
Sara Patel
Product Manager, OliveTech
Attachments: usage_report_may2027.csv (CSV, 12KB)
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@olivetech.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 1 May 2027 09:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Trial expired — request for credits to restart POC

Hi Sara — thanks for the quick note and for the usage attachment. 

Short answer: we can usually do a one-time make-good for pilot customers while procurement finishes. I need to loop in my manager (Neha) and finance (Marcus) for approval on the exact amount. My initial recommendation: 100k tokens + 21 days of sandbox access so your team can finish the tests and sign the MAP/PO. If you think that's not enough, share the expected runs breakdown (you already did — looks like ~80–120k) and a target sign date.

Next steps from our side:
- I will propose a one-time credit (100k) internally and ask for a conditional approval tied to a signed MAP or PO within 30 days.
- Can procurement share a tentative PO ETA? If it's >30 days we may need finance to treat it as a credit with collection terms.

I’ll follow up once Neha/Marcus weigh in.

—Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0187
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 2 May 2027 08:14:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: OliveTech — trial credit forgiveness request / recommendation

Forwarding OliveTech request — they lost access when their sandbox expired because procurement was delayed. Key facts: customer is a mid-stage startup (seed + Series A MRR), POC is production-relevant (latency+throughput win), likely 1–2 seat expansion after sign.

My recommended posture: grant a one-time 100k-token credit and reopen their sandbox for 21 days. Condition: signed Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or PO within 30 days. Rationale: small short-term cost, high chance of conversion and expansion; preserves sales momentum.

Estimated short-term cost impact: 100k tokens @ typical model mix ~= $75–120 (gross cost) — negligible vs value. If approved, I’ll record deal_id DL-2027-3819 and request a billing hold so finance knows to expect PO/collection.

Attachment: usage_report_may2027.csv (from customer)

—Avery
From: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: OliveTech — trial credit forgiveness request / recommendation

Thanks for the context, Avery. I’m comfortable approving a conditional make-good. Approve 100k tokens and a 21-day sandbox reactivation under these conditions:

1) OliveTech signs the attached MAP (olive_MAP_2027.docx) or provides a PO within 30 calendar days of today (May 3, 2027).
2) We log deal_id DL-2027-3819 in HubSpot and set a 30-day billing follow-up task on the account owner.
3) Finance (Marcus) places a billing hold/flag and does not waive invoicing — this is a credit advance contingent on receipt of PO.

Marcus — please confirm you’ve noted the deal and can place a billing hold. Once Marcus confirms, Avery please execute the sandbox reopen and send the MAP/DocuSign link to OliveTech.

—Neha
Neha Kapoor
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
neha_kapoor@redwood.com
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@olivetech.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 15:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Trial expired — request for credits to restart POC

Sara — good news. Approved: Redwood will apply a one-time credit of 100,000 tokens and reopen your sandbox for 21 days effective now. Conditions (per our policy):

• MAP or PO signature required within 30 days (by June 2, 2027).
• We’ll log this as deal DL-2027-3819 in our system.
• Finance will place a billing hold — if the PO is not received in 30 days, Redwood reserves the right to invoice the advance credit.

Next steps for you:
1) Click this DocuSign link to review & sign the MAP: https://app.redwood.com/docs/mutual-action-plan/DL-2027-3819
2) Reply with an expected PO ETA (if procurement is close, say estimated date).

I’ve reopened the sandbox and applied the credit. You should see environmental access restored within the hour. Attached is the MAP and a short note from Neha confirming the conditional approval.

Thanks — excited to get you back on the platform and across the finish line.

Best,
Avery
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0187

> On Mon, May 3, 2027 at 10:02, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Thanks for the context, Avery. I’m comfortable approving a conditional make-good. Approve 100k tokens and a 21-day sandbox reactivation under these conditions:
> >
> > 1) OliveTech signs the attached MAP (olive_MAP_2027.docx) or provides a PO within 30 calendar days of today (May 3, 2027).
> > 2) We log deal_id DL-2027-3819 in HubSpot and set a 30-day billing follow-up task on the account owner.
> > 3) Finance (Marcus) places a billing hold/flag and does not waive invoicing — this is a credit advance contingent on receipt of PO.
> >
> > —Neha
> >
Attachments: olive_MAP_2027.docx (DOCX, 28KB)

Short answer: we can usually do a one-time make-good for pilot customers while procurement finishes. I need to loop in my manager (Neha) and finance (Marcus) for approval on the exact amount. My initial recommendation: 100k tokens + 21 days of sandbox access so your team can finish the tests and sign the MAP/PO. If you think that's not enough, share the expected runs breakdown (you already did — looks like ~80–120k) and a target sign date.

Next steps from our side:
- I will propose a one-time credit (100k) internally and ask for a conditional approval tied to a signed MAP or PO within 30 days.
- Can procurement share a tentative PO ETA? If it's >30 days we may need finance to treat it as a credit with collection terms.

I’ll follow up once Neha/Marcus weigh in.

—Avery

My recommended posture: grant a one-time 100k-token credit and reopen their sandbox for 21 days. Condition: signed Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or PO within 30 days. Rationale: small short-term cost, high chance of conversion and expansion; preserves sales momentum.

Estimated short-term cost impact: 100k tokens @ typical model mix ~= $75–120 (gross cost) — negligible vs value. If approved, I’ll record deal_id DL-2027-3819 and request a billing hold so finance knows to expect PO/collection.

—Avery

Subject: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — GlyphData is excited to run a two-month pilot with Redwood for our inference-backed analytics. Procurement asked me to confirm whether we can be onboarded on an invoice-only basis for the first 90 days (no PO). Our ERP takes time to provision new vendor IDs, and we'd like to start billing sooner.

Attached is the draft SOW and a sample invoice format procurement wants to use. Can Redwood accept invoice-only for a pilot under $12k total, with a short-term W-9 and insurance cert on file?

Sincerely,
Maya Patel
Head of Data Partnerships
GlyphData Inc.

Attachments: GlyphData_SOW_v1.pdf (pdf), pilot_invoice_sample.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Team — forwarding GlyphData inquiry. Key asks: invoice-only for 90 days, pilot cap ~$12k, W-9 and insurance provided.

Quick asks:
- Priya: Is this eligible for our temporary invoice-only path? Any red flags on insurance/W-9?
- Marcus: If we allow invoice-only, what guardrails (thresholds, payment terms, collections) do we want?
- Laura: Finance — approve temporary exception subject to controls?

I think pilot looks low-risk, but want formal sign-off before confirming.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Maya Patel <maya@glyphdata.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@glyphdata.com
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Alex / team —

Quick checklist for an invoice-only pilot (based on current playbook):
1) Pilot cap: <= $15,000 (one-off) — ok to approve without PO if other conditions met.
2) Required evidence before start: signed SOW, W-9, COI (insurance).
3) Payment terms: Net 30; require procurement contact and billing email.
4) Risk controls: tag account as "invoice-only pilot" in ERP; 30-day internal review; no auto-renew without PO.
5) Collections: finance to send reminder at 45 days; if unpaid at 60 days, suspend access.

Notes/Questions:
- Insurance: the COI template must show general liability and cyber coverage >= $1M.
- Currency/Tax: assume USD; if GlyphData requires foreign tax docs, escalate.

If Glyph provides the docs and SOW is within the cap, we can move forward. Marcus, Laura — any objections?

Thanks,
Priya Natarajan
Vendor Intake & Procurement
Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 May 2027 10:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

Agree with Priya's checklist. A couple of additions from ops perspective:
- Threshold: recommend we set the operational threshold at $12,500 for a single pilot (to match their ask and leave headroom).
- Access gating: create a temporary API key with a quota cap (500k tokens / month) tied to the pilot SOW.
- Billing: require procurement billing contact in the SOW and a valid email to receive invoices; use explicit invoice subject including "Pilot — GlyphData — Redwood" to avoid routing delays.
- Escalation: if invoice not paid within 45 days, ops should suspend the API key; collections to cc account owner and procurement team.

I'll prepare the short form exception approval packet for Laura to sign.

Marcus Lin
Platform Operations
Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 May 2027 14:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

All — approved with conditions below.
1) Pilot cap approved at $12,500 total.
2) Required docs (signed SOW, W-9, COI with required coverage) must be uploaded to vendor profile before invoices are issued.
3) Net 30 billing; finance to send 2 reminders at 30 and 45 days, after which access will be suspended.
4) No automatic conversion to paid subscription. A PO and legal review required for any renewal or scale beyond $12.5k.

Alex — please confirm to GlyphData and request procurement to send the W-9 and COI. Marcus/Priya — I'll sign the exception packet once you attach it to the ticket.

Laura Bennett
Director, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>; Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>; Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Subject: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — can we do invoice-only for first 90 days?

messages:
From: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@acmehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 15:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC inquiry — next steps

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the intro — were excited to kick off a short sandbox trial and hands-on session to validate routing rules and prompt behavior for our claims assistant. Can we target a kickoff + working session next week? Ideally a 90-minute slot that includes: a short walkthrough, live prompt review, and a short routing/load test (simulated traffic, ~10-20 concurrent sessions).

A few constraints on our side: most of the product team is on ET, but we can shift to PT mornings. Also, well need a quick decision at the end on whether to continue on Hosted or request a Dedicated trial for the month.

Attaching a one-pager with customer goals (sent separately).

Best,
Lina
Lina Gomez
Director, Product — Acme Health

---
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@acmehealth.com>
Cc: poc-team@acmehealth.com
Date: Sat, 01 May 2027 08:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC inquiry — next steps

Hi Lina — thanks, this is perfect context. We have availability next week but a couple of folks on Redwoods side are booked on Mon/Tue. Would May 3 (Mon) 10:00 AM PT / 1:00 PM ET work for a 90-minute kickoff + hands-on? Agenda suggestion:

- 15m: introductions & goals
- 25m: prompt review + examples (well share a short prompt checklist)
- 25m: routing and fallback policy discussion + demo
- 25m: load test (10-20 concurrent sessions) and token budget review

If that slot is tough, propose alternative times and well rework. Ive also attached a tiny CSV (trial_params.csv) with a couple of optional trial configs (token budget, expected concurrency) so we can pre-provision quotas.

Quick Q: for the load test, what peak concurrent users should we simulate (per region)? Also let me know any IP ranges for an allowlist.

Thanks — Ill loop in Dev (solutions) and Marcus (SRE) to prep capacity options.

Best,
Aisha Rahn
Aisha Rahman | Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0132

---
From: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@acmehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 01 May 2027 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC inquiry — next steps

On Sat, May 1, 2027 at 8:42 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Lina  thanks, this is perfect context. We have availability next week but a couple of folks on Redwoods side are booked on Mon/Tue. Would May 3 (Mon) 10:00 AM PT / 1:00 PM ET work for a 90-minute kickoff + hands-on?

Thanks Aisha — May 3 at 10am PT works for our core team. For concurrency: please simulate 15 concurrent sessions from the east coast (US-E) and 5 concurrent from US-W; token budget estimate: 120k tokens for the session (including reruns). IP range for allowlist: 198.51.100.0/24.

Also: can you outline the difference well see during the POC between Hosted and Dedicated? Were particularly concerned about predictable throughput for the load test and whether rate limits will throttle simulated users.

Thanks,
Lina

---
From: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 01 May 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: INTERNAL: quick capacity prep for ACME POC

Team,

Ill pre-authorize a temporary quota bump on Hosted for the May 3 session to avoid rate-limit surface; specifics:
- bump concurrency to 25 for the trial window (one-hour test window)
- increase per-minute token throughput to 200k tokens/min for test bursts
- prepare a short Dedicated vs Hosted checklist we can run through live (latency/guarantees/observability differences)

Marcus: can you stage a test VM in us-east to seed 15 concurrent connections and run the routing policy at test time?
Lauren: once Ive updated the quota Ill post the internal approval thread here — please confirm were good on auditing/log capture for the session.

Ive attached a quick capacity model (capacity_model.xlsx) with the proposed numbers. Will mark the change as temporary and auto-revert 24 hours after the session.

Thanks,
Dev
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

---
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@acmehealth.com>
Cc: poc-team@acmehealth.com, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 01 May 2027 11:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC inquiry — next steps

Hi Lina — great, May 3 at 10:00 AM PT is confirmed. Ive sent a calendar invite to your poc-team alias (please accept). Quick logistics and what to expect:

- Redwood attendees: Aisha (AE), Dev (Solutions), Marcus (SRE), and Lauren (Product Ops) - well handle routing config, short kernel-level perf checks, and token-budgeting.
- Well start with prompt review: please have 6-8 representative prompts (happy to take them in advance).
- For routing/load test: well simulate 15 concurrent from us-east + 5 from us-west (per your note).
- Quotas: Dev has already proposed a temporary bump (25 concurrency / 200k tokens/min burst). We will auto-revert 24 hours after the session.
- Hosted vs Dedicated: during the session well show a side-by-side: Hosted gives faster setup and built-in autoscaling but is subject to shared tenancy rate limits; Dedicated provides isolated GPU pools and SLA-backed throughput for sustained high-concurrency runs. Well surface cost delta and how routing policies fallback between variants.

Action items before the call:
- Lina / Acme: share the 6 prompts + any slow-response examples by EOD Mon, 5/2. Confirm IP allowlist (198.51.100.0/24).
- Dev / Marcus / Lauren: confirm capacity bump is staged and audit logging is enabled.

Attachments: capacity_model.xlsx (capacity plan), trial_params.csv (trial configuration).

See you Monday — Ill include a short runbook in the invite with the exact test steps.

Best,
Aisha Rahn
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Aisha@redwood.com

---
From: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@acmehealth.com>

source.subject: Split billing and feature mapping for multi-team rollout
messages:
From: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Finnotech Procurement <procurement@finnotech.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week — Finnotech is excited about Redwood's latency and the KV cache savings for our ingestion pipelines. Before we engage procurement, a couple of packaging/ops questions from our side that will determine which route we take:

1) We have two engineering teams that will consume the service: Payments (latency sensitive, will need Dedicated capacity) and Data Science (research, okay on Hosted). Can Redwood issue split invoices (two invoice entities) under one master account so we can allocate spend to different BU budgets?

2) The Payments team wants the ability to "swap" seats between tiers mid-term (e.g., move 10 seats from Hosted Pro to Dedicated Lite), and we need to understand credit/proration rules and whether seat counts can be pooled across projects. How does Redwood handle upgrades/downgrades and partial-term credits?

3) We saw references to a "feature lattice" — a compact mapping of which features (streaming, function calling, shard-level isolation, longer context) are in which tier. Could you share the latest mapping and whether feature bundles can be purchased à la carte?

4) Any lift on discounts for committing to a 12-month blended commitment across both teams?

Attached is our internal MAT (high level). Procurement may follow with the standard questionnaire. Curious about timeline — procurement prefers a single agreement with addendum for the Dedicated pool if possible.

Appreciate any concrete options/examples we can show leadership during procurement.

Best,
Ella Novak
Director of AI Platforms
Finnotech

Attachment: Finnotech_MAT.pdf (PDF, 1 page - high-level environment diagram)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Hi Ella — thanks for the clear list. Short answers up front, then a couple of concrete options:

- Split invoicing: supported. We can create invoice aliases under one master account; each alias is a separate invoice (and GL code) and ties back to the same account ID. That covers your BU allocation ask.

- Seat swaps & proration: upgrades (Hosted -> Dedicated or tier upgrade) are prorated; we apply pro-rated credits for the unused portion of the previous tier and bill the delta for the remainder of the term. Downgrades are supported at term boundary only (no mid-term automatic downshift) unless you allow us to apply a small administrative fee. Seat pooling across projects is possible via "Seat Reserve" add-on (one-time config). That allows reassigning seats across invoice aliases without multiple invoices — good for dynamic teams.

- Feature lattice: we maintain a matrix; some features (long-context KV cache, regional pinning, dedicated NVMe caching) are Dedicated-only. Streaming & function calling are available on Hosted Pro and Dedicated. We can produce a one-page feature-lattice PDF and an a-la-carte price list; linked is the draft: https://drive.redwood.ai/feature-lattice-v2

- Discounts: yes, we typically provide a blended discount for a 12-month multi-product commit. Exact % depends on total committed spend and whether you take a minimum Dedicated baseline.

Two Suggested Paths (examples you can present to procurement):

1) Master account + invoice aliases (recommended):
   - Create 2 invoice aliases: PAYMENTS (Dedicated pool) and DS (Hosted).
   - Commit to 12 months, 40% of total spend in Dedicated baseline -> eligible for blended 15% discount.
   - Seat Reserve add-on ($2,000 one-time) enables mid-term reassignments between aliases; upgrades prorate automatically.

2) Single invoice, per-project tags:
   - Single invoice with internal cost tags; easier contracting but billing allocation is manual for you.
   - Lower admin overhead but no per-invoice GL separation.

I'll attach a sample quote for option 1 with rough numbers for 10 Dedicated seats + 30 Hosted seats. Want me to loop in finance for an exact commitment quote?

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya_rao@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0123

Attachment: split-billing-quote-AD-20270410.pdf (PDF, 2 pages - illustrative pricing)

On Sat, Apr 10, 2027 at 09:12 AM Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com> wrote:
> Hi Aditya,
> 
> Thanks again for the demo last week — Finnotech is excited about Redwood's latency...

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Hi Ella — looping in Marcus and adding a couple of commercial points.

If Finnotech is open to a 12-month commitment with a minimum Dedicated baseline (we typically require a 20% floor on Dedicated in blended deals), we can extend an additional 3% discount on top of the blended banding in Aditya's note. We can also include one month of no-cost seat swaps (up to 10 swaps) as a play to simplify your initial ramp.

We prefer to avoid mid-term downgrades unless it's a contract-year adjustment, but with the Seat Reserve add-on we can be flexible about reassigning capacity between aliases.

If procurement needs it, we can encode those allowances in an addendum (deal id D-2027-0451). Happy to run the exact math; if you're free, let's set a 30-minute sync this week to walk procurement through the proration examples.

Best,
Lauren Bishop
Head of Mid-Market Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

> From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
> Date: Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 10:22 AM
> Subject: Re: Split billing + feature mapping...

From: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Finnotech Procurement <procurement@finnotech.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Thanks for the clarity — this helps. A couple of follow-ups from procurement:

- Is the Seat Reserve add-on a one-time paid configuration (you mentioned $2k) and does it require changes to the MSA?
- For proration: can you provide an example invoice line showing how a 10-seat upgrade mid-term would look (numbers + credits) so our finance team can reconcile?
- We need to confirm whether token usage for the Payments team (on Dedicated) will be billed at the same per-token rate as Hosted Pro or at a different rate.

Procurement is attaching the standard questionnaire; also interested in your suggested contract language for allowing invoice aliases.

We'll aim for an internal decision next week once we have a sample invoice and the feature lattice.

Cheers,
Ella

Attachment: procurement_questionnaire.xlsx (XLSX, 6 sheets)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Ella — appreciated. A few operational guarantees and a recommended rollout plan from our side to make procurement comfortable:

1) Seat Reserve: one-time configuration fee ($2,000). No MSA amendment required for the configuration itself; we include the add-on in the SOW (exhibits). If an alias requires separate legal entity terms, that's handled via an addendum.

2) Proration example (illustrative):
   - Term length: 12 months, monthly billing cadence.
   - Customer has 30 Hosted seats at $X/seat/month and adds 10 Dedicated seats mid-term at $Y/seat/month.
   - We credit the unused portion of the Hosted seat rate for the exact days unused and bill Dedicated at the Dedicated rate for the remainder of the billing period. The invoice line shows: "Prorated credit - Hosted seats" (negative line), "Prorated charge - Dedicated seats" (positive line), and a single net line per alias. We will provide a sample PDF invoice with concrete numbers this afternoon.

3) Token rates: Dedicated pools carry a different per-token economics based on reserved capacity. For short-context, token rates are similar to Hosted Pro; for high-throughput or NVMe-backed configs, pricing will reflect the Dedicated baseline. We'll quote exact token/unit numbers in the sample quote.

Recommended rollout (minimize disruption):
   - Week 0: Create master account + aliases (PAYMENTS, DS); provision Dedicated Lite pool for Payments team with Seat Reserve.
   - Week 1: Mirror API keys and tags, run smoke tests.
   - Month 1–2: Use monthly usage reports to validate tag-to-invoice attribution; claim up to 10 no-cost seat reassignments to cover initial shuffle.

I'll circulate the sample invoice + final feature lattice link (PDF) and include the exact blended discount numbers tied to a 12-month commit. If this plan looks good, Aditya will follow up with the formal quote and SOW.

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Director, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

> On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 11:48 AM Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com> wrote:
> > Thanks for the clarity — this helps. A couple of follow-ups from procurement...

Ella — appreciated. A few operational guarantees and a recommended rollout plan from our side to make procurement comfortable:

1) Seat Reserve: one-time configuration fee ($2,000). No MSA amendment required for the configuration itself; we include the add-on in the SOW (exhibits). If an alias requires separate legal entity terms, that's handled via an addendum.

2) Proration example (illustrative):
   - Term length: 12 months, monthly billing cadence.
   - Customer has 30 Hosted seats at $X/seat/month and adds 10 Dedicated seats mid-term at $Y/seat/month.
   - We credit the unused portion of the Hosted seat rate for the exact days unused and bill Dedicated at the Dedicated rate for the remainder of the billing period. The invoice line shows: \"Prorated credit - Hosted seats\" (negative line), \"Prorated charge - Dedicated seats\" (positive line), and a single net line per alias. We will provide a sample PDF invoice with concrete numbers this afternoon.

3) Token rates: Dedicated pools carry a different per-token economics based on reserved capacity. For short-context, token rates are similar to Hosted Pro; for high-throughput or NVMe-backed configs, pricing will reflect the Dedicated baseline. We'll quote exact token/unit numbers in the sample quote.

Recommended rollout (minimize disruption):

   - Week 1: Mirror API keys and tags, run smoke tests.
   - Month 1–2: Use monthly usage reports to validate tag-to-invoice attribution; claim up to 10 no-cost seat reassignments to cover initial shuffle.

text:
From: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Finnotech Procurement <procurement@finnotech.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week — Finnotech is excited about Redwood's latency and the KV cache savings for our ingestion pipelines. Before we engage procurement, a couple of packaging/ops questions from our side that will determine which route we take:

1) We have two engineering teams that will consume the service: Payments (latency sensitive, will need Dedicated capacity) and Data Science (research, okay on Hosted). Can Redwood issue split invoices (two invoice entities) under one master account so we can allocate spend to different BU budgets?

2) The Payments team wants the ability to \"swap\" seats between tiers mid-term (e.g., move 10 seats from Hosted Pro to Dedicated Lite), and we need to understand credit/proration rules and whether seat counts can be pooled across projects. How does Redwood handle upgrades/downgrades and partial-term credits?

3) We saw references to a \"feature lattice\" — a compact mapping of which features (streaming, function calling, shard-level isolation, longer context) are in which tier. Could you share the latest mapping and whether feature bundles can be purchased à la carte?

4) Any lift on discounts for committing to a 12-month blended commitment across both teams?

Attached is our internal MAT (high level). Procurement may follow with the standard questionnaire. Curious about timeline — procurement prefers a single agreement with addendum for the Dedicated pool if possible.

Appreciate any concrete options/examples we can show leadership during procurement.

Best,
Ella Novak
Director of AI Platforms
Finnotech

Attachment: Finnotech_MAT.pdf (PDF, 1 page - high-level environment diagram)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Ella Novak <ella.novak@finnotech.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Split billing + seat migration questions for multi-team rollout

Hi Ella — thanks for the clear list. Short answers up front, then a couple of concrete options:

- Split invoicing: supported. We can create invoice aliases under one master account; each alias is a separate invoice (and GL code) and ties back to the same account ID. That covers your BU allocation ask.

- Seat swaps & proration: upgrades (Hosted -> Dedicated or tier upgrade) are prorated; we apply pro-rated credits for the unused portion of the previous tier and bill the delta for the remainder of the term. Downgrades are supported at term boundary only (no mid-term automatic downshift) unless you allow us to apply a small administrative fee. Seat pooling across projects is possible via \"Seat Reserve\" add-on (one-time config). That allows reassigning seats across invoice aliases without multiple invoices — good for dynamic teams.

- Feature lattice: we maintain a matrix; some features (long-context KV cache, regional pinning, dedicated NVMe caching) are Dedicated-only. Streaming & function calling are available on Hosted Pro and Dedicated. We can produce a one-page feature-lattice PDF and an a-la-carte price list; linked is the draft: https://drive.redwood.ai/feature-lattice-v2

- Discounts: yes, we typically provide a blended discount for a 12-month multi-product commit. Exact % depends on total committed spend and whether you take a minimum Dedicated baseline.

Two Suggested Paths (examples you can present to procurement):

1) Master account + invoice aliases (recommended):
   - Create 2 invoice aliases: PAYMENTS (Dedicated pool) and DS (Hosted).
   - Commit to 12 months, 40% of total spend in Dedicated baseline -> eligible for blended 15% discount.
   - Seat Reserve add-on ($2,000 one-time) enables mid-term reassignments between aliases; upgrades prorate automatically.

2) Single invoice, per-project tags:
   - Single invoice with internal cost tags; easier contracting but billing allocation is manual for you.
   - Lower admin overhead but no per-invoice GL separation.

I'll attach a sample quote for option 1 with rough numbers for 10 Dedicated seats + 30 Hosted seats. Want me to loop in finance for an exact commitment quote?

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya_rao@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0123

Attachment: split-billing-quote-AD-20270410.pdf (PDF, 2 pages - illustrative pricing)

source.subject: IvyWorks availability exception & gating — product / ops asks
messages:
From: Emma Park <emma@ivyworks.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@ivyworks.com
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We finished a brief internal technical review and have one request before we sign: IvyWorks needs a narrowly scoped availability commitment for the /chat/generate endpoint during US business hours (M-F, 8am-6pm PT). Specifically we want a contractual p95 response-time commitment at or below 80ms for our production key path for the first 12 months.

We understand this is outside standard terms — we're open to a higher-tier Reserved / Dedicated plan and a short ramp schedule. Can you outline what Redwood would need to agree to operationally and in contract terms? I've attached our SLO spreadsheet and a one-page mutual action plan.

Best,
Emma
CTO, IvyWorks

[Attachment: IvyWorks-SLO-request.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]
[Attachment: IvyWorks-MAP-v1.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 10:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Team — forwarding IvyWorks' ask. Quick summary: customer wants a targeted p95 <= 80ms for /chat/generate during US business hours for 12 months. They're willing to go to Dedicated and accept a ramp. Legal (Priya) will need to weigh in on risk language. Ops/Runtime (Marcus) — can we provide guidance today on whether this is feasible, and what guardrails / runbook items we would require? CS (Grace) — can you add expected QPS and concurrency + any historical traffic patterns?

Goal: have a short internal decision (yes/no + required conditions) by EOD tomorrow so I can respond to Emma with a proposed path (Dedicated capacity + warm-up + canary + formal runbook).

Thanks — Ava
Product, Redwood Inference

> On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 9:02 AM Emma Park <emma@ivyworks.com> wrote:
> > (see above)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 14:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Short answer: doable with constraints. Technical notes below — high level first:

- Feasible path: Dedicated GPU pool pinned to their tenant for business-hours traffic + pre-warmed KV cache + request routing to low-latency nodes in us-west region. Expect realistic steady-state p95 ~60-90ms for small-gen prompts if we (1) reserve capacity for peak concurrency they expect and (2) prevent cold-starts during hours.
- Hard guardrails: we need a clearly defined traffic envelope (QPS, concurrency, prompt length distribution). If they spike outside agreed envelope, commitment pauses until manual approval.
- Operational asks:
  * Pre-warm schedule + synthetic load test during canary
  * Dedicated monitoring dashboard + alerting thresholds for p95 and queue length
  * Incident response SLA for paging on real p95 breach (who to oncall)
  * A fallback model variant with broader latency but lower cost (automated fallback)

Attachments: rough ops runbook sketch attached. If legal requires a monetary SLA credit we need to size it against committed reserved capacity rather than per-token.

I'll need CS numbers from Grace to give capacity sizing. Also: we should avoid blanket "sub-80ms for all endpoints" language — make it specific to /chat/generate with defined max tokens and input size.

- Marcus
Engineering — Serving Runtime

[Attachment: proposed-ops-runbook.md (text/markdown)]

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks — adding customer context. IvyWorks is a strategic SMB from our Q1 book; current daily usage is ~30k tokens/day with a predictable diurnal pattern. Expected ramp: 2-3x over 6 weeks post-launch. Peak concurrency during business hours hits ~18 concurrent sessions today. Average prompt length: 40 tokens. They are adamant about p95 for the specific path they showed in the demo (short prompt, single-turn).

Notes for negotiation: they prefer a single-line SLO in the contract rather than a long appendix. That makes our engineering ask of a narrowly-scoped SLO even more important. Suggest we offer:
  - Commitment: p95 <= 85ms for /chat/generate (max input tokens 64, max output tokens 128) during M-F 8am-6pm PT
  - Preconditions: Dedicated capacity reservation (3x current concurrency as initial commit) + 7-day warm-up with synthetic traffic + agreed-upon ramp cap and an automatic throttle if envelope exceeded
  - Measurement: Redwood's telemetry on the dedicated pool; daily digests to IvyWorks and a monthly compliance report

If legal is okay with language around "service credit proportional to reserved capacity for verifiable breaches", I think we can close quickly.

— Grace
Customer Success

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks all — flagged legal considerations below. High-level: we can accept a narrowly-scoped availability/latency commitment if the following appear in the contract:

1) Explicit scope definition (endpoint, token limits, business hours).
2) Traffic envelope and failure modes: a clause that excludes outages caused by customer-initiated traffic above the envelope, abuse, or third-party network failures.
3) Limited SLA credit: define credits as percentage of monthly reserved fee (caps + tiers) rather than per-token.
4) Exit & review cadence: 90-day operational review clause to evaluate the SLO post-ramp; right to adjust terms if we see abuse or unanticipated costs.
5) Auditability: Redwood's telemetry will be the source of truth; provide sample report format to customer.

If product and engineering approve the operational guardrails Marcus outlined, legal can draft a short SLO addendum and redline the MAP. I recommend we avoid open-ended language like "guaranteed sub-80ms" without the other constraints.

I'll prepare a first-pass SLO addendum draft once Marcus confirms capacity sizing and Grace confirms QPS ramp.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

[Attachment: IvyWorks-SLO-request.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]
[Attachment: IvyWorks-MAP-v1.pdf (application/pdf)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 10:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Team — forwarding IvyWorks' ask. Quick summary: customer wants a targeted p95 <= 80ms for /chat/generate during US business hours for 12 months. They're willing to go to Dedicated and accept a ramp. Legal (Priya) will need to weigh in on risk language. Ops/Runtime (Marcus) — can we provide guidance today on whether this is feasible, and what guardrails / runbook items we would require? CS (Grace) — can you add expected QPS and concurrency + any historical traffic patterns?

Goal: have a short internal decision (yes/no + required conditions) by EOD tomorrow so I can respond to Emma with a proposed path (Dedicated capacity + warm-up + canary + formal runbook).

Thanks — Ava
Product, Redwood Inference

> On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 9:02 AM Emma Park <emma@ivyworks.com> wrote:
> > (see above)


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 14:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Short answer: doable with constraints. Technical notes below — high level first:

- Feasible path: Dedicated GPU pool pinned to their tenant for business-hours traffic + pre-warmed KV cache + request routing to low-latency nodes in us-west region. Expect realistic steady-state p95 ~60-90ms for small-gen prompts if we (1) reserve capacity for peak concurrency they expect and (2) prevent cold-starts during hours.
- Hard guardrails: we need a clearly defined traffic envelope (QPS, concurrency, prompt length distribution). If they spike outside agreed envelope, commitment pauses until manual approval.
- Operational asks:
  * Pre-warm schedule + synthetic load test during canary
  * Dedicated monitoring dashboard + alerting thresholds for p95 and queue length
  * Incident response SLA for paging on real p95 breach (who to oncall)
  * A fallback model variant with broader latency but lower cost (automated fallback)

Attachments: rough ops runbook sketch attached. If legal requires a monetary SLA credit we need to size it against committed reserved capacity rather than per-token.

I'll need CS numbers from Grace to give capacity sizing. Also: we should avoid blanket "sub-80ms for all endpoints" language — make it specific to /chat/generate with defined max tokens and input size.

- Marcus
Engineering — Serving Runtime

[Attachment: proposed-ops-runbook.md (text/markdown)]

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks — adding customer context. IvyWorks is a strategic SMB from our Q1 book; current daily usage is ~30k tokens/day with a predictable diurnal pattern. Expected ramp: 2-3x over 6 weeks post-launch. Peak concurrency during business hours hits ~18 concurrent sessions today. Average prompt length: 40 tokens. They are adamant about p95 for the specific path they showed in the demo (short prompt, single-turn).

Notes for negotiation: they prefer a single-line SLO in the contract rather than a long appendix. That makes our engineering ask of a narrowly-scoped SLO even more important. Suggest we offer:
  - Commitment: p95 <= 85ms for /chat/generate (max input tokens 64, max output tokens 128) during M-F 8am-6pm PT
  - Preconditions: Dedicated capacity reservation (3x current concurrency as initial commit) + 7-day warm-up with synthetic traffic + agreed-upon ramp cap and an automatic throttle if envelope exceeded
  - Measurement: Redwood's telemetry on the dedicated pool; daily digests to IvyWorks and a monthly compliance report

If legal is okay with language around "service credit proportional to reserved capacity for verifiable breaches", I think we can close quickly.

— Grace
Customer Success


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 22 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: p95 availability commitment for chat endpoint (IvyWorks)

Thanks all — flagged legal considerations below. High-level: we can accept a narrowly-scoped availability/latency commitment if the following appear in the contract:

1) Explicit scope definition (endpoint, token limits, business hours).
2) Traffic envelope and failure modes: a clause that excludes outages caused by customer-initiated traffic above the envelope, abuse, or third-party network failures.
3) Limited SLA credit: define credits as percentage of monthly reserved fee (caps + tiers) rather than per-token.
4) Exit & review cadence: 90-day operational review clause to evaluate the SLO post-ramp; right to adjust terms if we see abuse or unanticipated costs.
5) Auditability: Redwood's telemetry will be the source of truth; provide sample report format to customer.

If product and engineering approve the operational guardrails Marcus outlined, legal can draft a short SLO addendum and redline the MAP. I recommend we avoid open-ended language like "guaranteed sub-80ms" without the other constraints.

I'll prepare a first-pass SLO addendum draft once Marcus confirms capacity sizing and Grace confirms QPS ramp.

— Priya
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Dedicated safety-net reservation + autoscale soft-exemption for Brightline pilot
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Brightline CTO <cto@brightline.ai>, Brightline Procurement <procurement@brightline.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated safety-net reservation + autoscale soft-exemption for Brightline pilot

Team —

Brightline is ramping their pilot next week and has asked for a short-term dedicated safety-net to guarantee latency during demo windows and initial production traffic. Key ask from the customer: a reserved slice plus the ability to briefly burst beyond the reservation without being preempted immediately.

High level request (Brightline):
- Reserve: 4xA100-equivalent GPU slice in us-west for a 3-week pilot window (target start: 2027-04-25).
- Autoscale soft-exemption: allow up to ~50% additional burst capacity for short periods (max 90 minutes per day aggregated) before eviction — we want to avoid noisy preemptions during customer-facing demos.
- Fallback: automatic model downgrade or routing to a smaller variant if the cluster is saturated.

Attached: pilot_capacity_plan.xlsx (details on expected qps/token mix and demo windows). I also uploaded a short runbook draft.

Asks:
1) Kimberly — can we approve a temporary reservation and what procurement steps do you need?
2) Laura/Marcus/Soojin — what ops constraints or guardrails would we need to safely allow a "soft-burst" policy?
3) Finance/Procurement — rough cost delta for the burst allowance (for procurement to sign off).

This is time-sensitive: Brightline wants confirmation by EOD 2027-04-22 to lock sales cadence. If we can’t give the full 3 weeks, propose the minimum we can safely guarantee so the customer stays live.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Head of Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Brightline CTO <cto@brightline.ai>, Brightline Procurement <procurement@brightline.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated safety-net reservation + autoscale soft-exemption for Brightline pilot

Aditya — thanks for the summary and the plan doc. Quick read: this is feasible, but we need to gate with operational and billing guardrails. Proposed compromise to meet the EOD ask:

1) Reservation: we can allocate a 3-week reservation for 4xA100-equivalent, but initially approve for 2 weeks with an agreed extension clause (auto-extend to 3rd week if usage + cost approvals completed).

2) Soft-exemption (burst lane): we can add a "pilot-burst" tag to the slice that permits autoscale up to +50% of allocated vGPU for burst windows. Guardrails I require:
   - Burst is limited to 90 minutes/day aggregated, with per-window cap = 30 minutes.
   - Eviction window: if burst usage is sustained past 30 min *and* cluster pressure > 80%, we preempt into the fallback route.
   - Ops on-call notified via PagerDuty when burst triggers; a runbook must exist (please see attached safety_net_runbook.md).

3) Cost & procurement: finance needs a Delta estimate. Aditya, please confirm expected token volumes for the demo windows and daily average QPS so I can route to billing for an estimate. Procurement will need acceptance of the extension clause and an explicit acknowledgement of potential overage billing for burst minutes.

4) Compliance: any PII/data residency ask from Brightline? If not, default to standard hosted controls. If yes, we may need Private deployment (bigger lift).

If everyone is OK with the 2-week immediate reservation + documented extension flow, I can start the procurement paperwork today.

-Kim
Kimberly Park
Director, Dedicated Capacity
Redwood Inference

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Brightline CTO <cto@brightline.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated safety-net reservation + autoscale soft-exemption for Brightline pilot

Thanks — a few ops notes from runtime/product:

- Implementation approach: add a policy tag (pilot-burst) that adjusts scheduler thresholds for the reserved pool. When burst is requested we: warm KV cache aggressively, extend batching windows slightly (configurable), and permit ephemeral scale-up to the tagged nodes.

- Preemption / fallback: rather than hard eviction we should prefer model routing to a smaller quantized variant after 15 minutes of sustained >75% host load. This reduces customer-visible failures.

- Observability: require a dedicated dashboard (token-level latency + burst minutes) and a short alert that triggers at 60 burst-minutes used (so we can call procurement/engineering). Marcus — can you confirm we can add that alert by Friday?

- Safety: need a 2-hour load-test window prior to the first customer demo to validate KV warm-up and fallback. I propose Wednesday 2027-04-23 02:00–04:00 PDT. If that passes, I can greenlight the full 2-week reservation.

Questions for Aditya/Brightline: what are the demo windows (dates/times) and the expected peak token per second metrics? Please paste into the plan doc or reply inline.

-Laura
Laura Bennett
Product Manager, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 11:03 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Aditya — thanks for the summary and the plan doc. Quick read: this is feasible, but we need to gate with operational and billing guardrails. Proposed compromise to meet the EOD ask:
> ...

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Brightline CTO <cto@brightline.ai>, Brightline Procurement <procurement@brightline.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 16:44:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated safety-net reservation + autoscale soft-exemption for Brightline pilot

Thanks Kim/Laura/Marcus —

Answers and next steps from Brightline (copied from their PM reply):
- Demo windows: 2027-04-26 14:00–15:30 PDT (internal demo), 2027-04-28 10:00–11:30 PDT (customer-facing).
- Expected peak: 1.8M tokens/hour during demo spikes (~500 tokens/sec peak per instance cluster) and lower steady-state 100k tokens/hour.
- No additional PII/residency constraints beyond standard hosted controls.

Actions I’m taking now:
- Attached pilot_capacity_plan.xlsx with exact QPS/token numbers and a calendar with the demo windows.
- I accept the 2-week immediate reservation with the extension clause — we will sign the procurement amendment for the third week if the load test passes and cost delta is approved.
- Brightline agrees to the load-test window on 2027-04-23 02:00–04:00 PDT. They asked that the runbook be shared with their SRE team (I’ve cc’d their CTO).

Kimberly — please start the procurement paperwork for a 2-week reservation + explicit burst overage billing. Marcus/Soojin/Laura — can you confirm the runbook and the alerting by Friday EOD? If all green I’ll send Brightline a confirmation email EOD tomorrow.

Appreciate the quick turnaround —
Aditya Rao
Head of Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

Attachments: pilot_capacity_plan.xlsx
source.summary: Internal coordination to approve a short-term dedicated reservation and a controlled autoscale \"soft-burst\" for Brightline's pilot. Team agreed on a 2-week immediate reservation, a 90-min/day burst cap with guardrails, a 2-hour load test on 2027-04-23, and procurement paperwork to enable a possible 3rd-week extension pending cost approval.

- Attached pilot_capacity_plan.xlsx with exact QPS/token numbers and a calendar with the demo windows.
- I accept the 2-week immediate reservation with the extension clause — we will sign the procurement amendment for the third week if the load test passes and cost delta is approved.
- Brightline agrees to the load-test window on 2027-04-23 02:00–04:00 PDT. They asked that the runbook be shared with their SRE team (I’ve cc’d their CTO).

Kimberly — please start the procurement paperwork for a 2-week reservation + explicit burst overage billing. Marcus/Soojin/Laura — can you confirm the runbook and the alerting by Friday EOD? If all green I’ll send Brightline a confirmation email EOD tomorrow.

Appreciate the quick turnaround —
Aditya Rao
Head of Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

Attachments: pilot_capacity_plan.xlsx

Hi Sarah — thanks again for the time earlier. Quick recap and recommended next steps based on our discovery:

Context: HealthVect looking to add a document-QA assistant over PHI-adjacent clinical notes (redaction + retrieval). Goal is sub-500ms P95 for user-facing queries and sustainable throughput for ~30 QPS burst (mostly short queries, avg token output ~60).

- seq=768: P50 ~ 140ms, P95 ~ 320ms, P99 ~ 610ms (same config)

Data residency / pilot topology:

- Pilot acceptance criteria: P95 <= 350ms for seq<=512 at 25 TPS sustained; no data egress outside VPC; qualitative QA pass on 250 eval prompts in the prompt pack.

—
Camila Reyes
