source.subject: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sana Roy <sana.roy@medicore.com>, Timothy Park <tpark@medicore.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Hi Sana / Tim —

Thanks again for a helpful call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we have alignment before we spin up the trial environment. I pulled these points from our conversation; please flag anything I missed.

Summary of goals (what success looks like for Medicore):
- Verify end-to-end latency for query-heavy workflows under 60ms p50 and acceptable p95 for peak windows.
- Validate model quality on your diagnostic prompt set: >= 92% pass rate on intent extraction and <5% hallucination on the clinical QA set.
- Confirm telemetry and audit log fidelity (token-level tracing + request IDs) for compliance review.
- Exercise failover path (hosted -> dedicated sandbox) under simulated capacity limits.

Trial scope & environment options discussed:
1) Quick path: Hosted API sandbox (Redwood-hosted) — fastest to get started, minimal lift; best for early quality and latency baselines.
2) Dedicated sandbox (reserved GPUs in our staging cluster) — predictable throughput for load testing; recommended if you want to validate throughput at scale.
3) VPC trial (Private) — for evaluation with your network controls and KMS; requires network onboarding and ~2–3 weeks to complete.

Recommended initial plan (we suggest a 3-week exploratory trial):
- Week 0 (this week): Kickoff, access + sample prompt exchange, share evaluation prompt set and success criteria.
- Week 1: Hosted baseline runs (quality + latency) — Redwood provides host endpoints and initial eval dashboard.
- Week 2: If throughput testing is needed, switch to Dedicated sandbox for peak/scale tests.
- Week 3: Optional VPC onboarding for security review (parallel preparatory work can start week 1).

Owners & immediate asks:
- Medicore: share your evaluation prompt sets (clinical QA + extraction), and a list of sensitive fields for masking (Sana / Tim).
- Redwood AE (Ava): coordinate kickoff, schedule demo for engineering team, manage billing/trial limits.
- Redwood Solutions (Ben): SOC2 artifacts, trial SOW and monitoring dashboard configuration.
- Redwood Ops (Marcus): provisioning Dedicated sandbox if requested; network checklist for VPC.

Attachments: I attached a first-pass Method of Procedure (Medicore_MoP_v1.pdf) and a trial SOW draft to accelerate procurement.

Proposed immediate next step: 30-minute kickoff on Tue 4/19 to exchange prompt sets and finalize which environment to start with — are you available 10:00–10:30am PT?

Thanks,

Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

--
Redwood Inference | Developer-first inference platform
From: Sana Roy <sana.roy@medicore.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Timothy Park <tpark@medicore.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Thanks, Ava — this is great and aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side: 

- We definitely want to start with the Hosted sandbox so we can iterate on prompts quickly.
- We will need a Dedicated sandbox for load testing — target to run a 48-hour soak at simulated peak by the end of week 2.
- VPC: security will want to see the network checklist and KMS integration details before we commit (sounds like parallel work, agree).

Action items from Medicore: 
- I’ll upload our evaluation prompt set and the clinical QA spreadsheet (contains expected outputs) to the secure folder by EOD Monday.
- I’m attaching our security requirements spreadsheet for your solutions team to review (medicore_security_requirements.xlsx).

Kickoff time on Tue 4/19 10:00am PT works for Tim and me.

One quick ask on data retention: we need confirmation that token logs for the trial will be retained for 90 days and that we can disable any non-essential persistent storage. Can Ben confirm?

Appreciate the fast turnaround — looking forward to the kickoff.

— Sana Roy
Director of Clinical ML | Medicore Health
Sana.Roy@medicore.com | +1 (617) 555-2234
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Ava / Marcus / Rishi — forwarding Sana’s note. Quick internal plan and asks before the Tue kickoff: 

1) Data retention & telemetry: We can do 90 days for token logs in the trial environment; by default hosted sandboxes retain 30 days, but we can elevate retention to 90d for trial customers (have to toggle retention at the project level). Need Rishi to confirm any compliance caveats.

2) SOC2 artifacts: I’ll attach the SOC2 overview and a short evidence checklist to share with Medicore during kickoff. Also attaching the trial SOW draft I pulled into our template (trial-sow-draft.docx).

3) Dedicated sandbox prep: Marcus — can you block a small Dedicated pool (2 x A100 equivalent) for 4/20–4/22 for their 48-hour soak? If we need more throughput for the soak, we’ll scale to 4 GPUs but want to start small.

4) VPC onboarding: Rishi, please prepare the network onboarding checklist and KMS flow diagram. That can be shared as ‘informational’ during kickoff; they understand it’s parallel work.

5) Roles: I’ll own the SOC2 questions and SOW follow-up. Ava will own the kickoff and prompt handoff. Marcus will own Dedicated sandbox provisioning. Rishi owns VPC/network.

If everyone is OK, I’ll reply-all with SOC2 summary and link to the drive folder after this message.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineering Lead | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0144

Attachments: trial-sow-draft.docx (internal), SOC2_overview.pdf

preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sana Roy <sana.roy@medicore.com>, Timothy Park <tpark@medicore.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Hi Sana / Tim —

Thanks again for a helpful call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we have alignment before we spin up the trial environment. I pulled these points from our conversation; please flag anything I missed.

Summary of goals (what success looks like for Medicore):
- Verify end-to-end latency for query-heavy workflows under 60ms p50 and acceptable p95 for peak windows.
- Validate model quality on your diagnostic prompt set: >= 92% pass rate on intent extraction and <5% hallucination on the clinical QA set.
- Confirm telemetry and audit log fidelity (token-level tracing + request IDs) for compliance review.
- Exercise failover path (hosted -> dedicated sandbox) under simulated capacity limits.

Trial scope & environment options discussed:
1) Quick path: Hosted API sandbox (Redwood-hosted) — fastest to get started, minimal lift; best for early quality and latency baselines.
2) Dedicated sandbox (reserved GPUs in our staging cluster) — predictable throughput for load testing; recommended if you want to validate throughput at scale.
3) VPC trial (Private) — for evaluation with your network controls and KMS; requires network onboarding and ~2–3 weeks to complete.

Recommended initial plan (we suggest a 3-week exploratory trial):
- Week 0 (this week): Kickoff, access + sample prompt exchange, share evaluation prompt set and success criteria.
- Week 1: Hosted baseline runs (quality + latency) — Redwood provides host endpoints and initial eval dashboard.
- Week 2: If throughput testing is needed, switch to Dedicated sandbox for peak/scale tests.
- Week 3: Optional VPC onboarding for security review (parallel preparatory work can start week 1).

Owners & immediate asks:
- Medicore: share your evaluation prompt sets (clinical QA + extraction), and a list of sensitive fields for masking (Sana / Tim).
- Redwood AE (Ava): coordinate kickoff, schedule demo for engineering team, manage billing/trial limits.
- Redwood Solutions (Ben): SOC2 artifacts, trial SOW and monitoring dashboard configuration.
- Redwood Ops (Marcus): provisioning Dedicated sandbox if requested; network checklist for VPC.

Attachments: I attached a first-pass Method of Procedure (Medicore_MoP_v1.pdf) and a trial SOW draft to accelerate procurement.

Proposed immediate next step: 30-minute kickoff on Tue 4/19 to exchange prompt sets and finalize which environment to start with — are you available 10:00–10:30am PT?

Thanks,

Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

--
Redwood Inference | Developer-first inference platform

Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Medicore trial enablement: success criteria & environment options

Thanks, Ava — this is great and aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side: 

- We definitely want to start with the Hosted sandbox so we can iterate on prompts quickly.
- We will need a Dedicated sandbox for load testing — target to run a 48-hour soak at simulated peak by the end of week 2.
- VPC: security will want to see the network checklist and KMS integration details before we commit (sounds like parallel work, agree).

Action items from Medicore:

Kickoff time on Tue 4/19 10:00am PT works for Tim and me.

One quick ask on data retention: we need confirmation that token logs for the trial will be retained for 90 days and that we can disable any non-essential persistent storage. Can Ben confirm?

Appreciate the fast turnaround — looking forward to the kickoff.

— Sana Roy
Director of Clinical ML | Medicore Health
Sana.Roy@medicore.com | +1 (617) 555-2234


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>

2) SOC2 artifacts: I’ll attach the SOC2 overview and a short evidence checklist to share with Medicore during kickoff. Also attaching the trial SOW draft I pulled into our template (trial-sow-draft.docx).

3) Dedicated sandbox prep: Marcus — can you block a small Dedicated pool (2 x A100 equivalent) for 4/20–4/22 for their 48-hour soak? If we need more throughput for the soak, we’ll scale to 4 GPUs but want to start small.

4) VPC onboarding: Rishi, please prepare the network onboarding checklist and KMS flow diagram. That can be shared as ‘informational’ during kickoff; they understand it’s parallel work.

5) Roles: I’ll own the SOC2 questions and SOW follow-up. Ava will own the kickoff and prompt handoff. Marcus will own Dedicated sandbox provisioning. Rishi owns VPC/network.

If everyone is OK, I’ll reply-all with SOC2 summary and link to the drive folder after this message.

Thanks — Ben

source.subject: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: accounts-payable@acme-corp.com, Matt Rios <matt.rios@procurevault.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:12:45 -0700
Subject: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Hi Matt / Acme AP team —

Thanks for taking a look at our onboarding questions. We're finalizing vendor registration and need a quick confirmation on a few items so our billing team can start sending invoices without delays: 

- What exact PO fields must appear on invoice PDF for automatic matching in your ProcureVault workflow (PO number, PO line item ID, Bill-to legal name, company tax id)?
- Is the vendor registration portal the only acceptable place to submit tax documents (W-9 / VAT cert), or can we email a signed copy to accounts-payable?
- We plan to use a third-party remittance agent (Aurelia Payments) to collect funds on our behalf. Do you accept a Delegated Payment Authorization (signed letter) that designates Aurelia as the remit-to/payee on our vendor record, or do you require Redwood to have the vendor record with a remit-to subfield?
- If delegation is allowed, what proof do you need (sample invoice with remit-to, signed DPA, portal flag)?

I've attached a sample invoice template and our vendor legal name/tax ID for reference. If you prefer, happy to jump on a 20 minute call to walk through the fields.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0127
[Attachment: sample_invoice_template.pdf (pdf)]

From: Matt Rios <matt.rios@procurevault.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@acme-corp.com
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 10:05:30 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Ben —

Thanks for the clear list. Quick answers inline and attached enrollment guidance.

- Matching: We require PO number, vendor legal name, and vendor tax identifier to be present on the invoice PDF. Line-level matching requires the PO line ID and quantity/unit price when invoicing against specific lines.
- Tax docs: Complete vendor registration in the ProcureVault Supplier Portal is preferred so docs are linked to the vendor record. We will accept emailed W-9s or VAT certs temporarily but final enrollment must be through the portal.
- Delegated remittance: We support third-party remittance agents but need a signed Delegated Payment Authorization (DPA) on file that authorizes Aurelia to receive payment on behalf of Redwood. The DPA must be signed by an authorized Redwood officer and reference the Redwood vendor ID in our system.
- Proof needed: A signed DPA, a sample invoice indicating the remit-to address, and confirmation that the vendor portal lists Aurelia as the remit-to agent.

Attached: ProcureVault_vendor_enroll_guide.pdf; PO_mapping_template.xlsx

If you send the DPA as a PDF and the enrollment form, we can expedite and issue a vendor ID within 48 hours after verification.

Best,
Matt Rios
Senior Partnerships Manager | ProcureVault
matt.rios@procurevault.com
[Attachment: ProcureVault_vendor_enroll_guide.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: PO_mapping_template.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 08:45:12 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

FYI — forwarding Matt's details from ProcureVault / Acme AP.

Questions for ops/legal:
- Can we sign the DPA as-is or do we need legal redlines for the standard Aurelia template?
- Do we create a separate vendor record in Acme as "Redwood Inference (c/o Aurelia Payments)" or keep a single vendor record for Redwood and add Aurelia in the remit-to field? Which approach reduces PO/invoice matching friction?
- Grace — can you confirm which tax docs we can upload now (W-9 on file) and whether EU VAT cert needs a separate channel?

I attached a rough draft of the DPA we would send them. If you can reply with preferred approach by Monday I'd like to get enrollment submitted this week.

Thanks —
Ben

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Matt Rios <matt.rios@procurevault.com>
Date: Thu, Apr 15, 2027 at 10:05 AM
Subject: Re: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, accounts-payable@acme-corp.com

[ProcureVault info and attachments omitted in this forward — see original thread]

[Attachment: delegate_authorization_draft.docx (docx)]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Ben —

Legal did a quick pass on the delegate authorization draft. We can sign the DPA after two small edits: (1) add a clause that Redwood remains the invoice-originating party for tax and audit purposes; (2) include our EIN in the header and a limited liability statement for Aurelia as a collection agent.

Operational note: Creating a single vendor record for Redwood in the customer's portal and setting Aurelia as the remit-to agent is preferable — it preserves PO matching on Redwood's vendor ID while allowing payments to go to the agent. Creating separate vendor records tends to break PO auto-acceptance (many AP systems treat separate vendor names as distinct suppliers).

I've attached the redlined DPA draft. Marcus — can you confirm whether our billing system can produce a sample invoice with the remit-to fields as ProcureVault requested? If yes, we should include that sample when we submit the enrollment.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com
[Attachment: DPA_Redwood_Aurelia_draft.pdf (pdf)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: accounts-payable@acme-corp.com, Matt Rios <matt.rios@procurevault.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:10:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Hi Matt / Acme AP —

Thanks for the patience. We've completed the ProcureVault portal enrollment and uploaded the following: vendor enrollment form, signed W-9, and the redlined DPA (as requested by Kimberly). We also uploaded a sample invoice that includes the Remit-To block populated with Aurelia Payments (Aurelia EFT) and Redwood's vendor ID for PO matching.

Attachments uploaded and attached to this email: Redwood_vendor_enrollment_form_filled.pdf; W9_Redwood_signed.pdf; PO-ACME-77231.pdf (sample invoice showing PO acceptance and remit-to).

Next steps from our side: please confirm the vendor ID assigned in ProcureVault / Acme so we can validate auto-matching. Also, if there's a preferred file naming convention for invoices (e.g. PO-<number>_Invoice_<invoice#>.pdf) let us know and we'll adopt it.

DocuSign link to the final DPA: https://app.docusign.com/some/docusign-link/DPA-RWD-2027

Thanks —
Marcus Lin
Billing Systems Lead | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com
[Attachment: Redwood_vendor_enrollment_form_filled.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: W9_Redwood_signed.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: PO-ACME-77231.pdf (pdf)]

Questions for ops/legal:
- Can we sign the DPA as-is or do we need legal redlines for the standard Aurelia template?
- Do we create a separate vendor record in Acme as "Redwood Inference (c/o Aurelia Payments)" or keep a single vendor record for Redwood and add Aurelia in the remit-to field? Which approach reduces PO/invoice matching friction?

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>

Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Ben —

Legal did a quick pass on the delegate authorization draft. We can sign the DPA after two small edits: (1) add a clause that Redwood remains the invoice-originating party for tax and audit purposes; (2) include our EIN in the header and a limited liability statement for Aurelia as a collection agent.

Operational note: Creating a single vendor record for Redwood in the customer's portal and setting Aurelia as the remit-to agent is preferable — it preserves PO matching on Redwood's vendor ID while allowing payments to go to the agent. Creating separate vendor records tends to break PO auto-acceptance (many AP systems treat separate vendor names as distinct suppliers).

I've attached the redlined DPA draft. Marcus — can you confirm whether our billing system can produce a sample invoice with the remit-to fields as ProcureVault requested? If yes, we should include that sample when we submit the enrollment.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: accounts-payable@acme-corp.com, Matt Rios <matt.rios@procurevault.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:12:45 -0700
Subject: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Hi Matt / Acme AP team —

Thanks for taking a look at our onboarding questions. We're finalizing vendor registration and need a quick confirmation on a few items so our billing team can start sending invoices without delays: 

- What exact PO fields must appear on invoice PDF for automatic matching in your ProcureVault workflow (PO number, PO line item ID, Bill-to legal name, company tax id)?
- Is the vendor registration portal the only acceptable place to submit tax documents (W-9 / VAT cert), or can we email a signed copy to accounts-payable?
- We plan to use a third-party remittance agent (Aurelia Payments) to collect funds on our behalf. Do you accept a Delegated Payment Authorization (signed letter) that designates Aurelia as the remit-to/payee on our vendor record, or do you require Redwood to have the vendor record with a remit-to subfield?
- If delegation is allowed, what proof do you need (sample invoice with remit-to, signed DPA, portal flag)?

I've attached a sample invoice template and our vendor legal name/tax ID for reference. If you prefer, happy to jump on a 20 minute call to walk through the fields.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0127
[Attachment: sample_invoice_template.pdf (pdf)]

From: Matt Rios <matt.rios@procurevault.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@acme-corp.com
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 10:05:30 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice routing authority & PO proof requirements

Ben —

Thanks for the clear list. Quick answers inline and attached enrollment guidance.

- Matching: We require PO number, vendor legal name, and vendor tax identifier to be present on the invoice PDF. Line-level matching requires the PO line ID and quantity/unit price when invoicing against specific lines.
- Tax docs: Complete vendor registration in the ProcureVault Supplier Portal is preferred so docs are linked to the vendor record. We will accept emailed W-9s or VAT certs temporarily but final enrollment must be through the portal.
- Delegated remittance: We support third-party remittance agents but need a signed Delegated Payment Authorization (DPA) on file that authorizes Aurelia to receive payment on behalf of Redwood. The DPA must be signed by an authorized Redwood officer and reference the Redwood vendor ID in our system.
- Proof needed: A signed DPA, a sample invoice indicating the remit-to address, and confirmation that the vendor portal lists Aurelia as the remit-to agent.

Attached: ProcureVault_vendor_enroll_guide.pdf; PO_mapping_template.xlsx

If you send the DPA as a PDF and the enrollment form, we can expedite and issue a vendor ID within 48 hours after verification.

Best,
Matt Rios
Senior Partnerships Manager | ProcureVault
matt.rios@procurevault.com
[Attachment: ProcureVault_vendor_enroll_guide.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: PO_mapping_template.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

source.subject: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Team —

I pulled together a first-pass migration lane matrix for the v1 -> rfv2 model transition and an initial proposal for an "escape-hatch" policy for customers who need more time. Attached: migration_lane_matrix.csv and a short comm draft (comm_draft.md).

Key asks: 
- Product: confirm which model variants we must keep in the slow-lane (compatibility) for 90 days vs 30 days.
- Sales/CS: flag customers who need exemption (we currently have a few in legal procurement) and prioritize by revenue/impact.
- Infra/Runtime (Marcus): are there known perf regressions on rfv2 at <64 token sequences we should call out in comms?

My proposed lanes in the matrix: 
- Fast lane: auto-migrate at day 0, full deprecation fallback after 30 days
- Slow lane: 90-day soft support, manual opt-in migration, technical support priority
- Escape-hatch: ad-hoc 120-day exceptions for validated compliance/regulatory cases (manual review)

I want us to land on a policy by end-of-day Tuesday (2027-04-20) so we can start customer outreach Wednesday. Please reply inline with your lane suggestions and any required policy text.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Sr. PM, Platform
Redwood Inference

Attachment: migration_lane_matrix.csv (csv)
Attachment: comm_draft.md (markdown)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

On Sun, Apr 18, 2027 at 08:12 AM Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> I pulled together a first-pass migration lane matrix for the v1 -> rfv2 model transition...

Thanks for pulling this together, Ava. Quick product notes after skimming the matrix: 

1) Compatibility surface: rfv2 removes the legacy reranking endpoint semantics (scoring mode). Any customer using the old /rerank path will need a shim or explicit rerun. We should add a compatibility flag (query param) that we can support for 30 days to avoid breaking customers in the fast lane.

2) Latency/regressions: Marcus — I've seen 5-8% higher p95 for 1-16 token sequences on A100 when quantized; if infra can't fix that quickly, call it out as a risk in comms. If we offer a per-customer small fast-path reservation (dedicated 1-slot burst) we can keep latency SLOs for key accounts.

3) Escape-hatch gating criteria: propose a 3-step validation: (a) sales attestation of customer impact, (b) security/compliance check (if data residency/regulatory), (c) PM approval. Use the customer_exemptions_list.xlsx to capture approvals.

I added inline comments in the matrix and flagged two customers (OakWallet and Olympus Payments) as likely requests.

-Karthik
Head of Product, Serving
Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Ava / team —

Flagging customer context from the field:
- OakWallet (Katie Ham, katie.ham@oakwallet.com): heavy use of embeddings + long-running session state. They asked explicitly for a 90-day slow lane previously; legal is pushing back on new SLAs unless we provide a concrete rollback plan.
- Olympus Payments (support@olympus-payments.com): mid-sized, high-volume. Ops is nervous about token-cost regressions; they want an explicit burst reservation for peak hours.

Suggested sales-side commitments: 
- Offer OakWallet a timed escape-hatch (90 -> 120 days) if they sign a migration support addendum (1-2 week dedicated onboarding from Solutions Eng).
- For customers with cost concerns, offer a brief cost-analysis workshop and a one-week rollback window during initial migration.

I uploaded a draft exemption spreadsheet (customer_exemptions_list.xlsx) with the two accounts noted and preliminary revenue tiers. Also added a short draft email we can send once policy approved.

Laura Bennett
Senior AE, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment: customer_exemptions_list.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 17:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Appreciate the input. A few roadmap/comms asks from PM side: 

- Timeline: We need formal canary dates for the first 10% of traffic, and a rollback plan that is executed automatically (feature flag + model routing) in case of severe regressions. Proposed schedule:
  * Week 0 (Apr 21): enable canary for internal traffic and Redwood demo orgs
  * Week 1 (Apr 28): 10% customer traffic (fast lane only) + monitor quality metrics
  * Week 3 (May 12): begin slow-lane invitations for selected customers with SE support

- Messaging: comm_draft.md needs a clear "what to expect" section and explicit SLOs for any customers granted escape-hatch. We should also add an FAQ for the outages/rollback process.

- Exceptions: We should limit active escape-hatches to no more than 8 customers at a time to keep support overhead manageable. Add that constraint to the matrix.

Marcus — can you confirm whether the runtime team can make the temporary compatibility-flag available for fast-lane rollback? If not, we should escalate to infra leadership today.

-Marissa
Group PM, Model Infra
Redwood Inference
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Thanks everyone — good alignment. Short summary and decisions: 

1) Lanes finalized: fast (auto, 30d rollback window), slow (90d), escape-hatch (120d limited to 8 active customers).
2) Compatibility flag: Marcus confirms runtime will ship a temporary query param flag for /generate and /chat routes by Thu EOD (2027-04-22).
3) Sales commitments: Laura to get OakWallet to sign the addendum; if OakWallet requests 120d, they must agree to a 2-week SE onboarding and a migration checklist.
4) Comms: Ava will publish a one-page FAQ + comm_draft.md to Drive and send a sales-ready email template by Wed morning.

Action items: 
- Laura: update customer_exemptions_list.xlsx and capture signatures (due 2027-04-26).
- Marcus: confirm rollout plan and post canary runbook (due 2027-04-22).
- Karthik/Marissa: finalize the compatibility gating language in the policy doc (due 2027-04-21).

I'll send the customer note once we have the SE addendum for OakWallet. Thanks all.

– Ava
Sr. PM, Platform
Redwood Inference

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3-migration-playbooks (comm_draft.md, canary-runbook)

- Fast lane: auto-migrate at day 0, full deprecation fallback after 30 days
- Slow lane: 90-day soft support, manual opt-in migration, technical support priority
- Escape-hatch: ad-hoc 120-day exceptions for validated compliance/regulatory cases (manual review)

I want us to land on a policy by end-of-day Tuesday (2027-04-20) so we can start customer outreach Wednesday. Please reply inline with your lane suggestions and any required policy text.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Sr. PM, Platform
Redwood Inference

Attachment: migration_lane_matrix.csv (csv)
Attachment: comm_draft.md (markdown)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

On Sun, Apr 18, 2027 at 08:12 AM Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> I pulled together a first-pass migration lane matrix for the v1 -> rfv2 model transition...

Thanks for pulling this together, Ava. Quick product notes after skimming the matrix: 

1) Compatibility surface: rfv2 removes the legacy reranking endpoint semantics (scoring mode). Any customer using the old /rerank path will need a shim or explicit rerun. We should add a compatibility flag (query param) that we can support for 30 days to avoid breaking customers in the fast lane.

2) Latency/regressions: Marcus — I've seen 5-8% higher p95 for 1-16 token sequences on A100 when quantized; if infra can't fix that quickly, call it out as a risk in comms. If we offer a per-customer small fast-path reservation (dedicated 1-slot burst) we can keep latency SLOs for key accounts.

3) Escape-hatch gating criteria: propose a 3-step validation: (a) sales attestation of customer impact, (b) security/compliance check (if data residency/regulatory), (c) PM approval. Use the customer_exemptions_list.xlsx to capture approvals.

I added inline comments in the matrix and flagged two customers (OakWallet and Olympus Payments) as likely requests.

-Karthik
Head of Product, Serving
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Ava / team —

Flagging customer context from the field:
- OakWallet (Katie Ham, katie.ham@oakwallet.com): heavy use of embeddings + long-running session state. They asked explicitly for a 90-day slow lane previously; legal is pushing back on new SLAs unless we provide a concrete rollback plan.
- Olympus Payments (support@olympus-payments.com): mid-sized, high-volume. Ops is nervous about token-cost regressions; they want an explicit burst reservation for peak hours.

Suggested sales-side commitments: 
- Offer OakWallet a timed escape-hatch (90 -> 120 days) if they sign a migration support addendum (1-2 week dedicated onboarding from Solutions Eng).
- For customers with cost concerns, offer a brief cost-analysis workshop and a one-week rollback window during initial migration.

I uploaded a draft exemption spreadsheet (customer_exemptions_list.xlsx) with the two accounts noted and preliminary revenue tiers. Also added a short draft email we can send once policy approved.

Laura Bennett
Senior AE, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment: customer_exemptions_list.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 17:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Appreciate the input. A few roadmap/comms asks from PM side: 

- Timeline: We need formal canary dates for the first 10% of traffic, and a rollback plan that is executed automatically (feature flag + model routing) in case of severe regressions. Proposed schedule:
  * Week 0 (Apr 21): enable canary for internal traffic and Redwood demo orgs
  * Week 1 (Apr 28): 10% customer traffic (fast lane only) + monitor quality metrics
  * Week 3 (May 12): begin slow-lane invitations for selected customers with SE support

- Messaging: comm_draft.md needs a clear "what to expect" section and explicit SLOs for any customers granted escape-hatch. We should also add an FAQ for the outages/rollback process.

- Exceptions: We should limit active escape-hatches to no more than 8 customers at a time to keep support overhead manageable. Add that constraint to the matrix.

Marcus — can you confirm whether the runtime team can make the temporary compatibility-flag available for fast-lane rollback? If not, we should escalate to infra leadership today.

-Marissa
Group PM, Model Infra
Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Migration lane allocations, escape-hatch policy, and customer comms cadence

Thanks everyone — good alignment. Short summary and decisions: 

1) Lanes finalized: fast (auto, 30d rollback window), slow (90d), escape-hatch (120d limited to 8 active customers).
2) Compatibility flag: Marcus confirms runtime will ship a temporary query param flag for /generate and /chat routes by Thu EOD (2027-04-22).
3) Sales commitments: Laura to get OakWallet to sign the addendum; if OakWallet requests 120d, they must agree to a 2-week SE onboarding and a migration checklist.
4) Comms: Ava will publish a one-page FAQ + comm_draft.md to Drive and send a sales-ready email template by Wed morning.

Action items: 
- Laura: update customer_exemptions_list.xlsx and capture signatures (due 2027-04-26).
- Marcus: confirm rollout plan and post canary runbook (due 2027-04-22).
- Karthik/Marissa: finalize the compatibility gating language in the policy doc (due 2027-04-21).

I'll send the customer note once we have the SE addendum for OakWallet. Thanks all.

– Ava
Sr. PM, Platform
Redwood Inference

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3-migration-playbooks (comm_draft.md, canary-runbook)

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>, Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Hi Rahul / Aditi —

Quick note as we approach the June renewal for HelixHealth: you mentioned considering an on-prem environment split for regulatory isolation across two new clusters (one for analytics, one for patient-facing inference) as part of the renewal. We should scope this into the upcoming contract so delivery can start right after renewal sign.

High-level ask: can you confirm the target scope (number of clusters, any hard isolation/regulatory requirements, and expected go-live window)? From our side we’ll prepare a draft SOW, cost delta, and an updated term proposal (option to extend from 12->36 months with capacity commits to lower unit pricing).

If helpful I can set a 30-min call this week to align stakeholders — propose Wed 4/14 10am PT.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0168

Attachments: helix_request_notes.txt (summary notes)
From: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Thanks Ben — we are definitely interested. A few immediate clarifications: 

- Scope: we want two *physically isolated* clusters (air-gapped network segments within our data centers) with separate VPCs and KMS keys; one cluster sized for sustained low-latency inference (customer-facing), the other for large-batch analytics (nightly jobs).
- Timeline: target production by end of Q3 (Sept 30).
- Compliance: we will need SOC 2 evidence and an inventory of data residency and logging features.
- Commercial: looking at a 36-month committed term if price/SLAs look good.

Please send topology diagrams and an estimated cost delta vs our existing private footprint. We can take a call on Wed — 10am PT works.

—Rahul
Rahul Gupta
CTO, HelixHealth
rahul.gupta@helixhealth.com

On Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 3:02 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: HelixHealth renewal: on-prem expansion (internal)

Team — forwarding Rahul's note. Key asks: two physically isolated clusters, separate VPCs/KMS, SOC2 artifacts, target prod by 2027-09-30, interested in 36-month term.

Action items I want from the delivery/pricing side before Wed 10am call: 
- Marcus: high-level topology diagram and ops approach for isolation + rough delivery timeline.
- Evelyn: draft SOW (milestones, acceptance criteria, risk items) and resource needs.
- Priya: confirm whether private package supports separate KMS per cluster and any additional runbook items for air-gapped network setup.
- Laura: please prep an initial commercial delta (monthly and committed pricing) and options for 12/24/36 months.

I’ll be on the call with the customer. Please share artifacts to me and I’ll circulate to HelixHealth before the meeting.

Thanks —
Ben

--- Forwarded message attached: helix_request_notes.txt
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: HelixHealth renewal: on-prem expansion (internal)

Thanks Ben — I pulled together a first-pass SOW and timeline. High-level notes below and attached is SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf plus a network topology (HelixTopology_v2.png).

Summary (draft):
- Scope: provision two isolated clusters in HelixHealth data centers (Cluster-A: low-latency inference; Cluster-B: batch analytics), each in separate VPCs, separate KMS keys, distinct logging sinks.
- Timeline: 12 weeks from contract signature to pilot (Cluster-A limited inference), 20 weeks to full production for both clusters. Buffer for procurement and rack provisioning not included.
- Deliverables: infra buildbook, runbooks, acceptance test suite, 6-month hypercare.
- Preconditions: HelixHealth to provide network racking windows, firewall change windows, and KMS provisioning details.
- Pricing drivers: cluster sizing, desired replication, and committed term. Estimated capex/one-time setup and OPEX delta included in pricing spreadsheet (pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx).

Risks and notes:
- Air-gap/physical isolation introduces extended lead times for hardware/network approvals; recommend we include explicit procurement lead-time in SOW.
- If they commit to 36 months we can give a tiered discount that materially reduces per-token inference cost.

I’ll iterate once Marcus and Priya confirm the KMS/network details.

Evelyn Hart
Director, Customer Delivery | Redwood Inference
From: Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>
Cc: procurement-ops@helixhealth.com
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Ben / Rahul — thanks for the context. Procurement needs two things to move: 

1) An updated quote that shows one-time setup fees and ongoing recurring fees (monthly) for 12/24/36 month terms. Please highlight the savings at 36 months.
2) Security/compliance artifacts (we need SOC2 attestation, logging/retention policy, and KMS key handling documentation) to run through our security review.

Also: can you confirm proposed payment terms and any termination clauses if hardware provisioning is delayed? We prefer net 60 but can be flexible with a small discount for net 30.

Please send the commercial package and the SOC2 stub by EOD Mon so we can start internal review.

Regards,
Aditi Mehra
Sr. Procurement Manager | HelixHealth
procurement@helixhealth.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>, Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HelixHealth renewal: proposed on-prem expansion & isolated clusters

Hi Rahul / Aditi — appreciate the quick turnaround. Attached are the consolidated artifacts for your review: SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf, HelixTopology_v2.png, pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx, and a SOC2 attestation stub. Summary of our commercial proposal: 

- Term options: 12/24/36 months (see pricing spreadsheet). 36-month committed term includes a 22% reduction on per-token fees and waived onboarding fees if signed by 2027-06-01.
- One-time setup: covers infra build, runbooks, and on-site validation (detailed in SOW).
- Payment terms: default Net 60; Net 30 available with 1.5% early-payment discount.
- SLA: production latency and availability targets included in SOW; we propose 99.95% uptime for production clusters with standard remedies (credits).

Next steps: 
1) Aditi — if you can confirm receipt and flag any legal/contracting constraints we’ll get a redline-ready contract over to you by EOD Wed.
2) Rahul — if the topology looks good I’ll ask Evelyn to prep a detailed delivery milestone plan for pilot cutover.
3) Internal: Evelyn/Marcus/Priya — please post the final SOW + pricing into the Drive link below and I’ll push the package to DocuSign.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-helix-expansion
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/HE-0043
DocuSign (preview): https://www.docusign.com/helixt-2027-onprem

Thanks — we’re aligned to move quickly if you are.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

source.subject: Renewal: reserved GPU increase & updated per-hour terms
messages:
From: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@vectorcloud.ai>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Hi Ava,

Hope you had a great week. We're coming up on our renewal on May 1 and are evaluating moving from our current 16-GPU reserved pool to a 32-GPU pool for the next 12 months. Growth in inference volume has been faster than we forecasted, and our latency-sensitive endpoints need the additional headroom and KV-cache improvements.

A few asks up front:
- Can you share an updated reserved per-hour rate for 32 GPUs (12-month term) and the incremental discount thresholds?
- Is there a throughput SLO or baseline you can commit to once we double capacity? We currently see occasional token throttling during morning spikes.
- Any recommended change to burst capacity / autoscale settings for predictable cost?

We have procurement and security reviews starting next week. If you can send a proposed SOW + pricing, we can kick off internal approvals.

Thanks,
Jordan
Jordan Ellis
Head of Platform Ops
VectorCloud
[Attachment: vectorcloud_current_usage_q1.csv (csv, 58KB)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Hi Jordan — great to hear from you and congrats on the growth. We can absolutely support a move to a 32-GPU reserved pool for your renewal.

High level:
- I'll attach a proposed SOW and a pricing sheet for a 12-month reserved term here. The sheet includes two pricing bands: a standard reserved per-hour and a committed-throughput tier with tighter per-token economics.
- On throughput SLOs: for a 32-GPU reserved pool we can guarantee 99.5% availability at your current median sequence length and provide a throughput baseline of ~1.8x your current sustained tokens/sec (we'll include the measurement assumptions). source.subject: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay - hosted API
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Chen <emily@ezpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Hi Emily —

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday. As discussed, I put together a lightweight proposal that tries to balance EzPay's ask for predictability and your desire to capture upside. The attached PDF (EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf) contains the full table; below is the executive summary and a suggested mutual action plan.

Executive summary
- Baseline per-token list rate: $0.00045 (hosted API, text-generation)
- Anchored breakpoints (quarterly aggregated):
  * Tier A: 0 - 50M tokens -> baseline rate
  * Tier B: 50M - 200M tokens -> marginal rebate 12%
  * Tier C: 200M+ tokens -> marginal rebate 22%
- Transitional smoothing credit: for Q1 post-commit, we apply a retroactive blended credit up to 6% if you ramp slower than forecast (protects you vs. step cliffs)
- Overage treatment: marginal overage rebate capped at 15% (to avoid runaway discounts)

Why this structure? It preserves marginal economics (you only get the higher rebate for incremental tokens beyond a breakpoint) while adding an anchored smoothing credit so billing is readable for your finance team. This is a middle ground between pure blended and pure marginal approaches.

Next steps / asks
1) Internal confirmation: can procurement accept retroactive credits applied as invoice adjustments?
2) Would EzPay prefer rebates applied retroactively (credit back on invoice) or as upfront monthly bill-credit?
3) Can we aim for an LOI signature within the next two weeks to lock pricing for calendar Q3?

Proposed mutual action plan (high-level)
- Week 0: Aisha shares v1 proposal (attached)
- Week 1: Procurement review & confirm billing approach; Redwood legal to prepare LOI
- Week 2: Sign LOI; schedule technical kickoff

Attaching: EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf with the breakpoint tables and examples. Happy to hop on a short call to walk through the math.

Best,

Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
<aisha@redwood.inference>
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf
From: Emily Chen <emily@ezpay.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
Cc: EzPay Procurement <procurement@ezpay.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Aisha — thanks for the quick turn. I read the proposal and the structure makes sense in principle but our finance team has a few practical asks before we can move forward: 

1) Simplicity: our billing ops prefers either a simple blended effective rate applied monthly or a very clearly documented marginal process. The mix with a transitional smoothing credit is new to them — can you show a sample invoice and two worked examples (one slow ramp, one fast ramp)?
2) Overage concern: if we spike in usage for a short period (e.g., product launch), will we be penalized by lower rebates vs. a blended model?
3) Commitment term: is the proposal tied to a 12-month commitment? What flexibility do we have to re-open thresholds mid-term if market/usage changes?

Also looping in procurement@ezpay.com for contract/PO questions. If you can share a one-page summary and the sample invoice examples, that would help speed our internal review.

Best,
Emily Chen
Head of Platform, EzPay
emily@ezpay.com

On Fri, Apr 10, 2027 at 09:12 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference> wrote:
> (original message omitted)
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Folks — forwarding Emily's reply (excerpted below). Procurement is worried about complexity and overage spikes. Can we propose a defensible bridging approach that keeps margin integrity for Redwood but addresses their "simplicity" ask?

Key asks from Emily/procurement:
- Provide two worked invoice examples (slow ramp vs fast ramp)
- Clarify how overage spikes are treated vs blended alternatives
- Confirm term flexibility (can they re-open thresholds if usage materially deviates)

My suggested options to evaluate: 
A) Retroactive marginal with a 90-day smoothing bridge: rebates applied retroactively but we provide a bill credit smoothing buffer that attenuates a step cliff in the first quarter.
B) Hybrid monthly blended floor + marginal upside: apply a floor blended rate for monthly billing and then true-up quarterly with marginal rebates (this makes monthly invoices predictable).
C) Simplified blended option: offer a blended effective rate at an agreed forecast with a runway for re-negotiation at 6 months. Lower upside but simplest for ops.

Please review and annotate in-line. I want to get back to Emily with a clear recommendation and sample numbers by EOD Monday.

Thanks,
Aisha
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Agree with the three-option breakdown. My recommendation (and the one I think minimizes ops friction) is Option B with a small tweak: quarterly true-up combined with a one-time ramp credit that phases out after Q1. Practical details below and I attached a working model (pricing_model_sheet.xlsx).

Recommended approach (Option B, detailed)
- Monthly invoice: apply a predictable floor blended rate equal to a conservative forecasted effective rate (e.g., $0.00042/token for Q1).
- Quarterly true-up: after quarter close, compute actual marginal rebates retroactively and apply as an invoice credit. This preserves marginal economics while keeping monthly invoices simple.
- One-time ramp credit: if actual Q1 usage < forecast by >20%, apply a smoothing credit up to 6% of billed amount (not to exceed $50k) to avoid an early churn risk.
- Overage: treat as incremental tokens above the top tier; rebate applied at marginal rates but capped at 15% (we can negotiate higher cap for higher commits).
- Term flexibility: 12-month initial commitment with a mid-term review at 6 months; thresholds can be adjusted by mutual consent if usage deviates >40% vs forecast.

Sample numbers (see attached workbook for math)
- Forecast: 120M tokens / quarter
- Floor blended monthly: $0.00042/token -> monthly invoice simplicity
- Quarterly retroactive calc: if actual tokens hit tier C, effective retroactive rebate increases effective rate by ~10-15% on marginal tokens

Impacts to Redwood: minor complexity in running quarterly true-ups (we already do this for several book-of-business deals). Finance ops: we should document the crediting mechanism clearly (invoice line item: "Quarterly rebate adjustment - EZPay").

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>

Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Hi Emily —

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday. As discussed, I put together a lightweight proposal that tries to balance EzPay's ask for predictability and your desire to capture upside. The attached PDF (EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf) contains the full table; below is the executive summary and a suggested mutual action plan.

Executive summary
- Baseline per-token list rate: $0.00045 (hosted API, text-generation)
- Anchored breakpoints (quarterly aggregated):
  * Tier A: 0 - 50M tokens -> baseline rate
  * Tier B: 50M - 200M tokens -> marginal rebate 12%
  * Tier C: 200M+ tokens -> marginal rebate 22%
- Transitional smoothing credit: for Q1 post-commit, we apply a retroactive blended credit up to 6% if you ramp slower than forecast (protects you vs. step cliffs)
- Overage treatment: marginal overage rebate capped at 15% (to avoid runaway discounts)

Why this structure? It preserves marginal economics (you only get the higher rebate for incremental tokens beyond a breakpoint) while adding an anchored smoothing credit so billing is readable for your finance team. This is a middle ground between pure blended and pure marginal approaches.

Next steps / asks
1) Internal confirmation: can procurement accept retroactive credits applied as invoice adjustments?
2) Would EzPay prefer rebates applied retroactively (credit back on invoice) or as upfront monthly bill-credit?
3) Can we aim for an LOI signature within the next two weeks to lock pricing for calendar Q3?

Proposed mutual action plan (high-level)
- Week 0: Aisha shares v1 proposal (attached)
- Week 1: Procurement review & confirm billing approach; Redwood legal to prepare LOI
- Week 2: Sign LOI; schedule technical kickoff

Attaching: EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf with the breakpoint tables and examples. Happy to hop on a short call to walk through the math.

Best,

Aisha Rahman

Aisha — thanks for the quick turn. I read the proposal and the structure makes sense in principle but our finance team has a few practical asks before we can move forward: 

1) Simplicity: our billing ops prefers either a simple blended effective rate applied monthly or a very clearly documented marginal process. The mix with a transitional smoothing credit is new to them — can you show a sample invoice and two worked examples (one slow ramp, one fast ramp)?
2) Overage concern: if we spike in usage for a short period (e.g., product launch), will we be penalized by lower rebates vs. a blended model?
3) Commitment term: is the proposal tied to a 12-month commitment? What flexibility do we have to re-open thresholds mid-term if market/usage changes?

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Folks — forwarding Emily's reply (excerpted below). Procurement is worried about complexity and overage spikes. Can we propose a defensible bridging approach that keeps margin integrity for Redwood but addresses their "simplicity" ask?

Key asks from Emily/procurement:
- Provide two worked invoice examples (slow ramp vs fast ramp)
- Clarify how overage spikes are treated vs blended alternatives
- Confirm term flexibility (can they re-open thresholds if usage materially deviates)

My suggested options to evaluate: 
A) Retroactive marginal with a 90-day smoothing bridge: rebates applied retroactively but we provide a bill credit smoothing buffer that attenuates a step cliff in the first quarter.
B) Hybrid monthly blended floor + marginal upside: apply a floor blended rate for monthly billing and then true-up quarterly with marginal rebates (this makes monthly invoices predictable).
C) Simplified blended option: offer a blended effective rate at an agreed forecast with a runway for re-negotiation at 6 months. Lower upside but simplest for ops.

Please review and annotate in-line. I want to get back to Emily with a clear recommendation and sample numbers by EOD Monday.

Thanks,
Aisha


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Recommended approach (Option B, detailed)
- Monthly invoice: apply a predictable floor blended rate equal to a conservative forecasted effective rate (e.g., $0.00042/token for Q1).
- Quarterly true-up: after quarter close, compute actual marginal rebates retroactively and apply as an invoice credit. This preserves marginal economics while keeping monthly invoices simple.
- One-time ramp credit: if actual Q1 usage < forecast by >20%, apply a smoothing credit up to 6% of billed amount (not to exceed $50k) to avoid an early churn risk.
- Overage: treat as incremental tokens above the top tier; rebate applied at marginal rates but capped at 15% (we can negotiate higher cap for higher commits).
- Term flexibility: 12-month initial commitment with a mid-term review at 6 months; thresholds can be adjusted by mutual consent if usage deviates >40% vs forecast.

Sample numbers (see attached workbook for math)
- Forecast: 120M tokens / quarter
- Floor blended monthly: $0.00042/token -> monthly invoice simplicity
- Quarterly retroactive calc: if actual tokens hit tier C, effective retroactive rebate increases effective rate by ~10-15% on marginal tokens

Impacts to Redwood: minor complexity in running quarterly true-ups (we already do this for several book-of-business deals). Finance ops: we should document the crediting mechanism clearly (invoice line item: "Quarterly rebate adjustment - EZPay").

If you want, I can draft the one-page summary (blurb + two examples) and Aisha can send it to Emily. Recommend we include the workbook as a supplement.

source.subject: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — schedule options
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@finly.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — schedule options

Hi Samantha —

Thanks again for the interest in running a hands-on kickoff with the routing policy team. We'd like to get a 90-minute working session on the calendar to do the following:

Agenda (90 min)
- 15m: Quick intros & success criteria (what Finly needs from the POC)
- 25m: Prompt walk-through and promptsets we should test
- 25m: Routing policy decisions (model tiers, fallback rules, region mapping)
- 20m: Load-test plan and next steps (dry-run timing, traffic profile, observability points)

Proposed session windows (pick one):
- Tue Apr 6 — 10:00–11:30 PT / 13:00–14:30 ET
- Wed Apr 7 — 15:00–16:30 PT / 18:00–19:30 ET
- Thu Apr 8 — 07:00–08:30 PT / 10:00–11:30 ET (our preference; mid-morning ET)

Pre-work
- If you can, please share a sample traffic profile (percentages by endpoint, typical request sizes) and any redlines for latency/cost.
- We attached a short runbook (rti-runbook-v0.9.pdf) with proposed roles for the session.

Logistics
- We'll run the hands-on session with a shared Drive doc for prompts and a short load plan for the dry-run. If you want to loop in DevOps or infra, please cc them when you reply.

Would Thu Apr 8 at 10:00am ET work for you? If not, tell us which of the other two slots is best.

Thanks —
Ava
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: rti-runbook-v0.9.pdf (13 KB)
From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@finly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Briggs <t.briggs@finly.com>, devops@finly.com
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 12:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — schedule options

Hi Ava —

Thanks — Thu Apr 8 at 10am ET works best for our team. I'm looping in Tom (DevOps) and our infra alias so they can help with environment access and traffic samples.

Quick asks from our side:
- Can we do a 20-minute pre-call on Fri Apr 2 to share a brief traffic profile and keys/hosts for the dry-run?
- We will attach a small sample of March traffic (anonymized) for planning.

If you can confirm the dry-run window (we prefer Wed Apr 7 late afternoon PT) and who will own the load generator, that would help.

Best,
Samantha
Samantha Lee
Head of Integrations, Finly
Samantha.Lee@finly.com | +1 (646) 555-0220

Attachment: (will send traffic sample as ZIP)
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — initial prework

Ava / team —

Putting together a short model matrix to bring to the session. Attached is a draft shortlist (model_shortlist.xlsx) with recommended default fallback order and quantization options. High level suggestions:

- Tier 1 (low-latency, higher cost): Redwood-optimized gpt-4o-small-quant — good for critical endpoints with strict latency SLAs.
- Tier 2 (balanced): vetted open LLM v1.3 (8-bit) — good quality/cost tradeoff for general traffic.
- Tier 3 (economic): Claude-lite / instruction-tuned small models — best for background and batch tasks.

Fallback policy (proposed defaults)
- If Tier 1 latency >= 200ms P50 or a 5% error spike, failover to Tier 2 for 60s with circuit-breaker backoff.
- Region mapping: prefer region nearest to customer; if capacity constrained, route to secondary region within same GDPR boundary.

Rohan — can you check capacity and whether we can reserve a small dedicated pool for the dry-run on Apr 7? Connor, Evelyn — please flag any models you think shouldn't be on the shortlist.

Marcus
Lead, Inference Ops
marcus.lin@redwood.com

Attachment: model_shortlist.xlsx (6 KB)
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@finly.com>, Tom Briggs <t.briggs@finly.com>, devops@finly.com
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Confirmed: Thu Apr 8 10:00am ET — plus dry-run details

Thanks everyone — booking notes below. Finalizing the invite now and will include links.

Confirmed schedule
- Dry-run (access + load generator test): Wed Apr 7, 14:00–15:00 PT (our infra will run a lightweight load to validate observability).
- Hands-on session (90m): Thu Apr 8, 10:00–11:30 ET.
- 20m pre-call to share traffic profile: Fri Apr 2, 09:00 PT / 12:00 ET (Ava, Samantha, Tom + 1 infra).

Action owners
- Ava / Marcus: runbook & model shortlist (we've attached runbook & Marcus' shortlist).
- Rohan: reserve a dedicated test pool for Wed Apr 7 load test and confirm instance types (please mark reserved in console).
- Samantha / Tom: share traffic sample + any IP allowlist for the dry-run.
- Monica: I'll ask you to synthesize a short failover runbook we can hand to Finly during the session.

Shared prep doc: https://drive.redwoodinc/repo/finly-routing-playbook (I'll add the prompts and the load plan). Sarah in CS is on standby for onboarding.

If anything changes, flag here — otherwise I'll send the calendar invites with the Drive link and a short pre-read.

— Ava
From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@finly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Tom Briggs <t.briggs@finly.com>, devops@finly.com
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Rohan Mehta <rohan.mehta@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Confirmed: Thu Apr 8 10:00am ET — plus dry-run details

Thanks — all confirmed from Finly. Attached is a small, anonymized traffic bundle (traffic-sample-202703.zip). Tom will own the load generator and will validate the IP allowlist tomorrow.

We appreciate the clarity on owners — looking forward to the session.

Samantha

source.subject: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Team —

Quick background: Cloudstream is targeting a May 10 public launch for a major feature that depends on our multi-model routing (MMR) to orchestrate embeddings + retrieval + generator pipelines. We certificated a compatibility shim but the product team needs a few weeks beyond their original timeline to deliver the native MMR support (DEPLOY-834). Cloudstream is telling us the launch cannot proceed without the native behavior because performance/latency regressions with the shim break UX.

They are asking for a short commercial remedy so they can keep pricing/promotional commitments with their customers. Requested: a one-time pricing holdback (50% off hosted inference for 45 days post-launch) + a $25k one-time credit to bridge ARR recognition timing.

I attached a short commercial_summary.pdf and a draft term sheet. My ask: can we approve a conditional, timeboxed remedy that ties the concession to a product milestone (native MMR in prod by May 31) and to a usage cap?

Key constraints:
- Finance will need a clear dollar cap and accounting language (I included a draft in the term sheet).
- Product (Marcus) — can you confirm the May 31 target and whether a limited scope release (AP-surface only) would satisfy Cloudstream?
- CS (Maya) — what's the customer risk (churn / legal escalation) if we don't offer any remedy?

Proposed quick decision path: Karthik signs off on exec-level exception with the above conditions, I convey to Cloudstream and attach DS link for approval.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: commercial_summary.pdf, draft_term_sheet.docx

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Ben —

Thanks for the context and the clear ask. Short answers up front: we are targeting to have native MMR in staging by May 15 and GA by May 31 in the us-west-2 region. The remaining work is around routing policy stability and KV-cache eviction edge cases under high-concurrency scenarios.

We can commit to the May 31 GA date subject to the following guardrails:
- The release will be scoped to our generator 2.1+ path and the routing for embeddings->retrieval->generator for sequences up to 1k tokens initially.
- We will provide documented fallbacks and a migration runbook for Cloudstream.
- If any critical regressions surface in the 48 hours after GA, we will trigger a rollback window and the concession will be voided unless we deliver the fix within 7 days.

If Karthik and finance agree to a usage cap and a timebound credit, product is comfortable supporting this as an exec-approved commercial remedy. Marcus

Marcus Lin
VP, Product — Serving & Runtime
marcus_lin@redwood.com
Attachment: product_timeline.xlsx

From: Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Ben —

Customer perspective: Cloudstream has escalated to their CFO and product VP. If we do nothing they will accelerate conversations with two competitors who already have a multi-model orchestration story. Risk: conversion impact on their early enterprise pipeline and a potential pause on the pilot that would delay our FY27 revenue recognition.

CS recommendation: accept a conditional remedy to preserve the relationship and lock in a short-term commitment from Cloudstream (6 month minimum commit post-launch) + an explicit joint migration plan. We should require:
- A usage cap (suggestion: $150k billable tokens equivalent over 45 days)
- A written sign-off on the migration runbook and a shared rollback SLA

I can own the migration onboarding and the post-launch health checks.

— Maya
Maya Srinivasan
Director, Customer Success
maya_srinivasan@redwood.com

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 12:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

All —

I support a timeboxed, conditional commercial remedy with the conditions proposed. My decision principles: preserve revenue pipeline without setting an open precedent, and make any concession contingent on product deliverables and a clear cap. Final decision here:

Approve a one-time concession composed of:
- $25,000 one-time credit (applied up front against invoicing)
- 45 days of 50% off hosted inference up to a usage cap of $150,000 equivalent (after which normal rates apply)
- Concession void if native MMR GA is not in prod by 2027-05-31 or if a critical regression triggers the rollback condition Marcus outlined
- Customer must sign the attached draft_term_sheet.docx and commit to a minimum 6-month post-launch usage agreement to be eligible

Ben, please get Cloudstream to sign the term sheet and send me the DocuSign link. Marcus and Maya — please coordinate on the migration runbook and the monitoring plan; I want a short exec note on the plan by EOD tomorrow so I can record the approval and align finance.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
SVP, Global Sales
karthik_iyer@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jake Miller <jake@cloudstream.com>, Anita Rao <anita@cloudstream.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Jake / Anita —

Thanks for the call this morning. Redwood is prepared to offer a short, conditional commercial remedy to keep your launch on schedule while we finalize native multi-model routing. Summary of the approved package (subject to the attached term sheet and signatures):

- $25,000 one-time credit applied at invoice issuance
- 45 days of 50% hosted inference pricing, capped at $150,000 equivalent usage
- Cloudstream must sign the attached term sheet and agree to a 6-month minimum billing commitment post-launch
- This remedy is contingent on Redwood delivering native MMR to production by 2027-05-31 and the migration runbook acceptance; if GA is missed or a critical regression causes a rollback, the concession will be voided

Full term sheet and DocuSign link (for signature) attached. Ben

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
Attachments: draft_term_sheet.docx
