source.subject: Renewal negotiation: tiered surge credit + temporary SLA latency hold
messages:
From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 05 Mar 2027 10:14:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal conversation - cost and burst needs

Ben,

Quick note as we start planning our renewal: our usage profile changed a lot in Q4 — steady baseline but frequent short surges during peak routing windows. We need to reduce unit cost and preserve the ability to burst for short windows. Can Redwood offer a renewal that balances a committed discount with a predictable burst capacity? Procurement also asked about any SLA credits tied to latency spikes.

We're aiming to finalize terms by mid-March. Happy to jump on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Sam Patel
Head of Platform Engineering
Blue Harbor Logistics

P.S. attaching our expected monthly token curve (will send file separately).

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 07 Mar 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation - cost and burst needs

Sam — really appreciate the context and the timelines. Short answer: yes, we can structure a renewal that mixes a committed baseline (for a better unit price) plus a guaranteed burst pool and a small buffer of on-demand capacity. Two things I want to call out up front:

1) Pricing mechanics: we typically offer a stepped commitment (12 or 24 months) with a burst pool that is guaranteed for short windows. Customers on the stepped plan get a per-token discount on committed usage and a lower burst fee than pure on-demand.

2) SLA credits: we can include a temporary SLA latency hold — essentially a pricing credit if latency exceeds the target during covered bursts — but the credit calculation needs to be tightly defined so it isn't ambiguous.

I'll draft a concrete option set and loop in finance/legal for wording. Would you prefer a 12- or 24-month commit? If you expect continued growth, 24 months unlocks a deeper discount.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0189

--
Attachment: None in this message.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 09 Mar 2027 08:43:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: Blue Harbor renewal — proposed concession structure (need approval)

Team — copying the renewal thread with Blue Harbor. They've asked for a hybrid structure: committed baseline + guaranteed burst pool + an SLA-linked credit for latency during bursts. Summary of what I'm planning to offer (high level):

- Baseline commit: 12 months at 30% off hosted list rate for committed token volume (customer will expand committed volume by ~25% relative to current committed avg).
- Burst pool: guaranteed pool sized for expected peak windows (they'll pay a smaller recurring fee to reserve pool capacity; usage inside pool billed at committed unit rate, usage beyond pool billed at 1.5x committed rate).
- Temporary SLA latency hold: for the first 90 days post-renewal we propose a latency credit if 95th percentile latency during covered bursts exceeds 350ms; credit capped at 5% of monthly invoice and calculated pro-rata per incident.
- True-up: standard calendar-quarter true-up for overages; one free true-up month in year one to smooth migration.

Asks: 
- Mina: can CS/Finance sign off on the 5% credit cap and the 90-day temporary window?
- Marcus: legal needs to confirm the credit language and an amendment approach (we want to avoid a long-term SLA commitment here; keep it scoped to the 90-day stabilization).
- Grace: need your approval on giving a stepped discount of 30% for 12 months (or 38% for 24 months) — this is moderately aggressive.

I've attached the usage heatmap (operational-sla-history-2026.csv) and a starter redline doc. I plan to send the formal proposal to Sam Wednesday if we agree.

Thanks — Ben

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 11:17:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal: Blue Harbor renewal — proposed concession structure (need approval)

Ben — quick read from legal: the structure is reasonable with two suggested edits:

1) Replace the vague incident language with a simple metric calculation: credit triggers when 95th percentile P90 latency for requests routed through the committed/burst pool exceeds 350ms measured across a single calendar day; credit equals min(5% of that daily prorated invoice, $20k). This gives a hard dollar cap and a measurement window.

2) Instead of a free true-up month, propose a <30-day> forgiveness window for first-quarter overages to avoid perpetual extensions.

Mina: if finance is comfortable with the $20k cap and the 5% monthly cap, I can draft clean amendment wording. Grace — flag if the discount levels need to be pulled back.

— Marcus

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal attached: Renewal options and burst safeguard (Blue Harbor)

Sam — attaching the formal proposal and a short one-page summary of options:

Option A (12-month stepped commit): 30% off committed tokens, guaranteed burst pool sized for expected peak windows, on-demand beyond pool at 1.5x committed rate. Temporary SLA latency credit for first 90 days: triggers if 95th percentile latency during covered bursts > 350ms; credit capped at 5% monthly invoice or $20,000, whichever is lower. One-time forgiveness window for first-quarter overage.

Option B (24-month commit): 38% off committed tokens and slightly larger baseline capacity. Burst pool reserved at lower recurring fee. SLA credit same as Option A.

Operational notes: we included an addendum describing how the burst pool is reserved and billed, a simple true-up cadence, and an appendix with the last 12 months of latency and usage (operational-sla-history-2026.csv).

Next steps if you like either option: 1) procurement signature on the attached amendment; 2) DocuSign for the commitment; 3) opt into the reserved burst pool and schedule a cutover week for KV cache priming.

Attachments: redwood-proposal-2027-03-14.pdf, operational-sla-history-2026.csv, amendment-redlines-BlueHarbor.docx

Look forward to your feedback — happy to hop on a call to walk through numbers.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: redwood-proposal-2027-03-14.pdf

From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@blueharborlogistics.com
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 17:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposal attached: Renewal options and burst safeguard (Blue Harbor)

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear options and the appendix. Procurement and I reviewed Option A and have three small asks before we sign: 

1) Can the SLA credit measurement window be daily as you proposed, but with a 48-hour aggregation buffer so transient network blips don't immediately trigger credits?
2) Can we get a 30-day notice on when the burst pool size will change (so ops can coordinate their routing)?
3) Minor legal redline: include an explicit clause that the 90-day temporary SLA credit window can be extended by mutual written agreement if we both agree on an additional stabilization period.

If you can accept the three asks, procurement is ready to route the amendment for signature this week. We prefer Option A to keep flexibility in year one.

Sam
Head of Platform Engineering
Blue Harbor Logistics

On Tue, Mar 14, 2027 at 2:05 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Sam — attaching the formal proposal and a short one-page summary of options:
> ... (proposal attached)
>
> Best,
> Ben

We're aiming to finalize terms by mid-March. Happy to jump on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Sam Patel
Head of Platform Engineering
Blue Harbor Logistics

P.S. attaching our expected monthly token curve (will send file separately).

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 07 Mar 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation - cost and burst needs

Sam — really appreciate the context and the timelines. Short answer: yes, we can structure a renewal that mixes a committed baseline (for a better unit price) plus a guaranteed burst pool and a small buffer of on-demand capacity. Two things I want to call out up front:

1) Pricing mechanics: we typically offer a stepped commitment (12 or 24 months) with a burst pool that is guaranteed for short windows. Customers on the stepped plan get a per-token discount on committed usage and a lower burst fee than pure on-demand.

2) SLA credits: we can include a temporary SLA latency hold — essentially a pricing credit if latency exceeds the target during covered bursts — but the credit calculation needs to be tightly defined so it isn't ambiguous.

I'll draft a concrete option set and loop in finance/legal for wording. Would you prefer a 12- or 24-month commit? If you expect continued growth, 24 months unlocks a deeper discount.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0189

--
Attachment: None in this message.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 09 Mar 2027 08:43:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: Blue Harbor renewal — proposed concession structure (need approval)

Team — copying the renewal thread with Blue Harbor. They've asked for a hybrid structure: committed baseline + guaranteed burst pool + an SLA-linked credit for latency during bursts. Summary of what I'm planning to offer (high level):

- Baseline commit: 12 months at 30% off hosted list rate for committed token volume (customer will expand committed volume by ~25% relative to current committed avg).
- Burst pool: guaranteed pool sized for expected peak windows (they'll pay a smaller recurring fee to reserve pool capacity; usage inside pool billed at committed unit rate, usage beyond pool billed at 1.5x committed rate).
- Temporary SLA latency hold: for the first 90 days post-renewal we propose a latency credit if 95th percentile latency during covered bursts exceeds 350ms; credit capped at 5% of monthly invoice and calculated pro-rata per incident.
- True-up: standard calendar-quarter true-up for overages; one free true-up month in year one to smooth migration.

Asks: 
- Mina: can CS/Finance sign off on the 5% credit cap and the 90-day temporary window?
- Marcus: legal needs to confirm the credit language and an amendment approach (we want to avoid a long-term SLA commitment here; keep it scoped to the 90-day stabilization).
- Grace: need your approval on giving a stepped discount of 30% for 12 months (or 38% for 24 months) — this is moderately aggressive.

I've attached the usage heatmap (operational-sla-history-2026.csv) and a starter redline doc. I plan to send the formal proposal to Sam Wednesday if we agree.

Thanks — Ben

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 11:17:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal: Blue Harbor renewal — proposed concession structure (need approval)

Ben — quick read from legal: the structure is reasonable with two suggested edits:

1) Replace the vague incident language with a simple metric calculation: credit triggers when 95th percentile P90 latency for requests routed through the committed/burst pool exceeds 350ms measured across a single calendar day; credit equals min(5% of that daily prorated invoice, $20k). This gives a hard dollar cap and a measurement window.

2) Instead of a free true-up month, propose a <30-day> forgiveness window for first-quarter overages to avoid perpetual extensions.

Mina: if finance is comfortable with the $20k cap and the 5% monthly cap, I can draft clean amendment wording. Grace — flag if the discount levels need to be pulled back.

— Marcus

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal attached: Renewal options and burst safeguard (Blue Harbor)

Sam — attaching the formal proposal and a short one-page summary of options:

Option A (12-month stepped commit): 30% off committed tokens, guaranteed burst pool sized for expected peak windows, on-demand beyond pool at 1.5x committed rate. Temporary SLA latency credit for first 90 days: triggers if 95th percentile latency during covered bursts > 350ms; credit capped at 5% monthly invoice or $20,000, whichever is lower. One-time forgiveness window for first-quarter overage.

Option B (24-month commit): 38% off committed tokens and slightly larger baseline capacity. Burst pool reserved at lower recurring fee. SLA credit same as Option A.

Operational notes: we included an addendum describing how the burst pool is reserved and billed, a simple true-up cadence, and an appendix with the last 12 months of latency and usage (operational-sla-history-2026.csv).

Next steps if you like either option: 1) procurement signature on the attached amendment; 2) DocuSign for the commitment; 3) opt into the reserved burst pool and schedule a cutover week for KV cache priming.

Attachments: redwood-proposal-2027-03-14.pdf, operational-sla-history-2026.csv, amendment-redlines-BlueHarbor.docx

Look forward to your feedback — happy to hop on a call to walk through numbers.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: redwood-proposal-2027-03-14.pdf

From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@blueharborlogistics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@blueharborlogistics.com
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 17:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Proposal attached: Renewal options and burst safeguard (Blue Harbor)

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear options and the appendix. Procurement and I reviewed Option A and have three small asks before we sign: 

1) Can the SLA credit measurement window be daily as you proposed, but with a 48-hour aggregation buffer so transient network blips don't immediately trigger credits?
2) Can we get a 30-day notice on when the burst pool size will change (so ops can coordinate their routing)?
3) Minor legal redline: include an explicit clause that the 90-day temporary SLA credit window can be extended by mutual written agreement if we both agree on an additional stabilization period.

If you can accept the three asks, procurement is ready to route the amendment for signature this week. We prefer Option A to keep flexibility in year one.

Sam
Head of Platform Engineering
Blue Harbor Logistics

> Best,

source.subject: Structured Prepayment Release Schedule & VAT Treatment Checklist (FarisTech renewal)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 05 Mar 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Hi Anna —

Following our renewal call this morning, I wanted to put a simple proposed approach for the committed spend prepay and the drawdown schedule. High level:

- Redwood issues a single annual prepay invoice for the committed amount (USD or EUR as you prefer).
- We establish four milestone release buckets (Q2/Q3/Q4/Q1) for drawdowns; each bucket can be consumed monthly by your engineers and we will allocate credits against usage.
- Finance ops: we can either issue periodic credit memos as drawdowns happen or post monthly consumption invoices tied to the prepay ledger.
- VAT: we need to confirm whether FarisTech wants net EUR invoices (you are in DE) or if we keep USD invoices and have you apply reverse-charge.

I attached a tentative schedule (Redwood_Prepay_Schedule_Q2-2027.xlsx) and a short VAT mapping doc (VAT_Mapping_EU.pdf). Can you confirm:
1) preferred billing currency (EUR vs USD)
2) whether you require invoices net of VAT with your VAT ID on the invoice, or if reverse-charge is preferred on your side
3) PO/reference to include on the invoice

If you confirm net EUR and require supplier registration on our side, we’ll loop in tax to confirm next steps — otherwise we’ll include the reverse-charge language on the invoice and include your VAT ID.

Next steps from Redwood: once you confirm the above we’ll produce a draft invoice + credit schedule for sign-off.

Thanks —

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0124

From: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: finance@faristech.de, Thomas Green <t.green@faristech.de>
Date: Fri, 05 Mar 2027 16:47:22 +0100
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

HI Ben —

Thanks for the clear summary. A few quick answers:

- Currency: we prefer EUR (internal ledger and treasury are EUR).
- VAT: FarisTech GmbH will take reverse-charge where possible, but our accounting team prefers supplier invoices that show net amounts and a clear reference to reverse-charge rules. Our VAT ID: DE123456789.
- PO: please reference PO FTG-2027-450 when issuing.

We would like the invoice split into four release milestones (one per quarter) for internal budget allocations. If you can provide a version with 1) annual invoice + amortization schedule OR 2) four milestone invoices (we prefer the latter), that would be best.

I’ve copied our AP contact (finance@faristech.de) and Thomas for payment timing. Can you also provide a supplier form (standard vendor form) we can attach to our records?

Best,
Anna Müller
Head of Procurement | FarisTech GmbH
anna.mueller@faristech.de | +49 30 555 2345

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 06 Mar 2027 09:03:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Ben / team —

Thanks — a few accounting notes before we produce paperwork:

- If FarisTech wants net EUR invoices we need to confirm whether Redwood should register for VAT in DE. That creates additional compliance overhead (monthly filings). Alternative: Redwood invoices in EUR with a reverse-charge line and leaves VAT handling to the buyer — simpler from our side. We need Anna to confirm their preference and confirm they will accept reverse-charge wording on our invoice.
- For ledgering: our ops prefer to issue a single invoice referencing the annual committed amount, but with four invoice numbers for milestone recognition so AP can post to their periods. That covers both desires: 1 annual commitment on our books, 4 recognized invoice docs on theirs.
- Please ask for the vendor/supplier form (they requested it). If FarisTech insists on four independent invoices (not just milestone refs) we should ensure DocuSign signatures will be in place for each invoice to satisfy audit.

I'll prepare two templates: (A) "Annual invoice with four milestone refs" and (B) "Four discrete invoices (Q invoicing)" — we'll send both to Anna for selection. Marcus: can you confirm the ledger/bucket names and GL mapping so we can produce template fields?

Lauren Bishop
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.ai

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 08 Mar 2027 11:25:42 -0700
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Quick ledger note —

We should create distinct ledger buckets for the contract: FTG-2027-ANNUAL-01_Q2, _Q3, _Q4, _Q1. Each bucket will hold the allocated prepay credits. When consumption occurs we debit the bucket and post a monthly usage memo. For accounting close: if Anna wants four invoices that is fine — we will map each invoice to the corresponding bucket. If we do one annual invoice with four refs we will still post revenue recognition per bucket on our side.

DocuSign / Invoicing workflow: we should route the initial invoice envelope to Anna and their AP contact; for multiple invoices we can prepare one envelope with four documents to speed signature. Also: include the following on each invoice: PO FTG-2027-450, FarisTech VAT ID DE123456789, and the phrase: "Reverse charge — Article 196 (intra-EU supply)" if they accept reverse-charge.

Attachments: I’ve dropped a sample invoice template and the suggested GL mapping into the shared Drive (link will be sent by Ops).

Marcus Lin
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

From: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: finance@faristech.de, Karin Meyer <karin.meyer@faristech.de>
Date: Tue, 09 Mar 2027 10:58:03 +0100
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Hi everyone —

Thanks for the details. FarisTech confirms the following:

- We prefer four discrete invoices (one per quarter) rather than a single invoice with refs. This helps our capex/opex allocation.
- Invoices should be in EUR and net of German VAT. Please include our VAT ID (DE123456789) and the line: "Reverse charge — Article 196 (intra-EU supply)".
- PO FTG-2027-450 must be on every invoice.
- Our AP contact is Karin Meyer (karin.meyer@faristech.de). Payment terms: 30 days from invoice date; we will initiate payment at milestone acceptance.

Also attached is our supplier onboarding form (FarisTech_Supplier_Form_Sample.pdf) — please complete the supplier section and return so we can onboard Redwood in our vendor system.

Best regards,
Anna

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Anna / team —

Appreciate the clarity — we’ll proceed with four discrete EUR invoices, net of VAT, each referencing PO FTG-2027-450 and including your VAT ID and the reverse-charge language. Action plan: 

1) Redwood to complete and return the supplier form (attached).
2) Marcus will generate four invoice documents and route via DocuSign for signature this week.
3) Lauren will upload the GL mapping and invoice templates to the HubSpot deal and attach the DocuSign envelope link.
4) Ben to share final invoice PDFs once signed.

Attachments: Prepay_Split-Invoice_Template.pdf (draft), Redwood_Prepay_Schedule_Q2-2027.xlsx, VAT_Mapping_EU.pdf

If there are any special remit instructions (IBAN/beneficiary) please send Karin’s details and we’ll ensure wire block references match.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0124

Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai


From: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: finance@faristech.de, Karin Meyer <karin.meyer@faristech.de>
Date: Tue, 09 Mar 2027 10:58:03 +0100
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Hi everyone —

Thanks for the details. FarisTech confirms the following:

- We prefer four discrete invoices (one per quarter) rather than a single invoice with refs. This helps our capex/opex allocation.
- Invoices should be in EUR and net of German VAT. Please include our VAT ID (DE123456789) and the line: \"Reverse charge — Article 196 (intra-EU supply)\".
- PO FTG-2027-450 must be on every invoice.
- Our AP contact is Karin Meyer (karin.meyer@faristech.de). Payment terms: 30 days from invoice date; we will initiate payment at milestone acceptance.

Also attached is our supplier onboarding form (FarisTech_Supplier_Form_Sample.pdf) — please complete the supplier section and return so we can onboard Redwood in our vendor system.

Best regards,
Anna


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Anna / team —

Appreciate the clarity — we’ll proceed with four discrete EUR invoices, net of VAT, each referencing PO FTG-2027-450 and including your VAT ID and the reverse-charge language. Action plan: 

1) Redwood to complete and return the supplier form (attached).
2) Marcus will generate four invoice documents and route via DocuSign for signature this week.
3) Lauren will upload the GL mapping and invoice templates to the HubSpot deal and attach the DocuSign envelope link.
4) Ben to share final invoice PDFs once signed.

Attachments: Prepay_Split-Invoice_Template.pdf (draft), Redwood_Prepay_Schedule_Q2-2027.xlsx, VAT_Mapping_EU.pdf

If there are any special remit instructions (IBAN/beneficiary) please send Karin’s details and we’ll ensure wire block references match.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0124
text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 05 Mar 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Hi Anna —

Following our renewal call this morning, I wanted to put a simple proposed approach for the committed spend prepay and the drawdown schedule. High level:

- Redwood issues a single annual prepay invoice for the committed amount (USD or EUR as you prefer).
- We establish four milestone release buckets (Q2/Q3/Q4/Q1) for drawdowns; each bucket can be consumed monthly by your engineers and we will allocate credits against usage.
- Finance ops: we can either issue periodic credit memos as drawdowns happen or post monthly consumption invoices tied to the prepay ledger.
- VAT: we need to confirm whether FarisTech wants net EUR invoices (you are in DE) or if we keep USD invoices and have you apply reverse-charge.

I attached a tentative schedule (Redwood_Prepay_Schedule_Q2-2027.xlsx) and a short VAT mapping doc (VAT_Mapping_EU.pdf). Can you confirm:
1) preferred billing currency (EUR vs USD)
2) whether you require invoices net of VAT with your VAT ID on the invoice, or if reverse-charge is preferred on your side
3) PO/reference to include on the invoice

If you confirm net EUR and require supplier registration on our side, we’ll loop in tax to confirm next steps — otherwise we’ll include the reverse-charge language on the invoice and include your VAT ID.

Next steps from Redwood: once you confirm the above we’ll produce a draft invoice + credit schedule for sign-off.

Thanks —

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0124


From: Anna Müller <anna.mueller@faristech.de>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: finance@faristech.de, Thomas Green <t.green@faristech.de>
Date: Fri, 05 Mar 2027 16:47:22 +0100
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

HI Ben —

Thanks for the clear summary. A few quick answers:

- Currency: we prefer EUR (internal ledger and treasury are EUR).
- VAT: FarisTech GmbH will take reverse-charge where possible, but our accounting team prefers supplier invoices that show net amounts and a clear reference to reverse-charge rules. Our VAT ID: DE123456789.
- PO: please reference PO FTG-2027-450 when issuing.

We would like the invoice split into four release milestones (one per quarter) for internal budget allocations. If you can provide a version with 1) annual invoice + amortization schedule OR 2) four milestone invoices (we prefer the latter), that would be best.

I’ve copied our AP contact (finance@faristech.de) and Thomas for payment timing. Can you also provide a supplier form (standard vendor form) we can attach to our records?

Best,
Anna Müller
Head of Procurement | FarisTech GmbH
anna.mueller@faristech.de | +49 30 555 2345


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 06 Mar 2027 09:03:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Ben / team —

Thanks — a few accounting notes before we produce paperwork:

- If FarisTech wants net EUR invoices we need to confirm whether Redwood should register for VAT in DE. That creates additional compliance overhead (monthly filings). Alternative: Redwood invoices in EUR with a reverse-charge line and leaves VAT handling to the buyer — simpler from our side. We need Anna to confirm their preference and confirm they will accept reverse-charge wording on our invoice.
- For ledgering: our ops prefer to issue a single invoice referencing the annual committed amount, but with four invoice numbers for milestone recognition so AP can post to their periods. That covers both desires: 1 annual commitment on our books, 4 recognized invoice docs on theirs.
- Please ask for the vendor/supplier form (they requested it). If FarisTech insists on four independent invoices (not just milestone refs) we should ensure DocuSign signatures will be in place for each invoice to satisfy audit.

I'll prepare two templates: (A) \"Annual invoice with four milestone refs\" and (B) \"Four discrete invoices (Q invoicing)\" — we'll send both to Anna for selection. Marcus: can you confirm the ledger/bucket names and GL mapping so we can produce template fields?

Lauren Bishop
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.ai


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 08 Mar 2027 11:25:42 -0700
Subject: Re: Structured prepayment release schedule & VAT checklist for renewal

Quick ledger note —

We should create distinct ledger buckets for the contract: FTG-2027-ANNUAL-01_Q2, _Q3, _Q4, _Q1. Each bucket will hold the allocated prepay credits. When consumption occurs we debit the bucket and post a monthly usage memo. For accounting close: if Anna wants four invoices that is fine — we will map each invoice to the corresponding bucket. If we do one annual invoice with four refs we will still post revenue recognition per bucket on our side.

DocuSign / Invoicing workflow: we should route the initial invoice envelope to Anna and their AP contact; for multiple invoices we can prepare one envelope with four documents to speed signature. Also: include the following on each invoice: PO FTG-2027-450, FarisTech VAT ID DE123456789, and the phrase: \"Reverse charge — Article 196 (intra-EU supply)\" if they accept reverse-charge.

Attachments: I’ve dropped a sample invoice template and the suggested GL mapping into the shared Drive (link will be sent by Ops).

Marcus Lin
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

source.subject: Need to shift demo — new security/procurement/infra stakeholders added
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@mediq.com>, Laura Chen <laura.chen@mediq.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2027 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Intro demo — proposed times and agenda
Attachments: Demo_Agenda_v1.pdf

Hi Samir / Laura,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — excited to show Mediq how Redwood can help reduce inference costs and standardize serving across your environments. I pulled together a short agenda (attached) and three proposed windows next week:

- Wed 2/24, 10:00–11:00 PT
- Wed 2/24, 14:00–15:00 PT
- Thu 2/25, 09:00–10:00 PT

Goal: 60-minute live demo + 15 minutes for procurement/security Q&A. If you expect additional reviewers (sec/infra/procurement), flag which teams and we'll expand the session.

Please confirm a preferred slot and any extra attendees.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

---
Redwood Inference — Make inference predictable and economical
From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@mediq.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Chen <laura.chen@mediq.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2027 15:29:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intro demo — proposed times and agenda
Attachments: Security_Questionnaire_Mediq.pdf

Thanks Aisha — appreciated. Quick update from our side: procurement and our security lead need to be on this call. Also want to include our infra/platform lead for the KV-cache and deployment discussion. Attendees so far:

- Samir Patel (Applied ML lead, Mediq)
- Laura Chen (Head of Product, Mediq)
- Priya Verma (Security, Mediq) — travel next week, unclear availability
- Carlos Ortega (Procurement, Mediq)
- Kenji Ito (Platform/Infra, Mediq)

Given that Priya will be on a field trip next week and Carlos is OOO Thurs afternoon, we can't guarantee the folks who need to sign off will make the 2/24–2/25 windows. Also, since you mentioned procurement questions, would prefer a slightly longer slot (75–90 min) so security can run the questionnaire and procurement can walk through contract questions.

Can we move this to the following week? I attached the security questionnaire so you can see the level of detail they'll expect.

Thanks — Samir
Samir Patel
Applied ML & Data Lead, Mediq Health
samir.patel@mediq.com | +1 617-555-2203
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@mediq.com>, Laura Chen <laura.chen@mediq.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2027 16:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Intro demo — proposed times and agenda (security + procurement added)
Attachments: MutualActionPlan_v3.xlsx

Folks — forwarding Samir's note. Mediq needs security, procurement, and platform on the call and their sec lead is traveling next week. They'd like a 75–90 minute session. Can you check availability next week and the week after?

Questions:
- Can we split into two segments (platform/infra deep dive + security/procurement review) if a single 90-min block is tight?
- Who from infra/platform should be on the call (I suggested Marcus for runtime/ops and Vivek for deployment/SLA details)?
- Will product/commercial need to join for procurement redlines? (Neha — can you backstop procurement Qs?)

Suggested internal attendees I put on tentative: Vivek, Marcus, Neha (cc'd). If another SE or solutions architect should join, flag here. I attached the latest mutual action plan draft; I updated milestones related to security assessment.

Thanks — I'll hold off on proposing new dates to Mediq until I hear availability.

Aisha
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@mediq.com>, Laura Chen <laura.chen@mediq.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Feb 2027 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Intro demo — proposed times and agenda (security + procurement added)
Attachments: Platform_Playbook_SA_notes.txt

Thanks Aisha — quick availability and plan from our side. I can cover deployment/SLA and run the platform/infra deep dive. Marcus can do the runtime/ops and cache/kv questions. Neha can support commercial/procurement Qs — she'll triage contractual asks and prepare a procurement summary.

Proposed approach to keep Mediq engaged while accomodating schedules:

Option A — Single session (preferred):
- Wed 10 Mar, 10:00–11:30 PT (90 min) — combined demo + security + procurement Q&A

Option B — Split sessions (if scheduling proves hard):
- Tue 9 Mar, 10:00–11:00 PT — Platform + Infra deep dive (technical)
- Wed 10 Mar, 10:00–11:00 PT — Security + Procurement (questionnaire walkthrough + contract touchpoints)

We can bring a solutions architect to the Platform session to run a short KV-cache demo and a security engineer to the sec session for the questionnaire walkthrough. Marcus and I will prepare a 15-min appendix on fallback/failover and caching to address their cost/latency concerns.

A couple of asks for Mediq to confirm in the calendar invite:
- Final attendee list (who will sign off on infra/security/procurement)
- Any specific SSO/VPC questions ahead of time

If Aisha confirms, I'll block the times and send internal calendar holds.

Vivek Kulkarni
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@mediq.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Feb 2027 15:42:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Intro demo — proposed times and agenda (security + procurement added)
Attachments: Final_Attendees_List.xlsx

Hi all — thanks for the quick coordination. Option A on Wed 10 Mar, 10:00–11:30 PT works for our steering committee. Priya's travel clears that week and Carlos confirmed availability on 3/10. We're comfortable with a combined session; procurement only needs the last 30 minutes for contract questions.

I've attached the attendee list (names + roles). Please send a calendar invite for the 90-min slot and any pre-read you'd like the security team to review before the meeting.

Look forward to it — Samir
Samir Patel
Applied ML & Data Lead, Mediq Health

- Wed 10 Mar, 10:00–11:00 PT — Security + Procurement (questionnaire walkthrough + contract touchpoints)

Hi Elena —

Great to connect earlier. Quick recap from our discovery call and a proposed path to get you into a hands-on technical session with the ML/infra team. We heard that Borealis' immediate priorities are: reducing per-request latency during peak inference, clarifying data isolation for PII, and validating throughput at 5–10 TPS for your 2k token average requests.

Proposed next step: a 60–90 minute technical session (engineers + infra + security) to:
- review current workload profile and sequence-length distribution
- validate model/quantization options and expected unit cost
- run a short capacity/latency Q&A and discuss fallback routing

- Thu 18 Feb, 13:00–14:30 (90m)

source.subject: SOC 2 Type II: snapshot checkpoint / bridge letter questions
messages:
From: Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@brightwave.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

Hi Aisha,

We're preparing Brightwave's internal compliance notes ahead of our procurement sign-off and have a couple of quick clarifications on the SOC 2 Type II you shared. Two items from our audit team: 

1) Inclusive dates: your report lists the period ending 2026-12-31 with supporting evidence for January–February 2026 in some of the exports we received. Can you confirm which months are formally within the attested period (i.e., is Feb 2026 fully covered or treated as a partial/month-of-transition)?

2) Point-in-time snapshots: for controls that rely on system snapshots (user provisioning, key rotation, routing rules), what exact timestamp is used for the "point-in-time" captures? Are those taken at 00:00 UTC on the snapshot date, or in local PST? Our auditors prefer explicit UTC timestamps to avoid daylight savings ambiguity.

Finally, can Redwood provide a short bridge letter confirming (a) the attested period, (b) the exact timestamp convention for snapshots, and (c) any known scheduled maintenance windows during the period we should note? If you can draft that and share, we can route to our auditors for sign-off.

Thanks — appreciate the quick turnaround.

Daniel
Daniel Cho
Head of InfoSec 
Brightwave Therapeutics

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@brightwave.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

Hi Daniel — thanks for the good questions. I copied Marcus (security ops) to confirm the technical snapshot behavior; I'll also pull together a bridge-letter draft and attach the SOC 2 PDF you requested. 

High-level answers while Marcus confirms the logs:
- Attested period: Our Type II report covers the period 2026-01-01 through 2026-12-31 inclusive. Any evidence we provided labeled Jan/Feb 2026 were example exports from early 2026; the formal attestation period is the calendar year 2026.
- Snapshot timestamp convention: Marcus will confirm, but we use UTC for point-in-time exports by default.
- Maintenance windows: there was one scheduled maintenance on 2026-02-14 (UTC window 02:00–04:00) that we recommend you list as a known maintenance event.

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Report_2026.pdf (redacted PII) and Bridge_Letter_Draft.docx — the bridge letter is a short template, please review and let us know any wording your auditors prefer.

Next steps: Marcus — can you confirm exact snapshot timestamp semantics (are snapshots taken at 00:00:00 UTC or at process-completion timestamps?), and whether exports for user provisioning are point-in-time dumps or rolling states?

Best,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Sales Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@brightwave.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 13:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

On Tue, Feb 16, 2027 at 10:03 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marcus — can you confirm exact snapshot timestamp semantics...?

Hi all — technical clarity below. Short answer: snapshots are UTC-aligned and the exports we hand to auditors are consistent point-in-time extracts. Details: 

- Snapshot cadence: Daily snapshots are taken at 00:00:00 UTC for stateful controls (user/provisioning, group memberships, KMS key metadata). The snapshot job checkpoints at that timestamp; any late writes after 00:00:00 are part of the next day's snapshot. 
- Rolling logs: Application and access logs are ingested in near real-time; for attestations we provide log slices matched to the snapshot timestamp (i.e., logs up to 00:00:00 UTC).
- Evidence exports: For provisioning controls we provide point-in-time CSV/JSON extracts (single-file) that represent the exact state at 00:00:00 UTC of the snapshot date.

One caveat: we had a scheduled platform maintenance on 2026-02-14 that required brief node restarts; that window was logged and excluded from some continuous availability metrics per auditor guidance. If QSR needs an explicit statement about that maintenance impact, we can add a short paragraph to the bridge letter.

Attaching a recommended phrasing below (Marcus) for Aisha to include in the bridge letter: 
"Redwood Inference confirms that point-in-time evidence provided for the attested period was produced using daily UTC-aligned snapshots taken at 00:00:00 UTC. Rolling logs were correlated to those snapshots; any scheduled maintenance windows during the period are documented and specifically noted in our evidence package."

Let me know if your auditors require hashes or signed exports — we can provide S3 object hashes.

Marcus Lin
Principal Security Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Rachel Miller <rachel.miller@qsr-audit.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@brightwave.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 17:05:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

Thanks Aisha/Marcus — that's helpful and aligns with what we need. A few quick notes from the audit desk: 

- Confirmation: 2026-01-01 through 2026-12-31 will be treated as the attested period (inclusive). We will record Feb 2026 as within the attested year; the early-2026 example exports are fine as supplementary evidence.
- Timestamp: 00:00:00 UTC snapshots are acceptable. Please include the exact phrasing Marcus suggested in the bridge letter.
- Maintenance: we will note the 2026-02-14 maintenance window as a scheduled event and do not expect that to disqualify coverage, provided it's called out in the bridge letter.
- Minor edit: in section 2 of the draft bridge letter please change "excluded from continuous availability metrics" to "excluded from specific availability measurements for the maintenance window" — that wording is clearer for our report footnotes.

If you update the bridge letter with that wording, send back a final PDF and we can attach our signed acceptance (I'll return the signed acceptance as QSR_Audit_Acceptance_20270216.pdf).

Regards,
Rachel Miller
Lead Auditor, QSR Audit Services

Attachments: QSR_Audit_Acceptance_20270216.pdf (signed acceptance stub)

Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

Hi Daniel — thanks for the good questions. I copied Marcus (security ops) to confirm the technical snapshot behavior; I'll also pull together a bridge-letter draft and attach the SOC 2 PDF you requested. 

High-level answers while Marcus confirms the logs:
- Attested period: Our Type II report covers the period 2026-01-01 through 2026-12-31 inclusive. Any evidence we provided labeled Jan/Feb 2026 were example exports from early 2026; the formal attestation period is the calendar year 2026.
- Snapshot timestamp convention: Marcus will confirm, but we use UTC for point-in-time exports by default.
- Maintenance windows: there was one scheduled maintenance on 2026-02-14 (UTC window 02:00–04:00) that we recommend you list as a known maintenance event.

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Report_2026.pdf (redacted PII) and Bridge_Letter_Draft.docx — the bridge letter is a short template, please review and let us know any wording your auditors prefer.

Next steps: Marcus — can you confirm exact snapshot timestamp semantics (are snapshots taken at 00:00:00 UTC or at process-completion timestamps?), and whether exports for user provisioning are point-in-time dumps or rolling states?

Best,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Sales Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@brightwave.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 13:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

On Tue, Feb 16, 2027 at 10:03 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marcus — can you confirm exact snapshot timestamp semantics...?

Hi all — technical clarity below. Short answer: snapshots are UTC-aligned and the exports we hand to auditors are consistent point-in-time extracts. Details: 

- Snapshot cadence: Daily snapshots are taken at 00:00:00 UTC for stateful controls (user/provisioning, group memberships, KMS key metadata). The snapshot job checkpoints at that timestamp; any late writes after 00:00:00 are part of the next day's snapshot. 
- Rolling logs: Application and access logs are ingested in near real-time; for attestations we provide log slices matched to the snapshot timestamp (i.e., logs up to 00:00:00 UTC).
- Evidence exports: For provisioning controls we provide point-in-time CSV/JSON extracts (single-file) that represent the exact state at 00:00:00 UTC of the snapshot date.

One caveat: we had a scheduled platform maintenance on 2026-02-14 that required brief node restarts; that window was logged and excluded from some continuous availability metrics per auditor guidance. If QSR needs an explicit statement about that maintenance impact, we can add a short paragraph to the bridge letter.

Attaching a recommended phrasing below (Marcus) for Aisha to include in the bridge letter: 
\"Redwood Inference confirms that point-in-time evidence provided for the attested period was produced using daily UTC-aligned snapshots taken at 00:00:00 UTC. Rolling logs were correlated to those snapshots; any scheduled maintenance windows during the period are documented and specifically noted in our evidence package.\"

Let me know if your auditors require hashes or signed exports — we can provide S3 object hashes.

Marcus Lin
Principal Security Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Rachel Miller <rachel.miller@qsr-audit.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@brightwave.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 17:05:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

Thanks Aisha/Marcus — that's helpful and aligns with what we need. A few quick notes from the audit desk: 

- Confirmation: 2026-01-01 through 2026-12-31 will be treated as the attested period (inclusive). We will record Feb 2026 as within the attested year; the early-2026 example exports are fine as supplementary evidence.
- Timestamp: 00:00:00 UTC snapshots are acceptable. Please include the exact phrasing Marcus suggested in the bridge letter.
- Maintenance: we will note the 2026-02-14 maintenance window as a scheduled event and do not expect that to disqualify coverage, provided it's called out in the bridge letter.
- Minor edit: in section 2 of the draft bridge letter please change "excluded from continuous availability metrics" to "excluded from specific availability measurements for the maintenance window" — that wording is clearer for our report footnotes.

If you update the bridge letter with that wording, send back a final PDF and we can attach our signed acceptance (I'll return the signed acceptance as QSR_Audit_Acceptance_20270216.pdf).

Regards,
Rachel Miller
Lead Auditor, QSR Audit Services

Attachments: QSR_Audit_Acceptance_20270216.pdf (signed acceptance stub)

To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick clarification on SOC 2 window and bridge letter

Hi Aisha,

We're preparing Brightwave's internal compliance notes ahead of our procurement sign-off and have a couple of quick clarifications on the SOC 2 Type II you shared. Two items from our audit team: 

1) Inclusive dates: your report lists the period ending 2026-12-31 with supporting evidence for January–February 2026 in some of the exports we received. Can you confirm which months are formally within the attested period (i.e., is Feb 2026 fully covered or treated as a partial/month-of-transition)?

2) Point-in-time snapshots: for controls that rely on system snapshots (user provisioning, key rotation, routing rules), what exact timestamp is used for the "point-in-time" captures? Are those taken at 00:00 UTC on the snapshot date, or in local PST? Our auditors prefer explicit UTC timestamps to avoid daylight savings ambiguity.

Finally, can Redwood provide a short bridge letter confirming (a) the attested period, (b) the exact timestamp convention for snapshots, and (c) any known scheduled maintenance windows during the period we should note? If you can draft that and share, we can route to our auditors for sign-off.

Thanks — appreciate the quick turnaround.

Daniel
Daniel Cho
Head of InfoSec 
Brightwave Therapeutics

source.subject: Meridian Labs: security playbook for private deployment (VPC / air-gap asks)
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>; secops@meridian-labs.com
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2027 15:12:00 +0000
Subject: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Hi Alice / Meridian security team,

Thanks again for taking the time today — great discussion. Below is a concise recap of what we heard + the follow-ups we promised. Please correct anything that doesn't match your internal notes.

Key items we captured
- PII scope: customer names, emails, order IDs, and clinician notes (free text) will be sent to Redwood in encrypted form for embeddings and generation.
- Retention ask: Meridian requires 30-day ephemeral retention for request/response payloads on the pilot, with logs retained for 180 days for audit.
- Logging: request-level audit logs are required (who called, model version, request_id). No full-text retention beyond 30 days unless explicitly approved.
- Residency/egress: pilot will run in a Redwood Private VPC in us-east-1. No external egress to third-party endpoints; outbound to Meridian endpoints allowed over approved peering tunnels only.
- Air-gap expectation: for a future on-prem iteration there may be a soft air-gap requirement — discussing feasibility and timelines separately.

Open technical clarification items (action owners):
1) PII redaction options: can Redwood redact stored embeddings automatically? (Action: Redwood — Ben to confirm embedding retention options)
2) KV/prefetch logging: how much token-level metadata is kept by the runtime? (Action: Redwood — Marcus to provide runtime logging matrix)
3) Network controls: can we enforce deny-by-default egress and granular port rules on the dedicated VPC? (Action: Redwood — Ava to produce VPC stub + recommended firewall rules)

Next steps and timeline
- Redwood to share an initial VPC network playbook and a one-page security checklist (today EOD). Attachment: redwood-arch-vpc-stub.pdf (stub).
- Meridian to share SOC extract and any mandatory CLA language (by end of week).
- Schedule a 45-min technical POC walk-through next week to validate egress and routing.

If that looks right I can convert these into a one-page mutual action plan.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Attachment: redwood-arch-vpc-stub.pdf (pdf)
Link: https://drive.redwood.com/s/meridian-vpc-playbook

From: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: secops@meridian-labs.com
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2027 09:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Ava — thanks for the detailed recap. This matches our notes. A few inline items and a doc drop: see answers below.

On Tue, 10 Feb 2027 at 15:12, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Key items we captured
> - PII scope: customer names, emails, order IDs, and clinician notes (free text) will be sent to Redwood in encrypted form for embeddings and generation.

Confirming: clinician notes are sensitive PHI; Meridian will only send them after DPA and willing to support encrypted-in-transit + at-rest keys. We will provide a limited dataset for the pilot (no more than 10k records).

> - Retention ask: Meridian requires 30-day ephemeral retention for request/response payloads on the pilot, with logs retained for 180 days for audit.

We expect retention control to be enforceable by tenant/config. Is this configurable per-deployment? If not, we need to know the manual process.

> - Logging: request-level audit logs are required (who called, model version, request_id). No full-text retention beyond 30 days unless explicitly approved.

We will send an extract from our internal SOC checklist; attaching a short excerpt that lists required audit fields. Attachment: meridian-soc-extract.pdf

> Open technical clarification items
> 1) PII redaction options: can Redwood redact stored embeddings automatically? (Action: Redwood — Ben to confirm embedding retention options)

Question for Redwood: do embeddings count as stored telemetry (i.e., subject to the 30-day retention) or do you treat embeddings as derived artifacts with separate retention?

> 2) KV/prefetch logging: how much token-level metadata is kept by the runtime? (Action: Redwood — Marcus to provide runtime logging matrix)

We need detail on whether KV cache keys or prefixes could contain identifiable tokens. If there is any chance, we need a plan to salt or hash keys.

> 3) Network controls: can we enforce deny-by-default egress and granular port rules on the dedicated VPC? (Action: Redwood — Ava to produce VPC stub + recommended firewall rules)

Yes — we require deny-by-default and explicit allow-lists. Also need to know whether the VPC supports private endpoints only (no NAT) and how we validate no unexpected outbound connections during the POC.

I think a short technical POC walkthrough focused on egress proofing and retention config will answer most items.

Best,
Alice Park
Director, IT Security — Meridian Labs
Attachment: meridian-soc-extract.pdf (pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2027 11:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Thanks Alice — good clarifications. A few technical clarifications from the runtime/retention side (short):

- Embeddings: treated as derived artifacts. By default we retain embeddings for 30 days for cache effectiveness, but we can enable a tenant-level policy to purge embeddings immediately after generation for the pilot. That purge can be automated via lifecycle policy.
- Logging matrix (summary): we record request_id, calling principal (API key or mTLS identity), model id/version, latency, token counts. We do NOT persist full request/response payloads beyond configured retention.
- KV cache: keys are deterministic by prefix + hashed request id. We can enable salted hashing to reduce collision/identifiability risk — Marcus will provide exact schema.
- Retention enforcement: configurable per-deployment; for Private VPC we run a policy agent that enforces lifecycle rules.

Actionable: Marcus, can you drop the runtime logging matrix (field names) to the shared drive so Alice can validate against your SOC fields?

Ava — our recommended minimal firewall policy for the pilot (high level):
- Deny all outbound by default
- Allow egress to Meridian CIDR blocks on TCP/443 only, and to Redwood internal monitoring endpoints over an approved peering (if agreed)
- Block DNS to public resolvers; provide a private resolver over the peering

I attached a short checklist spreadsheet we typically use to validate pilot readiness. Attachment: private-deploy-checklist.xlsx

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Feb 2027 09:24:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Alice / team — following up with the runtime artifacts + final pre-POC checklist.

On Thu, 12 Feb 2027 at 11:45, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> - KV cache: keys are deterministic by prefix + hashed request id. We can enable salted hashing to reduce collision/identifiability risk — Marcus will provide exact schema.

Attached: a short runtime logging matrix (fields listed), and a minimal test plan we'll run during the POC to validate egress controls and retention enforcement. Summary here:

Runtime logging matrix (high level)
- event_time
- tenant_id (hashed)
- request_id
- model_id
- caller_identity (mTLS CN or API key id)
- token_count_in
- token_count_out
- action (generate/embedding)

No full-text bodies are persisted beyond retention windows. We can hash tenant_id with a salt you control for extra protection.

Pre-POC checklist (we will run together):
1) Validate VPC peering and route tables (no NAT, deny-by-default egress enforced).
2) Verify TLS mTLS endpoints and confirm mutual authentication for admin APIs.
3) Run synthetic calls that include PII placeholders and confirm purge at 30s / 30 days (configurable).
4) Execute port-scan from inside VPC to ensure no unexpected outbound sockets.
5) Confirm monitoring endpoints are restricted to Redwood IPs (if Meridian requires zero external endpoints we will route monitoring over the peering).

Can we lock in a 45-min slot on Wed 17 Feb 2027 at 14:00 ET for the POC walk-through? If that works for Meridian I'll send a calendar invite and a short runbook.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Director, Runtime Engineering — Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com
Attachment: runtime-logging-matrix.csv (csv), private-deploy-checklist.xlsx (xlsx)
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/MD-2027-0421

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>; secops@meridian-labs.com
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2027 15:12:00 +0000
Subject: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Hi Alice / Meridian security team,

Thanks again for taking the time today — great discussion. Below is a concise recap of what we heard + the follow-ups we promised. Please correct anything that doesn't match your internal notes.

Key items we captured
- PII scope: customer names, emails, order IDs, and clinician notes (free text) will be sent to Redwood in encrypted form for embeddings and generation.
- Retention ask: Meridian requires 30-day ephemeral retention for request/response payloads on the pilot, with logs retained for 180 days for audit.
- Logging: request-level audit logs are required (who called, model version, request_id). No full-text retention beyond 30 days unless explicitly approved.
- Residency/egress: pilot will run in a Redwood Private VPC in us-east-1. No external egress to third-party endpoints; outbound to Meridian endpoints allowed over approved peering tunnels only.
- Air-gap expectation: for a future on-prem iteration there may be a soft air-gap requirement — discussing feasibility and timelines separately.

Open technical clarification items (action owners):
1) PII redaction options: can Redwood redact stored embeddings automatically? (Action: Redwood — Ben to confirm embedding retention options)
2) KV/prefetch logging: how much token-level metadata is kept by the runtime? (Action: Redwood — Marcus to provide runtime logging matrix)
3) Network controls: can we enforce deny-by-default egress and granular port rules on the dedicated VPC? (Action: Redwood — Ava to produce VPC stub + recommended firewall rules)

Next steps and timeline
- Redwood to share an initial VPC network playbook and a one-page security checklist (today EOD). Attachment: redwood-arch-vpc-stub.pdf (stub).
- Meridian to share SOC extract and any mandatory CLA language (by end of week).
- Schedule a 45-min technical POC walk-through next week to validate egress and routing.

If that looks right I can convert these into a one-page mutual action plan.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Attachment: redwood-arch-vpc-stub.pdf (pdf)
Link: https://drive.redwood.com/s/meridian-vpc-playbook



From: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: secops@meridian-labs.com
Date: Wed, 11 Feb 2027 09:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Ava — thanks for the detailed recap. This matches our notes. A few inline items and a doc drop: see answers below.

On Tue, 10 Feb 2027 at 15:12, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Key items we captured
> - PII scope: customer names, emails, order IDs, and clinician notes (free text) will be sent to Redwood in encrypted form for embeddings and generation.

Confirming: clinician notes are sensitive PHI; Meridian will only send them after DPA and willing to support encrypted-in-transit + at-rest keys. We will provide a limited dataset for the pilot (no more than 10k records).

> - Retention ask: Meridian requires 30-day ephemeral retention for request/response payloads on the pilot, with logs retained for 180 days for audit.

We expect retention control to be enforceable by tenant/config. Is this configurable per-deployment? If not, we need to know the manual process.

> - Logging: request-level audit logs are required (who called, model version, request_id). No full-text retention beyond 30 days unless explicitly approved.

We will send an extract from our internal SOC checklist; attaching a short excerpt that lists required audit fields. Attachment: meridian-soc-extract.pdf

> Open technical clarification items
> 1) PII redaction options: can Redwood redact stored embeddings automatically? (Action: Redwood — Ben to confirm embedding retention options)

Question for Redwood: do embeddings count as stored telemetry (i.e., subject to the 30-day retention) or do you treat embeddings as derived artifacts with separate retention?

> 2) KV/prefetch logging: how much token-level metadata is kept by the runtime? (Action: Redwood — Marcus to provide runtime logging matrix)

We need detail on whether KV cache keys or prefixes could contain identifiable tokens. If there is any chance, we need a plan to salt or hash keys.

> 3) Network controls: can we enforce deny-by-default egress and granular port rules on the dedicated VPC? (Action: Redwood — Ava to produce VPC stub + recommended firewall rules)

Yes — we require deny-by-default and explicit allow-lists. Also need to know whether the VPC supports private endpoints only (no NAT) and how we validate no unexpected outbound connections during the POC.

I think a short technical POC walkthrough focused on egress proofing and retention config will answer most items.

Best,
Alice Park
Director, IT Security — Meridian Labs
Attachment: meridian-soc-extract.pdf (pdf)



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2027 11:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Thanks Alice — good clarifications. A few technical clarifications from the runtime/retention side (short):

- Embeddings: treated as derived artifacts. By default we retain embeddings for 30 days for cache effectiveness, but we can enable a tenant-level policy to purge embeddings immediately after generation for the pilot. That purge can be automated via lifecycle policy.
- Logging matrix (summary): we record request_id, calling principal (API key or mTLS identity), model id/version, latency, token counts. We do NOT persist full request/response payloads beyond configured retention.
- KV cache: keys are deterministic by prefix + hashed request id. We can enable salted hashing to reduce collision/identifiability risk — Marcus will provide exact schema.
- Retention enforcement: configurable per-deployment; for Private VPC we run a policy agent that enforces lifecycle rules.

Actionable: Marcus, can you drop the runtime logging matrix (field names) to the shared drive so Alice can validate against your SOC fields?

Ava — our recommended minimal firewall policy for the pilot (high level):
- Deny all outbound by default
- Allow egress to Meridian CIDR blocks on TCP/443 only, and to Redwood internal monitoring endpoints over an approved peering (if agreed)
- Block DNS to public resolvers; provide a private resolver over the peering

I attached a short checklist spreadsheet we typically use to validate pilot readiness. Attachment: private-deploy-checklist.xlsx

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com



From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Alice Park <alice@meridian-labs.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Feb 2027 09:24:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Meridian: post-call recap & asks — private deployment security playbook

Alice / team — following up with the runtime artifacts + final pre-POC checklist.

On Thu, 12 Feb 2027 at 11:45, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> - KV cache: keys are deterministic by prefix + hashed request id. We can enable salted hashing to reduce collision/identifiability risk — Marcus will provide exact schema.

Attached: a short runtime logging matrix (fields listed), and a minimal test plan we'll run during the POC to validate egress controls and retention enforcement. Summary here:

Runtime logging matrix (high level)
- event_time
- tenant_id (hashed)
- request_id
- model_id
- caller_identity (mTLS CN or API key id)
- token_count_in
- token_count_out
- action (generate/embedding)

No full-text bodies are persisted beyond retention windows. We can hash tenant_id with a salt you control for extra protection.

Pre-POC checklist (we will run together):
1) Validate VPC peering and route tables (no NAT, deny-by-default egress enforced).
2) Verify TLS mTLS endpoints and confirm mutual authentication for admin APIs.
3) Run synthetic calls that include PII placeholders and confirm purge at 30s / 30 days (configurable).
4) Execute port-scan from inside VPC to ensure no unexpected outbound sockets.
5) Confirm monitoring endpoints are restricted to Redwood IPs (if Meridian requires zero external endpoints we will route monitoring over the peering).

Can we lock in a 45-min slot on Wed 17 Feb 2027 at 14:00 ET for the POC walk-through? If that works for Meridian I'll send a calendar invite and a short runbook.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Director, Runtime Engineering — Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com
Attachment: runtime-logging-matrix.csv (csv), private-deploy-checklist.xlsx (xlsx)
Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/MD-2027-0421

source.subject: ClearWave 8-week POC — success metrics, owners & next steps
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>, Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 Jan 2027 16:12:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Hi Sanjay and Claire,

Thanks again for a productive demo today. As promised, here’s a short recap and a proposed set of success criteria we can use to evaluate the 8-week POC. I focused on the areas you raised: latency, throughput, cost, and quality.

High-level asks / next steps:
- Confirm the sample dataset we should use (you mentioned ~25k anonymized queries).
- Sign the attached SOW so we can schedule reserved capacity for week 1.
- Confirm the stakeholder who will own evaluation and acceptance (QC/ML lead).

Proposed POC success KPIs (draft):
1) Latency: p95 generation time <= 220ms for 1–2k token prompts under our streamed response mode.
2) Throughput: baseline sustained 40 requests/sec with 80 concurrent KV-cache-backed sessions during busiest window.
3) Cost: average model cost <= $0.0007 per token (we’ll report token consumption and unit cost daily).
4) Quality: human-evaluated relevance >= 88% on your annotated 1k holdout set (sample rubric attached); automated BLEU/ROUGE comparisons for regression checks.
5) Reliability: <1% model error rate and endpoint availability >= 99.5% during the trial window.

Ownership (suggested):
- Redwood infra & perf: Marcus Lin (will own workload shaping, batching, and region routing)
- Evaluation & data: Rachel Kim (will coordinate test harness and human evaluation)
- Commercial & escalation: Ava Chen (single point of contact for delivery & contract items)

Tentative timeline (we can adjust after you confirm dataset and SOW):
- Week 0: SOW sign & data exchange (we provision dedicated pool)
- Week 1: Smoke tests + baseline profiling
- Week 2-3: Iteration on prompts & quantization profile; run throughput tests
- Week 4: Mid-POC checkpoint (metrics review; adjust acceptance thresholds if needed)
- Week 6-8: Final evaluation & acceptance decision

Attachments: ClearWave_POC_SOW.pdf, clearwave_sample_payloads.zip (sample queries + annotation guide)

Please confirm if these KPIs match what you discussed internally and who will be the acceptance owner on your side. I can push a signed SOW back today if the commercial terms look good.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198
From: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Feb 2027 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks Ava — this is great and aligns with our expectations. A few clarifications from our side:
- Dataset: we’ll provide 30k anonymized user queries plus a 1k holdout labeled set by Tue (2/2).
- Acceptance owner: Claire will be primary for the quality pass; she wants to be looped on the daily automated reports.
- Latency bar: Claire asked if p95 can be targeted to 200ms instead of 220ms — how much headroom do you expect?

Action: I’ll review the SOW with procurement; if terms are unchanged we can sign by Tue EOD.

—Sanjay
Sanjay Patel
Head of Product
ClearWave Analytics
From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Feb 2027 11:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Quick notes on feasibility:
- 200ms p95 is achievable for many of our LLM variants with streaming enabled, assuming avg prompt length stays under ~800 tokens and we route to an NVLink-backed pool. If they push longer prompts, latency jumps.
- Throughput: 40 req/sec sustained with 80 concurrent sessions is fine if we reserve a 4-GPU pool with continuous batching. Will coordinate with capacity team once SOW is signed.
- Cost: I can produce an expected cost/token table for two model classes (standard / low-cost quantized) by EOD Tue.

I’ll prep a short runbook for Claire that explains how we measure p95 and how to reproduce throughput tests.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Senior Infra Eng, Redwood Inference
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>, Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Feb 2027 09:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks Sanjay — that plan works. Claire, great to hear you’ll own the quality pass. A couple of operational notes:
- We measure p95 at the gateway after batching and just before byte encoding to reflect application latency. I’ll include the exact curl and k6 commands in the runbook.
- For quality: Rachel, can you confirm the rubric columns you need on the 1k holdout? I propose: Relevance (0–3), Factuality (0–1), and Response Appropriateness (0–2).

Next steps I’ll do once I get your go-ahead:
1) Send SOW for signature (DocuSign link)
2) Marcus will provision a 4-GPU reserved pool and post the capacity booking ETA
3) Rachel will get the evaluation harness spun up and share the annotation sheet by Wed EOD

Ava
From: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 03 Feb 2027 13:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Thanks — that rubric looks good. Couple of tweaks from QC:
- Relevance threshold: we’ll require >= 88% where relevance is 2 or 3 on your 0–3 scale.
- Factuality: we’ll flag any hallucinations; acceptable threshold is <= 3% hallucination rate on the holdout.

We’ll send the annotated 1k by EOD Thu (2/4). We can live with the 220ms p95 if Redwood can show a clear path to 200ms by the final evaluation (we need to justify this to our product leadership).

Claire Johnson
Head of Data Science
ClearWave Analytics
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Claire Johnson <c.johnson@clearwave.ai>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Feb 2027 09:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed 8-week POC metrics

Perfect — thanks for the rubric and timeline. I’ve attached the DocuSign SOW (link below).
DocuSign: https://docusign.com/link/rw-clearwave-7421

I’ll have Marcus send the capacity confirmation once provisioned and Rachel will share the evaluation sheet today. Looking forward to kick-off next week.

Best,
Ava
Enterprise Solutions | Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198
