messages:
From: Lydia Chen <lydia.chen@devbank.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: DevBank Security Team <ops-gov@devbank.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Jan 2027 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Request: Resilience artefacts and RTO/RPO pack for SIG review

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. As part of our SIG/CAIQ review we need a concise resilience pack focused on your BCP/DR posture — specifically:

- Current RTO and RPO commitments for the hosted API and Dedicated deployments (numbers and how they differ by tier).
- Evidence of most recent failover / restore tests (summary of test scope, date, outcomes).
- Any scheduled warm-cache / KV warm-up timings and measured recovery times.
- The runbook or playbook references you use for region failover.

If possible, please provide a 1–2 page summary PDF and the raw test logs or CSVs for the last full DR rehearsal. We have a tight internal review window and would like these by end of day Tuesday (local).

Appreciate it — Lydia

Lydia Chen
Senior InfoSec Manager
DevBank
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Jan 2027 10:03:00 -0500
Subject: Fwd: Request: Resilience artefacts and RTO/RPO pack for SIG review

Team — forwarding DevBank's ask (below). They want a short resilience pack + raw logs. Lydia gave a hard deadline (EOD Tuesday). Can we pull together:

- Marissa: 1–2 page summary for SIG/CAIQ context with RTO/RPO table and high-level test outcomes.
- Marcus: attach the last full DR rehearsal logs (CSV) and quick note on cache warm-up times; add any caveats re: Dedicated vs Hosted.
- Hannah: confirm whether the region failover runbook referenced is the public runbook or an internal variant and whether redaction is needed.

I'll collate and send. If we can't meet Tuesday, propose a short extension and send what we have.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 08:45:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Resilience artefacts and RTO/RPO pack for SIG review

Hi all — attaching the requested summary and the SIG section draft. Quick notes:

- RTO/RPO: Hosted API SLA target: RTO=30 minutes (limited scope for control-plane failover), RPO=15 minutes (best-effort for in-flight requests); Dedicated (reserved) pools: RTO=15 minutes for node replacement/fallback, RPO=5 minutes with KV prefix caching.
- Last full DR rehearsal: 2026-10-12 — primary->secondary failover across us-east/us-west for control plane; attached CSV is the post-run telemetry. Recovery times measured are in the CSV; summary in the PDF.
- KV warming: automated prefix-cache restore takes ~120–180s for prioritized prefixes; full KV ramp to steady-state depends on workload and may be up to 20 minutes. We call out mitigation (prefetch heuristics + sequential ramp).
- Runbook: we will provide a redacted runbook link (internal sections removed).

Attachments: rto_rpo_summary.pdf, recovery_test_log_20261012.csv, sig_section_BCP_responses.docx

Marissa Cole
Head of Compliance, Redwood Inference
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Lydia Chen <lydia.chen@devbank.com>, DevBank Security Team <ops-gov@devbank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 14:05:00 -0500
Subject: Resilience pack and DR rehearsal logs — Redwood -> DevBank

Hi Lydia / DevBank team,

Thanks for the request — please find our resilience pack attached and the raw rehearsal log for your reviewers. Highlights:

- rto_rpo_summary.pdf: 2-page executive summary with RTO/RPO commitments, test summary, and mitigation controls.
- recovery_test_log_20261012.csv: raw timestamps from our Oct 12, 2026 rehearsal (primary->secondary regional failover).
- sig_section_BCP_responses.docx: draft answers keyed to SIG/CAIQ BCP questions; please treat as draft during review.

Accessible resources: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcResiliencePack (contains an additional redacted runbook excerpt and the SRE post-mortem). Ticket for follow-up and tracking: JIRA INF-4821.

If you need any clarifications on the CSV columns or want the SRE team to walk through the reheasal telemetry with your reviewers, Marcus is happy to join a short call.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

---

source.subject: Follow-up: ops + model demo window & draft agenda
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Follow-up: ops + model demo window & draft agenda

Hi Sara —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Really helpful to get visibility into HelioHealth's prototype workload and the three primary goals you shared (reduce inference cost on peak traffic, tighten PII controls, and validate latency at 1–2k RPS bursts).

Next step we suggested on the call: a 60–90 minute technical deep dive with our ML infra + ops engineers to walk through a short proof-of-concept plan and run through integration constraints. To make scheduling simple I posted a few windows here: https://calendly.com/ben-carter/helio-tdx-60-90 — pick whichever works for your team.

Draft agenda (we can tighten based on who from Helio will attend):
- 5m: Introductions & desired outcomes
- 10m: Helio current architecture & traffic profile (you / platform)
- 25m: Redwood serving/runtime overview + quantization/latency tradeoffs (Marcus)
- 15m: Autoscaling, prefix/KV caching strategy, and cost levers (Grace)
- 10m: Security/dataflow & KMS considerations (Ben/Grace)
- 10–20m: Open Q&A / next steps / mutual action plan

Attachments: HelioPostDiscoveryNotes.pdf (call notes) and HHL-mutual-action-plan.xlsx (initial MAP draft). I also dropped a living agenda doc here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HelioMVPAgenda — feel free to add items.

Could you confirm who from Helio will join (platform lead + security / SRE recommended) and whether 60 or 90 minutes makes sense? If there's anything regulatory-specific you want on the agenda, we can add a 10–15m slot.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments:
- HelioPostDiscoveryNotes.pdf (summary)
- HHL-mutual-action-plan.xlsx (mutual action plan, draft)

From: Sara Patel <sara.patel@heliohealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Lee <tom.lee@heliohealth.com>, Priya Reddy <priya.reddy@heliohealth.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2026 11:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Follow-up: ops + model demo window & draft agenda

Ben —

Appreciate the quick recap and the agenda. I filled the Calendly link and selected the Tue 2027-01-05 10:00 PT slot (seems like the best overlap for our folks). Confirmed attendees from Helio: me (product/platform), Tom Lee (SRE lead), and Priya Reddy (security/compliance).

A couple of asks from our side before the deep dive:
- Can you include a short section on data retention and audit logs (we need to map to our SOC 2 checklist)?
- We may also want a short note on network egress costs — if you could surface any historical numbers for similar customers, that would help the business case.

I attached a short vendor-security intake (we'll fill the vendor portal but wanted to highlight the one-off items).

Thanks — looking forward to the session.

—Sara
Sara Patel
Head of Product (Platform) | HelioHealth
sara.patel@heliohealth.com

Attachments: HelioVendorIntake.docx

On Tue, Dec 21, 2026 at 09:03 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Sara —
> 
> Thanks again for the call this morning...

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@heliohealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2026 13:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Follow-up: ops + model demo window & draft agenda

Sara — great, thanks for picking a slot and for the attendee list. Tue 2027-01-05 10:00 PT works for Redwood as well — I've provisionally blocked 90 minutes so we have room if needed. Marcus and I will cover the runtime + cost levers; I'll take the autoscaling and caching walkthrough.

Per your asks, we'll add:
- 10m: Data retention & audit logs (how our control-plane + Private deployment options surface logs + KMS/HSM notes)
- 5m: Egress cost examples & rough sizing notes (we'll bring anonymized unit-costs from similar customers)

Proposed attendee roster (Redwood):
- Ben Carter (AE, customer lead)
- Grace O'Connor (Solutions Engineer, runtime/ops)
- Marcus Lin (Senior ML Infra Engineer, performance/quantization)

I've updated the shared agenda: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HelioMVPAgenda — added placeholders for Data Retention and Egress. Marcus, can you attach a short slide (2–3) summarizing quantization/latency tradeoffs and any perf numbers we can share?

Sara — once you confirm the vendor intake timeline we'll prep the SOC2 notes; if you prefer we can loop security into a separate 20-minute follow-up instead of in-session.

Thanks —
Grace O'Connor
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com

> On Tue, 21 Dec 2026 at 11:18 AM Sara Patel <sara.patel@heliohealth.com> wrote:
> > Ben —
> > 
> > Appreciate the quick recap...

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2026 16:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Follow-up: ops + model demo window & draft agenda

Perfect — thanks everyone. Calendar invite for Tue, Jan 5, 2027 10:00–11:30 PT sent to Sara/Tom/Priya and our team. Please accept when you get it.

Quick checklist before the deep dive:
- Helio to confirm sample traffic shape (CSV or perf snapshot) — Sara/Tom
- Helio to add any SOC2 intake answers to the vendor portal or share directly — Priya
- Redwood will attach Marcus' 2-slide perf summary and a one-pager on caching recommendations to the calendar event — Grace/Marcus

If anything changes, flag here and we'll adjust. Looking forward to it.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

[Calendar invite stub attached: Redwood-Helio-Technical-Deep-Dive.ics]

text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Follow-up: ops + model demo window & draft agenda

Hi Sara —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Really helpful to get visibility into HelioHealth's prototype workload and the three primary goals you shared (reduce inference cost on peak traffic, tighten PII controls, and validate latency at 1–2k RPS bursts).

Next step we suggested on the call: a 60–90 minute technical deep dive with our ML infra + ops engineers to walk through a short proof-of-concept plan and run through integration constraints. To make scheduling simple I posted a few windows here: https://calendly.com/ben-carter/helio-tdx-60-90 — pick whichever works for your team.

Draft agenda (we can tighten based on who from Helio will attend):
- 5m: Introductions & desired outcomes
- 10m: Helio current architecture & traffic profile (you / platform)
- 25m: Redwood serving/runtime overview + quantization/latency tradeoffs (Marcus)
- 15m: Autoscaling, prefix/KV caching strategy, and cost levers (Grace)
- 10m: Security/dataflow & KMS considerations (Ben/Grace)
- 10–20m: Open Q&A / next steps / mutual action plan

Attachments: HelioPostDiscoveryNotes.pdf (call notes) and HHL-mutual-action-plan.xlsx (initial MAP draft). I also dropped a living agenda doc here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HelioMVPAgenda — feel free to add items.

Could you confirm who from Helio will join (platform lead + security / SRE recommended) and whether 60 or 90 minutes makes sense? If there's anything regulatory-specific you want on the agenda, we can add a 10–15m slot.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments:
- HelioPostDiscoveryNotes.pdf (summary)
- HHL-mutual-action-plan.xlsx (mutual action plan, draft)

Appreciate the quick recap and the agenda. I filled the Calendly link and selected the Tue 2027-01-05 10:00 PT slot (seems like the best overlap for our folks). Confirmed attendees from Helio: me (product/platform), Tom Lee (SRE lead), and Priya Reddy (security/compliance).

A couple of asks from our side before the deep dive:
- Can you include a short section on data retention and audit logs (we need to map to our SOC 2 checklist)?
- We may also want a short note on network egress costs — if you could surface any historical numbers for similar customers, that would help the business case.

I attached a short vendor-security intake (we'll fill the vendor portal but wanted to highlight the one-off items).

Thanks — looking forward to the session.

—Sara
Sara Patel

Ideally we'd do a single intake to keep context, estimate 60–75 minutes.

Preferred windows: Dec 20–22 (AM CET) or Dec 21 (US afternoon). Can your team propose 2–3 slots and any pre-reads you need?

- For the pen-test scoping we'll request a brief inventory and network diagram up front.

Ben Carter

Subject: Re: Security intake — SOC 2 / DPA / Pen test kickoff

2) 12:40–13:10 CET — DPA / SOC 2 evidence map + evidence handoff process

- 12:40–13:10 CET — DPA / SOC 2 evidence map (Rachel, Ben)

source.subject: Capacity strain impacting VortexPay reserved pool — immediate mitigation & exec handoff
messages:
From: Elena Soto <elena@vortexpay.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 16:42:00 -0800
Subject: Spikes in latency + 5xx errors on reserved pool (VortexPay)

Alex —

We started seeing elevated tail latency and a rising 5xx rate on our production endpoints served from the reserved pool around 15:10 PST. Over the last two hours:

- P95 latency jumped from ~120ms to 420–700ms for several routes.
- 5xx rate climbed to ~4.8% (normally <0.3%).
- Throughput appears limited: token throughput dropped by ~22% vs baseline.

I've attached a short CSV with request samples and a small trace. We have a customer call Monday and this is blocking critical flows — requesting immediate help.

We are on the plan D-9134 (reserved set), so this is hitting production SLA.

Can someone from Redwood ops triage urgently?

Thanks,
Elena
Lead SRE, VortexPay
attachments: vortexpay_latency_samples_20261219.csv (csv), trace-snippet-20261219.txt (text)

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Soto <elena@vortexpay.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 17:00:00 -08:00
Subject: RE: Spikes in latency + 5xx errors on reserved pool (VortexPay)

Team — escalating. VortexPay (D-9134) is seeing sustained tail latency and increasing 5xxs against their reserved GPU pool. Customer says throughput down ~22%.

Immediate asks:
- Karthik / Marcus: can you confirm whether their dedicated pool is saturated? Check queue depth, GPU memory pressure, and any recent config rollouts in the last 24h.
- Naomi / Marissa: start comms with the customer (calm, concrete), and prepare a short list of remediation options and likely timelines. We should be ready to offer short-term credits if this materially impacts their SLAs.

source.subject: Request: Rapid-scale 30% onboarding discount & packaging reprice for CatalystX
messages:
From: James Park <james.park@catalystx.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Scaling pilot + pricing request (CatalystX)

Ben —

Appreciate the demo last week. CatalystX is greenlighting a rapid expansion of the pilot: we expect to move from ~50k tokens/day to ~1.2M tokens/day over the next 8 weeks if performance holds. Our legal + procurement asked for a clear commercial proposal to lock in capacity and enable an accelerated onboarding (we need dedicated routing and a predictable rate for our clinical inference workloads).

Two asks we need for an internal go/no-go next Tuesday:
- A one-time onboarding discount of 30% on billed tokens for months 1–3 to offset migration costs.
- A modified packaging: allow us to pre-buy a 9-month commitment at a blended effective rate (with a floor) rather than the standard tier.

We can commit to a minimum of $180k ARR if terms are reasonable; larger expansion is likely across their US clinics in H2. Can you confirm feasibility and send a term sheet? I attached our draft MAF (CatalystX_MAPlan_Q4.xlsx) and the org wants a marked term sheet back by Friday so procurement can begin redlines.

Thanks — James
James Park
VP Product, CatalystX
james.park@catalystx.com
+1 415-555-0142

Attachments: CatalystX_MAPlan_Q4.xlsx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Scaling pilot + pricing request (CatalystX) — request for VP/CFO clearance

Team — forwarding CatalystX ask. Summary and recommended path below. Please treat as time-sensitive (customer wants a reply by Friday).

Quick summary: CatalystX wants a rapid ramp (50k -> 1.2M tok/day) and requests a one-time 30% onboarding discount for months 1–3 + a 9-month blended commitment with an effective floor. They indicate ~$180k ARR minimum if we greenlight.

Why I recommend exception: strategic vertical fit (clinical inference), large expansion runway, and they’ll sign a 9-month prepay/commit which reduces churn risk. We have similar precedent but not at this scale or compression.

Recommended approval (what I’m asking you to sign off):
- One-time onboarding credit equal to 30% of billed token charges for months 1–3 (applied as a lump credit at month 1 invoice).
- Allow 9-month blended commitment structure with a guaranteed monthly minimum equal to 40% of the committed ARR for the first 3 months, then ramp up.
- Require dedicated routing to isolated pool (Dedicated) to preserve latency/SLO; pass-through cost uplift for dedicated pool to customer (we can cap at +8% surcharge).

Financial impact (preliminary):
- Upside: $1.08M ARR over 9 months at expected volume if they scale as projected.
- Downside (cost delta for 30% credit + dedicated uplift): see attached cost_impact_30pct.csv. Ballpark gross margin delta in Q1 ~ -6 percentage points versus standard pricing but recoverable by month 6.

Attachments: term sheet draft (term_sheet_redwood_v2.pdf), cost impact csv.

Ask: Marcus/Laura/Marissa — please review and advise ASAP. If we proceed, we need VP/CFO written clearance to move forward and send the marked term sheet back to James.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
(650) 555-0199

On Thu, Dec 10, 2026 at 2:03 PM James Park <james.park@catalystx.com> wrote:
> Ben —
> 
> Appreciate the demo last week. CatalystX is greenlighting a rapid expansion of the pilot: we expect to move from ~50k tokens/day to ~1.2M tokens/day over the next 8 weeks if performance holds. Our legal + procurement asked for a clear commercial proposal to lock in capacity and enable an accelerated onboarding (we need dedicated routing and a predictable rate for our clinical inference workloads).
> 
> Two asks we need for an internal go/no-go next Tuesday:
> - A one-time onboarding discount of 30% on billed tokens for months 1–3 to offset migration costs.
> - A modified packaging: allow us to pre-buy a 9-month commitment at a blended effective rate (with a floor) rather than the standard tier.
> 
> We can commit to a minimum of $180k ARR if terms are reasonable; larger expansion is likely across their US clinics in H2. Can you confirm feasibility and send a term sheet? I attached our draft MAF (CatalystX_MAPlan_Q4.xlsx) and the org wants a marked term sheet back by Friday so procurement can begin redlines.
> 
> Thanks — James
> 
> Attachments: CatalystX_MAPlan_Q4.xlsx

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 11:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Scaling pilot + pricing request (CatalystX) — request for VP/CFO clearance

Ben / Marissa —

Thanks for the summary. I agree the account is strategic, but I'll flag two immediate concerns from a margin and precedent perspective:
1) 30% one-time credit on billed tokens at this scale materially reduces our short-term unit economics — dedicated pool costs + credit could push first-quarter gross margin negative versus plan.
2) The proposed 9-month blended commitment with a low floor creates precedent that other enterprise customers will request, especially when linked to dedicated pool guarantees.

Alternative proposal I can support (middle ground):
- Approve a one-time onboarding credit of 20% (months 1–2), not 30% for three months.
- Require a 6-month minimum commitment rather than 9 months, with an optional extension tied to usage milestones.
- Approve dedicated pool routing but require CatalystX to cover 60% of dedicated uplift; Redwood covers the remaining 40% for months 1–3.

If leadership wants to proceed with Ben's 30%/9-month ask, we need explicit CFO signoff and a short-period carveout memo that documents why we made an exception (customer profile, CAC reduction, expansion likelihood, and exit clauses). I attached a quick cost delta file (cost_impact_30pct.csv).

Marcus Lin
Head of Commercial Strategy — Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

On Tue, Dec 15, 2026 at 9:22 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team — forwarding CatalystX ask. Summary and recommended path below. Please treat as time-sensitive (customer wants a reply by Friday).
> 
> Quick summary: CatalystX wants a rapid ramp (50k -> 1.2M tok/day) and requests a one-time 30% onboarding discount for months 1–3 + a 9-month blended commitment with an effective floor. They indicate ~$180k ARR minimum if we greenlight.
> 
> Recommended approval (what I’m asking you to sign off):
> - One-time onboarding credit equal to 30% of billed token charges for months 1–3 (applied as a lump credit at month 1 invoice).
> - Allow 9-month blended commitment structure with a guaranteed monthly minimum equal to 40% of the committed ARR for the first 3 months, then ramp up.
> 
> Attachments: term_sheet_redwood_v2.pdf, cost_impact_30pct.csv
>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 19 Dec 2026 11:12:00 -0800
Subject: Escalation: CatalystX pricing carve (Ben / Marcus recommend) — request for VP/CFO signoff by EOD

Laura —

We need your written signoff on the attached exception packet so we can return a marked term sheet to CatalystX today. Background in brief: Ben (AE) + Marcus (Commercial) recommend different concessions; Ben pushes for the full 30% x 3 months + 9-month blend; Marcus proposes a trimmed compromise (20% x 2 months + 6-month minimum). After customer conversations, CatalystX said the 30% onboarding credit is the gating item for their procurement team. They are prepared to sign a 9-month commitment and prepay 50% up front if we accept the onboarding credit.

Recommended final ask (sales view):
- One-time onboarding credit: 30% of billed tokens months 1–3, issued as a credit against month 1 invoice.
- 9-month blended commitment with prepay: require 50% prepayment at signature; remaining billed monthly against committed usage with a 40% minimum guaranteed for first 3 months.
- Dedicated routing: require CatalystX to accept dedicated pool surcharge (cap +8%) but Redwood will absorb up to 40% of that uplift for months 1–3.

Operational controls: add a 30-day out clause tied to a performance SLA and an overage clause if usage falls >30% below committed monthly minimum. Documentation attached (term_sheet_redwood_v2.pdf) and cost impact (cost_impact_30pct.csv).

From: James Park <james.park@catalystx.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Scaling pilot + pricing request (CatalystX)

Ben —

Appreciate the demo last week. CatalystX is greenlighting a rapid expansion of the pilot: we expect to move from ~50k tokens/day to ~1.2M tokens/day over the next 8 weeks if performance holds. Our legal + procurement asked for a clear commercial proposal to lock in capacity and enable an accelerated onboarding (we need dedicated routing and a predictable rate for our clinical inference workloads).

Two asks we need for an internal go/no-go next Tuesday:
- A one-time onboarding discount of 30% on billed tokens for months 1–3 to offset migration costs.
- A modified packaging: allow us to pre-buy a 9-month commitment at a blended effective rate (with a floor) rather than the standard tier.

We can commit to a minimum of $180k ARR if terms are reasonable; larger expansion is likely across their US clinics in H2. Can you confirm feasibility and send a term sheet? I attached our draft MAF (CatalystX_MAPlan_Q4.xlsx) and the org wants a marked term sheet back by Friday so procurement can begin redlines.

Team — forwarding CatalystX ask. Summary and recommended path below. Please treat as time-sensitive (customer wants a reply by Friday).

Quick summary: CatalystX wants a rapid ramp (50k -> 1.2M tok/day) and requests a one-time 30% onboarding discount for months 1–3 + a 9-month blended commitment with an effective floor. They indicate ~$180k ARR minimum if we greenlight.

Why I recommend exception: strategic vertical fit (clinical inference), large expansion runway, and they’ll sign a 9-month prepay/commit which reduces churn risk. We have similar precedent but not at this scale or compression.

Recommended approval (what I’m asking you to sign off):
- One-time onboarding credit equal to 30% of billed token charges for months 1–3 (applied as a lump credit at month 1 invoice).
- Allow 9-month blended commitment structure with a guaranteed monthly minimum equal to 40% of the committed ARR for the first 3 months, then ramp up.
- Require dedicated routing to isolated pool (Dedicated) to preserve latency/SLO; pass-through cost uplift for dedicated pool to customer (we can cap at +8% surcharge).

Financial impact (preliminary):
- Upside: $1.08M ARR over 9 months at expected volume if they scale as projected.
- Downside (cost delta for 30% credit + dedicated uplift): see attached cost_impact_30pct.csv. Ballpark gross margin delta in Q1 ~ -6 percentage points versus standard pricing but recoverable by month 6.

Attachments: term sheet draft (term_sheet_redwood_v2.pdf), cost impact csv.

Ask: Marcus/Laura/Marissa — please review and advise ASAP. If we proceed, we need VP/CFO written clearance to move forward and send the marked term sheet back to James.

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 11:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Scaling pilot + pricing request (CatalystX) — request for VP/CFO clearance

Ben / Marissa —

Thanks for the summary. I agree the account is strategic, but I'll flag two immediate concerns from a margin and precedent perspective:
1) 30% one-time credit on billed tokens at this scale materially reduces our short-term unit economics — dedicated pool costs + credit could push first-quarter gross margin negative versus plan.
2) The proposed 9-month blended commitment with a low floor creates precedent that other enterprise customers will request, especially when linked to dedicated pool guarantees.

Alternative proposal I can support (middle ground):
- Approve a one-time onboarding credit of 20% (months 1–2), not 30% for three months.
- Require a 6-month minimum commitment rather than 9 months, with an optional extension tied to usage milestones.
- Approve dedicated pool routing but require CatalystX to cover 60% of dedicated uplift; Redwood covers the remaining 40% for months 1–3.

If leadership wants to proceed with Ben's 30%/9-month ask, we need explicit CFO signoff and a short-period carveout memo that documents why we made an exception (customer profile, CAC reduction, expansion likelihood, and exit clauses). I attached a quick cost delta file (cost_impact_30pct.csv).

Marcus Lin
Head of Commercial Strategy — Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

Please indicate APPROVED / APPROVED WITH MODS / DENY and provide any wording you want reflected in the carveout memo for CFO records. We need direction by EOD to preserve the deal timeline.

source.subject: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Emma Russo <emma.russo@merchantco.eu>, AP Finance <ap@clientco.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 09:07:00 +0000
Subject: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps

Hi Emma / AP team,

Thanks again for the productive call last week. We want to make sure Redwood invoices and payment routing are aligned with MerchantCo EU's VAT residency to avoid delays on settlement. A few items we need to confirm before we finalize the December invoice: 

- Can you confirm the VAT ID that should appear on the invoice and provide the official VAT certificate (PDF)?
- Is MerchantCo set up to receive self-billed invoices, or do you require Redwood to issue invoices that include VAT and a reverse-charge notation?
- If reverse-charge applies, would your AP team accept a single invoice with the reverse-charge treatment, or do you prefer we split platform fees vs pass-through charges into separate lines/invoices for cross-border reporting?

If you can share the VAT certificate and the AP contact for routing, we'll generate a provisional settlement matrix and sample invoice for your review.

Appreciate a quick confirmation — we have a payment window next week and want to prevent a hold.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: none

From: Emma Russo <emma.russo@merchantco.eu>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Finance <ap@clientco.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 11:24:00 +0000
Subject: Re: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the note — attaching our VAT certificate. MerchantCo EU is registered in Germany: VAT DE274839102. Please use that ID on invoices. We are NOT set up for self-billing from Redwood at the moment; our AP requires vendor-issued invoices with a clear tax treatment line.

On reverse-charge: we typically accept the reverse-charge annotation on inbound invoices for B2B cross-border services, but our AP has asked for the following to avoid manual holds:

- Invoice must show: our VAT ID, vendor VAT ID, a statement: "Reverse-charge - EU VAT to be accounted for by the customer per Article 194 EU VAT Directive"
- A separate invoice line for reimbursable pass-through costs (if any) with net amounts only
- PDF copy of VAT certificate attached to the payment request (helps automated checks)

AP contact for routing and validation: finance.ap@merchantco.eu (they run the automated VAT checks).

I've attached MerchantCo_VAT_certificate.pdf.

If it helps, happy to join a 20-mins call tomorrow to walk through the fields.

Best,
Emma Russo
Head of Finance Operations
MerchantCo EU
emma.russo@merchantco.eu
+49 30 5556 7890

Attachment: MerchantCo_VAT_certificate.pdf (PDF, 120KB)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 13:10:00 +0000
Subject: Fwd: Re: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps

Ava / Priyom — forwarding Emma's response (below). Quick internal note:

- This is a classic reverse-charge case for platform services: we should mark invoices as reverse-charge and include MerchantCo's VAT ID and the statement Emma mentioned.
- For automation and to reduce AP friction, propose we send two things to MerchantCo: (1) single vendor invoice with reverse-charge and a separate invoice line for pass-through amounts (net only); and (2) a short "settlement matrix" that maps charges to jurisdictions and tax treatment — see attached draft.

Operational asks: 
- Priyom: please review the settlement matrix and flag any charge types that require different treatment (e.g., marketplace fees vs third-party pass-through).
- Ava: can you confirm whether Marketing/Platform credits are treated as discounts or separate taxable lines on our end (that affects invoicing labels)?
- Legal/tax may want to review the wording for the reverse-charge statement — I can loop them in if needed.

If MerchantCo's AP still rejects after we include the certificate and wording, we should offer a Payslip (two-line invoice) as a fallback.

Thanks,
Marcus
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
marcus_lin@redwood.com

-----Original Message-----
From: Emma Russo <emma.russo@merchantco.eu>
Sent: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 11:24
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Finance <ap@clientco.com>
Subject: Re: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps

(See attachment: MerchantCo_VAT_certificate.pdf)

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Emma Russo <emma.russo@merchantco.eu>, Atlas Payments <tax@atlas-pay.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 15:32:00 +0000
Subject: RE: EU entity payment flow & VAT residency — next steps

Hi Emma / Atlas Payments,

Thanks for sharing the certificate — received and uploaded to our portal. Marcus and Priyom helped draft a settlement matrix to reduce back-and-forth; attaching the draft XLSX. Summary / action items below: 

1) Invoice treatment: Redwood will issue a single vendor invoice dated Dec 31 that includes:
   - MerchantCo EU VAT ID (DE274839102) and Redwood vendor VAT ID
   - Statement: "Reverse-charge - EU VAT to be accounted for by the customer per Article 194 EU VAT Directive"
   - Separate line for pass-through reimbursements (net only)

2) Documents attached: redwood_settlement_matrix_draft.xlsx (maps charge types to tax treatment) and MerchantCo_VAT_certificate.pdf (from your email). Please confirm matrix rows for: platform subscription, overage tokens, marketplace passthrough.

3) AP routing: we'll send the invoice PDF and VAT cert to finance.ap@merchantco.eu and copy tax@atlas-pay.com as requested. If AP's automation still flags the invoice, we'll offer a two-line split invoice as fallback.

4) Meeting: propose a 20-min alignment call THU 16 Dec 09:00 CET to confirm fields and test a sample invoice — please confirm availability.

Next steps from your side: confirm acceptance of above, and confirm whether MerchantCo requires an additional supplier self-declaration form.

Thanks — aiming to avoid a payment hold on the next settlement run.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: redwood_settlement_matrix_draft.xlsx (Excel, 24KB); MerchantCo_VAT_certificate.pdf (as provided)

Thanks again for the productive call last week. We want to make sure Redwood invoices and payment routing are aligned with MerchantCo EU's VAT residency to avoid delays on settlement. A few items we need to confirm before we finalize the December invoice: 

- Can you confirm the VAT ID that should appear on the invoice and provide the official VAT certificate (PDF)?
- Is MerchantCo set up to receive self-billed invoices, or do you require Redwood to issue invoices that include VAT and a reverse-charge notation?
- If reverse-charge applies, would your AP team accept a single invoice with the reverse-charge treatment, or do you prefer we split platform fees vs pass-through charges into separate lines/invoices for cross-border reporting?

If you can share the VAT certificate and the AP contact for routing, we'll generate a provisional settlement matrix and sample invoice for your review.

Appreciate a quick confirmation — we have a payment window next week and want to prevent a hold.

Best,
Ava Chen

Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com

On reverse-charge: we typically accept the reverse-charge annotation on inbound invoices for B2B cross-border services, but our AP has asked for the following to avoid manual holds:

- A separate invoice line for reimbursable pass-through costs (if any) with net amounts only
- PDF copy of VAT certificate attached to the payment request (helps automated checks)

source.subject: Reignite: next steps on inference architecture after Ignite Summit
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@starkretail.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T15:23:00-08:00
Subject: Great to meet at Ignite — quick recap and suggested next steps

Hi Elena,

Really enjoyed our conversation at Ignite this morning — the demo at the retail innovation track was a great backdrop. Per our chat, Stark Retail is focused on low-latency in-store personalization for recommendations (sub-200ms SLO in region) and wants predictable unit costs across peak holiday traffic. A quick recap of what we discussed and how Redwood could help: 

- Goal: real-time personalization at point-of-sale and kiosk (<= 200ms median).
- Constraints: on-prem for PII, multi-region footprint, existing vector store (Faiss) for history.
- Suggested approach: Redwood Private in a VPC with a small Dedicated pool for store-level inference + prefix/KV caching and cost-aware routing to cheaper quantized variants during non-peak.

Action items I proposed:
1) Share a short PoC outline (what success looks like, metrics, timeline) — attached.
2) Quick technical sync with your infra/ML engineer to validate integration points (30–45m). Marcus (cc'd) can lead the architecture session.
3) Pricing/commit framing for a 3-month pilot to validate latency and unit costs.

I attached a short Ignite-deck and the PoC outline. If you’re ok with that sequencing, can we lock a time for a technical sync next week? My Calendly is below; Marcus and I are flexible mornings PST.

Calendly: https://calendly.com/aditya-redwood/30min

Thanks again for the conversation — excited to explore this with your team.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: ignite-inference-deck.pdf (12MB stub), stark-poc-outline.docx (3MB stub)
From: Elena Park <elena@starkretail.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Daniel Kim <daniel.kim@starkretail.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Great to meet at Ignite — quick recap and suggested next steps

Thanks, Aditya — great meeting you too. I shared this with Daniel (infra) and he had a couple of quick clarifying questions before we set the technical sync:

1) For the on-prem/private option, what does the control plane look like? Who retains keys and logs?
2) Can Redwood integrate with our existing Faiss index, or would you recommend migrating?
3) Any reference for handling cold-starts for new stores during ramp?

We’re roughly targeting a Feb pilot start to validate through the Q1 peak. Daniel is available next Tue/Wed morning PST for a 30m touch.

—Elena
Sr. Director, Data & AI | Stark Retail
elena@starkretail.com

On Fri, Dec 5, 2026 at 3:23 PM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message)
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@starkretail.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Daniel Kim <daniel.kim@starkretail.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-06T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Great to meet at Ignite — quick recap and suggested next steps

Elena — thanks for the quick reply and great questions. Answers inline and next steps below.

1) Control plane / keys / logs — Redwood Private gives you an isolated control plane. You keep customer-managed KMS (HSM/KMS options supported) for model keys and tokenization keys; logs can be shipped to your SIEM/CloudWatch/Splunk per our audit connectors. We can provide an architecture diagram in the tech sync.

2) Faiss integration — Redwood can call out to your existing Faiss index; we typically recommend keeping the vector store in your env and using secure connectors. Migration is optional and only recommended if you want to centralize KV caches into Redwood for lower latency at scale. Marcus can sketch the connector pattern in the sync.

3) Cold-starts — pragmatic approaches we use: pre-warming with representative prompts, short-term local KV warming for new store profiles, and conservative routing to a larger dedicated variant until caches are populated. We include these as config knobs in the PoC.

Next steps I propose:
- Lock technical sync: Marcus + Daniel + any infra folks (30–45m). Proposed windows: Tue 2026-12-14 9:00–12:00 PST.
- We’ll send an architecture sketch and a sample SOW for a 3-month pilot (deliverables + success metrics).

I attached the Ignite deck and PoC outline again; Marcus and I will prepare a two-slide architecture sketch to circulate before the call. If Tue 12/14 works, I’ll send a calendar invite.

Cheers,
Aditya

---
Aditya Rao | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@starkretail.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-12T09:41:00-08:00
Subject: quick nudge — Ignite follow-up and pilot timing

Hi Elena —

Just circling back on the sync and pilot timing. Wanted to make sure this didn’t fall between meetings after Ignite. We’re happy to be flexible on dates, but given your Q1 peak planning it’d be great to lock a short technical sync this week or next so we can get the architecture sketch and SOW out. 

If it helps, Marcus and I are available:
- Tue 2026-12-14: 9:00–11:30 PST
- Wed 2026-12-15: 8:30–10:00 PST

Or, feel free to pick any slot on my Calendly: https://calendly.com/aditya-redwood/30min

If timing has changed and this is lower priority for you right now, can you let me know so we can adjust outreach? Appreciate it either way.

Best,
Aditya
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai

On Mon, Dec 6, 2026 at 11:03 AM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (previous message with answers and attachments)

Migration is optional and only recommended if you want to centralize KV caches into Redwood for lower latency at scale. Marcus can sketch the connector pattern in the sync.

3) Cold-starts — pragmatic approaches we use: pre-warming with representative prompts, short-term local KV warming for new store profiles, and conservative routing to a larger dedicated variant until caches are populated. We include these as config knobs in the PoC.

Next steps I propose:
- Lock technical sync: Marcus + Daniel + any infra folks (30–45m). Proposed windows: Tue 2026-12-14 9:00–12:00 PST.
- We’ll send an architecture sketch and a sample SOW for a 3-month pilot (deliverables + success metrics).

I attached the Ignite deck and PoC outline again; Marcus and I will prepare a two-slide architecture sketch to circulate before the call. If Tue 12/14 works, I’ll send a calendar invite.

Cheers,
Aditya

---
Aditya Rao | Account Executive, Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@icare.health>, Aaron Patel <aaron.patel@icare.health>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Recap: Security evaluation & hosting options for iCare

Thanks again for taking the time today — quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment.

Key takeaways from our conversation:
- HIPAA coverage required; iCare needs an executed BAA before any PHI is processed.
- SOC 2 Type II attestation required for vendor selection; you asked for the most recent attestation and an index of audit evidence.
- Data residency: primary in us-east-1; egress restrictions in place for certain data classes.
- Retention / eDiscovery: 7 year retention requirement and ability to pull audit logs for compliance audits.
- Integration: EHR inbound/outbound channels (HL7 / FHIR) — low-volume but sensitive.

Deployment options we discussed (high-level):
1) Hosted API (fastest path): Redwood-hosted endpoints with executed BAA + optional VPC peering and dedicated tenancy for isolation.
2) Dedicated capacity: Reserved GPU pools for iCare with data isolation and SLA-backed throughput; supports customer-managed KMS.
3) Private (VPC or on-prem): Private control plane, can be deployed into your VPC or on-prem for strict air-gapped requirements (longer lead time).

Immediate asks from your side: timeline for procurement, whether iCare requires customer-managed keys (KMS/HSM), and the audit scope (internal vs third-party).

Next steps (proposed):
- I will share our SOC 2 attestation and a draft BAA for initial review (attached).
- Vanessa and Marcus will prepare a short technical appendix answering KMS, log retention, and PHI-handling specifics.
- If helpful, propose a technical deep-dive with your infra/security team next week.

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Attestation_2025.pdf, Redwood_BAA_Draft_v2.docx, network_diagram_vpc_peering.png

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.inference

From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@icare.health>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>, Aaron Patel <aaron.patel@icare.health>
Cc: procurement@icare.health
Date: Thu, 03 Dec 2026 09:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Recap: Security evaluation & hosting options for iCare

Aditya — thanks for the clear summary. A few clarifying items from our security and procurement teams:

1) Preferred hosting: our default preference is Dedicated or Private deployment for PHI workloads. Hosted may be acceptable only if we can validate logical separation, customer-managed KMS, and contractually guaranteed BAA terms.
2) Evidence needed: we need the SOC 2 Type II report (redacted is fine), an index of evidence items mapped to common criteria (logging, change control, vulnerability management), and a list of subprocessors.
3) Logs & PHI: clarify whether request/response logs can be configured to exclude or redact PHI, and the default retention period for request traces. Also need an export path for audit (CSV or SIEM integration).
4) Timeline: target vendor selection by the end of December; procurement wants a signed BAA + basic evidence by next week to start internal approval.

Can your team confirm the following explicitly (short bullet answers would be great):
- Does Hosted support customer-managed KMS?
- What is the shortest supported log retention window and can it be shortened to 30 days?
- For Dedicated, does Redwood support network-level VPC peering and private IPs for model endpoints?

Happy to set a technical call — our infra lead (Aaron) is available Dec 10–11 in the morning PST.

Thanks,
Samantha Lee
Director of Vendor Risk | iCare Health
samantha.lee@icare.health

On Tue, Dec 1, 2026 at 3:12 PM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference> wrote:
> [previous message omitted]

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.inference>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 04 Dec 2026 11:40:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: iCare questions — draft answers and artifacts to send

Aditya / Marcus — internal note with concise answers we can share with iCare. Please flag anything you want edited before I hand this off. I recommend sending the attestation + BAA draft and offering a technical deep-dive.

Short answers for the customer:
- Hosted + BAA: Yes. Our Hosted API can be covered by an industry-standard BAA. We can offer dedicated tenancy and VPC peering for extra isolation; however, Hosted shared-control-plane customers are logically separated. Customer-managed KMS is available for Dedicated and Private; Hosted supports Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) in specific regions — would need a short enablement window.
- Log retention / redaction: Default request logs retained 90 days. We can support configurable retention down to 30 days for PHI-sensitive customers. Redaction: we provide configurable request/response redaction policies and automated redaction helpers; we can also export sanitized logs for audits.
- VPC & private IPs: Dedicated supports private networking (VPC peering, private endpoints) and subnets in customer-owned VPCs. Private deployment (VPC or on-prem) offers full network isolation and control.
- Audit evidence: attached index maps evidence artifacts to SOC 2 control categories. For an audit we can provide copies of control operation evidence (change logs, vulnerability scans, access reviews) under an NDA / audit arrangement.
- Subprocessors: attached list; we are transparent about cloud infra providers and managed service partners.

Attachments included for outbound to iCare:
- Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Attestation_2025.pdf (redacted where appropriate)
- Redwood_BAA_Draft_v2.docx (starter BAA)
- network_diagram_vpc_peering.png (illustrates hosted with VPC peering and dedicated capacity)
- audit_evidence_index.csv (mapping of evidence items to SOC 2 criteria)

Suggested outbound language: share the attestation + BAA, call out the options (Hosted+BAA for quickest path; Dedicated or Private if they need stronger isolation), propose tech deep-dive on Dec 10, and loop procurement.

Vanessa Ortiz
Security & Compliance Manager | Redwood Inference
vanessa@redwood.inference

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@icare.health>, Aaron Patel <aaron.patel@icare.health>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 12 Dec 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: iCare — evidence & next steps (SOC2, BAA draft, technical deep-dive)

Samantha / Aaron — attaching the items we discussed and proposed next steps. Thanks again for the transparency on requirements.

Attachments included: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Attestation_2025.pdf, Redwood_BAA_Draft_v2.docx, network_diagram_vpc_peering.png, audit_evidence_index.csv

Short answers to your bullets:
- Hosted & BYOK: Hosted can be HIPAA-enabled under a signed BAA. BYOK (customer-managed KMS) is supported for Dedicated and Private by default; for Hosted we can enable BYOK for eligible regions — please flag if iCare requires KMS in-scope immediately.
- Log retention: default 90 days; we can set retention to 30 days for PHI-sensitive streams and provide sanitized exports for audits (CSV/S3 or SIEM integration).
- Dedicated networking: Dedicated supports VPC peering, private endpoints, and private IP addressing for endpoints. Private (VPC/on-prem) is also available if you need full control over the control plane.

Proposed next steps:
1) Review attached attestation + BAA draft. We can push the BAA into DocuSign if the legal language looks acceptable (DocuSign link: https://docusign.example/icare/BAA-ICARE-1234).
2) Technical deep-dive: Vanessa and Marcus can run a short architecture session for your infra team — suggested slots Dec 16 or Dec 17 morning PST.
3) Procurement: Lauren (cc'd) is ready to pick up timelines and terms once the BAA is agreed. Please share any procurement templates you want us to fill.

If helpful, we can start with Hosted + signed BAA for an initial proof-of-concept and migrate to Dedicated/Private for production. That path shortens time-to-value while meeting contractual obligations.

Open questions for you so we can prepare the deep-dive:
- Do you require CloudHSM-backed keys or are KMS-managed keys acceptable?
- Any specific SIEM or audit log schema we should prepare (Splunk, Sumo Logic, etc.)?

Thanks — will await confirmation on the deep-dive slot.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.inference

source.subject: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@stoneanalytics.com>, Nina Gómez <nina.gomez@stoneanalytics.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)

Hi Samir / Nina — thanks again for the discovery call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before kickoff: 

Summary of needs (from today)
- Real-time summarization for analyst notes (streaming output, sub-250ms per chunk)
- Embeddings index for semantic search across 18 months of reports (~200k documents)
- Tool calling to surface BI queries against their SQL layer and callouts to the document store

Success criteria we discussed
1) Latency: 95th perc token latency <= 300ms in our hosted trial environment
2) Throughput: support bursts up to 80 concurrent streaming sessions
3) Quality: key metric is F1 >= 0.78 on intent-extraction test set (we'll exchange test prompts)

source.subject: Request: strategic commercial guardrails and discount / MFN request
messages:
From: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finmetric.com
Date: 2026-12-06T08:22:00-08:00
Subject: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Ben —

Really enjoyed yesterday's demo. FinMetric is moving quickly and we have an internal budget window that closes mid-December. To move forward we need commercial clarity on a couple of non-standard items before procurement can sign:

- 40% introductory discount off list for year 1 (we anticipate 150k ARR initially).
- MFN clause tied to benchmark pricing/perf: if another vendor offers better unit pricing or published perf benchmark for comparable latency/throughput we expect parity or credit.
- 12-month exclusivity in the North American "Enterprise Accounting" vertical (we'd like Redwood to agree not to pursue other named enterprise accounting customers in the segment).

I'm attaching a short SOW and our procurement checklist. Can we get an answer by EOD Tuesday? Procurement is on a tight cadence and we have a pilot planned for January.

Thanks —
Elena Ortiz
Head of Strategic Partnerships
FinMetric Analytics
Attachment: FinMetric_SOW.pdf
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Elena —

Appreciate the quick follow-up and the clear ask. I'll loop in Deal Desk and Marcus on this thread so we can surface risks and get an exec decision. A few quick clarifying Qs that will help speed review:

1) The 40% — is that applied to all Redwood services in scope or just the inference hosting line-item in the SOW?
2) For MFN: are you asking for price parity only (pricing published to FinMetric) or parity against any customer/partner discount we offer?
3) For exclusivity: are you requesting a market carve (enterprise accounting segment only) or a named-customer restriction?

We're attaching our proposed-pricing spreadsheet for reference. Lauren — can you advise on the MFN/exclusivity risk matrix and what approvals we need? Marcus — flagging for potential margin impact and quota treatment.

Next steps: Lauren to provide redline recommendations. If exec signoff needed we'll escalate to Marcus.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-pricing.xlsx
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-07T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Team —

Summary of commercial/legal risk after initial review of FinMetric asks (see attached internal memo):

- 40% discount: within bounds for competitive pilot deals but requires approval above Deal Desk floor if applied to recurring list across all products. Recommend capping at 25% for bundled SKUs; 40% only for one-time professional services or a usage-based credit structure tied to milestones.

- MFN (benchmark pricing/perf): This is high risk. MFN that references "published benchmark" or "better unit pricing" can be open-ended and create perpetual obligation. Recommended approach: narrow the MFN to (a) direct list price published to FinMetric, (b) limited duration (12 months from signing), and (c) exclude marketplace/reseller channels and channel promotion discounts. We should also define what counts as a "benchmark" (specific test harness and workload).

- Exclusivity: 12 months full exclusivity across NA enterprise accounting is significant. Recommend instead: exclusivity limited to two named SKUs that are directly tied to the pilot, or a 6–9 month limited co-sell exclusivity for direct-sold enterprise accounts introduced by FinMetric. Also recommend a termination-for-convenience carve if performance targets are not met.

Ask for exec approval on the following recommended compromise:
- Approve up to 30% discount for Year 1 on the inference hosting line-item only (not across all product lines), with discounts ramping down in Year 2.
- Narrow MFN to direct list-price parity for 12 months and explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller pricing; include a defined benchmark test doc (we can attach a performance spec).
- Limit exclusivity to two SKUs and 9 months.

Attachments: term-sheet-redline.docx, RiskMemo_v1.pdf

Ben/Marcus — please advise whether you want me to proceed with a redline to FinMetric along the above or hold for different exec direction. If Marcus prefers, we can present the alternative floor (25% cap, 6 months exclusivity).

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Head of Deal Desk | Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-08T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails (EXEC DECISION)

Ben / Lauren / Elena —

Thanks for the analysis, Lauren, and for surfacing the ask, Ben. Exec decision below — I'm approving the deal to keep momentum but with specific, enforceable guardrails:

1) Discount: approve a 30% discount on the inference hosting line-item for Year 1 only. Discounts do not apply to professional services or add-on modules unless separately approved.

2) MFN: approve a narrowly scoped MFN limited to direct list-price parity for comparable published unit pricing, effective for 12 months from signature. Explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller programs, promotional campaign discounts, and partner-level rebates. Any benchmark/performance-based claims must be validated against Redwood's benchmark harness (we will provide the test spec).

3) Exclusivity: approve limited exclusivity covering two named SKUs (to be enumerated in the SOW) for 9 months. No blanket category or named-customer restrictions. All exclusivity language must include performance exit clauses and a defined SLA (availability + latency targets) that FinMetric can test against.

4) Risk controls: cap SLA credits at 10% of the annual contract value; require Legal to include an MFN sunset and audit rights limited to published receipts. Finance to confirm revenue recognition and quota treatment.

Lauren — proceed to incorporate these changes into the term sheet and send a redline to FinMetric. Ben — share the updated term sheet and the benchmark test spec when ready; ask FinMetric to confirm acceptance by EOD Wednesday to preserve the procurement cadence.

Marcus Lin
VP Sales | Redwood Inference

title: Request: strategic commercial guardrails and discount / MFN request
preview:
From: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finmetric.com
Date: 2026-12-06T08:22:00-08:00
Subject: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Ben —

Really enjoyed yesterday's demo. FinMetric is moving quickly and we have an internal budget window that closes mid-December. To move forward we need commercial clarity on a couple of non-standard items before procurement can sign:

- 40% introductory discount off list for year 1 (we anticipate 150k ARR initially).
- MFN clause tied to benchmark pricing/perf: if another vendor offers better unit pricing or published perf benchmark for comparable latency/throughput we expect parity or credit.
- 12-month exclusivity in the North American "Enterprise Accounting" vertical (we'd like Redwood to agree not to pursue other named enterprise accounting customers in the segment).

I'm attaching a short SOW and our procurement checklist. Can we get an answer by EOD Tuesday? Procurement is on a tight cadence and we have a pilot planned for January.

Thanks —
Elena Ortiz
Head of Strategic Partnerships
FinMetric Analytics
Attachment: FinMetric_SOW.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-06T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Elena —

Appreciate the quick follow-up and the clear ask. I'll loop in Deal Desk and Marcus on this thread so we can surface risks and get an exec decision. A few quick clarifying Qs that will help speed review:

1) The 40% — is that applied to all Redwood services in scope or just the inference hosting line-item in the SOW?
2) For MFN: are you asking for price parity only (pricing published to FinMetric) or parity against any customer/partner discount we offer?
3) For exclusivity: are you requesting a market carve (enterprise accounting segment only) or a named-customer restriction?

We're attaching our proposed-pricing spreadsheet for reference. Lauren — can you advise on the MFN/exclusivity risk matrix and what approvals we need? Marcus — flagging for potential margin impact and quota treatment.

Next steps: Lauren to provide redline recommendations. If exec signoff needed we'll escalate to Marcus.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-pricing.xlsx


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-07T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails

Team —

Summary of commercial/legal risk after initial review of FinMetric asks (see attached internal memo):

- 40% discount: within bounds for competitive pilot deals but requires approval above Deal Desk floor if applied to recurring list across all products. Recommend capping at 25% for bundled SKUs; 40% only for one-time professional services or a usage-based credit structure tied to milestones.

- MFN (benchmark pricing/perf): This is high risk. MFN that references "published benchmark" or "better unit pricing" can be open-ended and create perpetual obligation. Recommended approach: narrow the MFN to (a) direct list price published to FinMetric, (b) limited duration (12 months from signing), and (c) exclude marketplace/reseller channels and channel promotion discounts. We should also define what counts as a "benchmark" (specific test harness and workload).

- Exclusivity: 12 months full exclusivity across NA enterprise accounting is significant. Recommend instead: exclusivity limited to two named SKUs that are directly tied to the pilot, or a 6–9 month limited co-sell exclusivity for direct-sold enterprise accounts introduced by FinMetric. Also recommend a termination-for-convenience carve if performance targets are not met.

Ask for exec approval on the following recommended compromise:
- Approve up to 30% discount for Year 1 on the inference hosting line-item only (not across all product lines), with discounts ramping down in Year 2.
- Narrow MFN to direct list-price parity for 12 months and explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller pricing; include a defined benchmark test doc (we can attach a performance spec).
- Limit exclusivity to two SKUs and 9 months.

Attachments: term-sheet-redline.docx, RiskMemo_v1.pdf

Ben/Marcus — please advise whether you want me to proceed with a redline to FinMetric along the above or hold for different exec direction. If Marcus prefers, we can present the alternative floor (25% cap, 6 months exclusivity).

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Head of Deal Desk | Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ortiz <e.ortiz@finmetric.com>
Date: 2026-12-08T16:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: strategic partnership & pricing guardrails (EXEC DECISION)

Ben / Lauren / Elena —

Thanks for the analysis, Lauren, and for surfacing the ask, Ben. Exec decision below — I'm approving the deal to keep momentum but with specific, enforceable guardrails:

1) Discount: approve a 30% discount on the inference hosting line-item for Year 1 only. Discounts do not apply to professional services or add-on modules unless separately approved.

2) MFN: approve a narrowly scoped MFN limited to direct list-price parity for comparable published unit pricing, effective for 12 months from signature. Explicitly exclude marketplace/reseller programs, promotional campaign discounts, and partner-level rebates. Any benchmark/performance-based claims must be validated against Redwood's benchmark harness (we will provide the test spec).

3) Exclusivity: approve limited exclusivity covering two named SKUs (to be enumerated in the SOW) for 9 months. No blanket category or named-customer restrictions. All exclusivity language must include performance exit clauses and a defined SLA (availability + latency targets) that FinMetric can test against.

4) Risk controls: cap SLA credits at 10% of the annual contract value; require Legal to include an MFN sunset and audit rights limited to published receipts. Finance to confirm revenue recognition and quota treatment.

Lauren — proceed to incorporate these changes into the term sheet and send a redline to FinMetric. Ben — share the updated term sheet and the benchmark test spec when ready; ask FinMetric to confirm acceptance by EOD Wednesday to preserve the procurement cadence.

Marcus Lin
VP Sales | Redwood Inference

source.subject: Bridgecrest - request for bespoke p95 latency carveout and ops playbook
messages:
From: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Maya Reddy <maya.reddy@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the model outputs look promising. We have a narrow window to launch a latency-sensitive feature (real-time search suggestion) that requires a stronger p95 commitment than your standard SLA. Specifically:

- Target: p95 <= 100ms for text-generation inference at 512 tokens, sustained on expected QPS (burst to 300 TPS).
- Duration: initial 6-month commitment for the pilot, with a potential expansion if performance holds.

Our procurement team has asked for a written carveout or addendum that codifies this for the pilot (performance metric + operational runbook + rollback triggers). I attached a short PDF with our ask and estimated traffic patterns (Bridgecrest_SLA_Request_20261201.pdf).

Can Redwood support this via a dedicated pool / capacity reservation, or would you recommend a variant/fallback approach? We'd like to resolve commercial terms quickly — procurement will need a target DocuSign by Dec 15 to hit our internal launch.

Appreciate the quick guidance.

— Alex
Alex Cho
Head of Platform Partnerships
Bridgecrest.ai

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 09:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Thanks Alex — looping in infra and product to assess feasibility and the expected operational impact. Marcus / Grace: can you please review the attached ask and provide:

- A feasibility assessment (what we can meet today vs what requires dedicated capacity)
- An estimated incremental cost / RU for a dedicated pool to hit p95 <= 100ms at Bridgecrest's traffic profile
- Required runbook items (rollback triggers, throttles, fallbacks) we should include in the addendum

Proposed internal timeline:
- Dec 3: quick tech estimate from product/infra
- Dec 6: draft runbook + commercial ask for legal/procurement
- Dec 8–10: exec review (if needed)

CC'd Vivek in case we need SLA governance sign-off / escalation path.

Thanks — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 11:27:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Quick technical readout from infra: short answer — possible but conditional. Notes below. Attachment: latency-ops-estimates.csv (stub).

Baseline: our hosted p95 for comparable 512-token workloads on the current L2 family is ~140–180ms across multi-tenant pools under 99th percentile QPS.

Options to reach p95<=100ms for the pilot:

1) Dedicated GPU pool (preferred)
   - Pros: predictable tail latency, isolate noisy neighbors, we can tune batch sizes and kernel selection per-model.
   - Cons: cost jump (estimated +40–60% RU for reserved capacity at projected load).

2) Intelligent routing + KV prefix caching
   - Pros: lower incremental spend, best for repeat prefix patterns.
   - Cons: only helps if Bridgecrest traffic has high prefix locality.

3) Model variant (smaller but tuned) as fallback
   - Pros: graceful degradation with guaranteed lower latency; preserves uptime if dedicated pool saturates.

Operational asks if we proceed with dedicated pool:
- Canary deploy + 72-hour monitoring window (p95 telemetry in Datadog dashboard)
- Auto-fallback policy to L2 model with increased per-request guardrails when p95 crosses threshold
- Runbook sections: incident playbook, escalation matrix (include Redwood SLA governance contact), capacity scaling steps, contact list for Bridgecrest on-call

I'll attach the latency estimate spreadsheet. Need product to confirm which model family Bridgecrest wants to pin and whether they'll accept a fallback variant in the addendum.

— Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal Infra Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Sat, 5 Dec 2026 09:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

High-level governance note: Redwood can agree to a pilot-level carveout with conditions. Operational and legal prerequisites we need before any SLA promise:

- Signed addendum that defines the metric (exact inference config, tokenization, model version, dataset for test harness)
- Agreed rollback and incident thresholds (we should use metric-based guards, e.g., consecutive 5-minute windows where p95 > 120% of target)
- An ops-runbook itemizing who executes scaling steps and the time-to-scale expectations
- Commercial cliff: pilot pricing and a clear uplift for dedicated capacity; procurement must accept a capacity reservation with a minimum commitment for the pilot period

Product ask: Grace, can you draft the runbook skeleton (3–4 page) and include a short FAQ for procurement. Marcus: keep the latency spreadsheet ready for the commercial deck. Aisha: I will take point on the SLA governance review and prepare a one-slide exec summary if we need C-level sign-off.

I've uploaded a first-pass runbook draft to the internal drive and attached a markdown copy for review (redwood-ops-runbook-draft.md).

— Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Dec 2026 11:24:00 -08:00
Subject: Bridgecrest pilot path and next steps (addendum + runbook)

Alex / Maya / Procurement team — thanks for the patience and the clear ask. Short summary of what Redwood can commit for the 6-month pilot:

Agreed pilot approach:
- Dedicated reserved GPU pool sized for Bridgecrest's expected QPS (Marcus to provide precise RU delta).
- Measured p95 target: <= 100ms for the agreed inference config (model X, max tokens 512) with the following caveats:
  - Commitment is for the pilot window and contingent on Bridgecrest accepting the operational runbook and fallback policy.
  - Fallback: automatic routing to a tuned L2 variant if p95 breaches the threshold during sustained windows; this is explicitly allowed in the addendum.

Operational and commercial asks (action items):
- Bridgecrest: review and sign the addendum (DocuSign link below) by Dec 15 to keep the launch schedule. The addendum will reference the runbook and the model pin.
- Redwood (Marcus / Grace): finalize RU estimates and runbook draft by EOD Dec 6 (Grace to lead runbook).
- Redwood (Vivek): SLA governance will perform a final review upon runbook submission; will notify if exec sign-off is needed.

Links / docs:
- Addendum (DocuSign): https://www.docusign.com/tx/BRG-4521-addendum
- Internal runbook draft: https://redwood.atlassian.net/l/c/BRG-4521-runbook
- HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/BRG-4521

Next sync: propose Dec 8 at 10am PT to align on commercial terms and confirm sign-off owners. Please confirm availability.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

> On Sat, 5 Dec 2026 09:15:00 -0800 Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com> wrote:
> > High-level governance note: Redwood can agree to a pilot-level carveout with conditions. Operational and legal prerequisites we need before any SLA promise: ...
> >
> > — Vivek
> > Head of Customer Reliability
> > Redwood Inference
