text:
From: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Maya Reddy <maya.reddy@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the model outputs look promising. We have a narrow window to launch a latency-sensitive feature (real-time search suggestion) that requires a stronger p95 commitment than your standard SLA. Specifically:

- Target: p95 <= 100ms for text-generation inference at 512 tokens, sustained on expected QPS (burst to 300 TPS).
- Duration: initial 6-month commitment for the pilot, with a potential expansion if performance holds.

Our procurement team has asked for a written carveout or addendum that codifies this for the pilot (performance metric + operational runbook + rollback triggers). I attached a short PDF with our ask and estimated traffic patterns (Bridgecrest_SLA_Request_20261201.pdf).

Can Redwood support this via a dedicated pool / capacity reservation, or would you recommend a variant/fallback approach? We'd like to resolve commercial terms quickly — procurement will need a target DocuSign by Dec 15 to hit our internal launch.

Appreciate the quick guidance.

— Alex
Alex Cho
Head of Platform Partnerships
Bridgecrest.ai



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 09:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Thanks Alex — looping in infra and product to assess feasibility and the expected operational impact. Marcus / Grace: can you please review the attached ask and provide:

- A feasibility assessment (what we can meet today vs what requires dedicated capacity)
- An estimated incremental cost / RU for a dedicated pool to hit p95 <= 100ms at Bridgecrest's traffic profile
- Required runbook items (rollback triggers, throttles, fallbacks) we should include in the addendum

Proposed internal timeline:
- Dec 3: quick tech estimate from product/infra
- Dec 6: draft runbook + commercial ask for legal/procurement
- Dec 8–10: exec review (if needed)

CC'd Vivek in case we need SLA governance sign-off / escalation path.

Thanks — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 11:27:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

Quick technical readout from infra: short answer — possible but conditional. Notes below. Attachment: latency-ops-estimates.csv (stub).

Baseline: our hosted p95 for comparable 512-token workloads on the current L2 family is ~140–180ms across multi-tenant pools under 99th percentile QPS.

Options to reach p95<=100ms for the pilot:

1) Dedicated GPU pool (preferred)
   - Pros: predictable tail latency, isolate noisy neighbors, we can tune batch sizes and kernel selection per-model.
   - Cons: cost jump (estimated +40–60% RU for reserved capacity at projected load).

2) Intelligent routing + KV prefix caching
   - Pros: lower incremental spend, best for repeat prefix patterns.
   - Cons: only helps if Bridgecrest traffic has high prefix locality.

3) Model variant (smaller but tuned) as fallback
   - Pros: graceful degradation with guaranteed lower latency; preserves uptime if dedicated pool saturates.

Operational asks if we proceed with dedicated pool:
- Canary deploy + 72-hour monitoring window (p95 telemetry in Datadog dashboard)
- Auto-fallback policy to L2 model with increased per-request guardrails when p95 crosses threshold
- Runbook sections: incident playbook, escalation matrix (include Redwood SLA governance contact), capacity scaling steps, contact list for Bridgecrest on-call

I'll attach the latency estimate spreadsheet. Need product to confirm which model family Bridgecrest wants to pin and whether they'll accept a fallback variant in the addendum.

— Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal Infra Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Date: Sat, 5 Dec 2026 09:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 commitment for Bridgecrest integration

High-level governance note: Redwood can agree to a pilot-level carveout with conditions. Operational and legal prerequisites we need before any SLA promise:

- Signed addendum that defines the metric (exact inference config, tokenization, model version, dataset for test harness)
- Agreed rollback and incident thresholds (we should use metric-based guards, e.g., consecutive 5-minute windows where p95 > 120% of target)
- An ops-runbook itemizing who executes scaling steps and the time-to-scale expectations
- Commercial cliff: pilot pricing and a clear uplift for dedicated capacity; procurement must accept a capacity reservation with a minimum commitment for the pilot period

Product ask: Grace, can you draft the runbook skeleton (3–4 page) and include a short FAQ for procurement. Marcus: keep the latency spreadsheet ready for the commercial deck. Aisha: I will take point on the SLA governance review and prepare a one-slide exec summary if we need C-level sign-off.

I've uploaded a first-pass runbook draft to the internal drive and attached a markdown copy for review (redwood-ops-runbook-draft.md).

— Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Alex Cho <alex.cho@bridgecrest.ai>, Bridgecrest Procurement <procurement@bridgecrest.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Dec 2026 11:24:00 -08:00
Subject: Bridgecrest pilot path and next steps (addendum + runbook)

Alex / Maya / Procurement team — thanks for the patience and the clear ask. Short summary of what Redwood can commit for the 6-month pilot:

Agreed pilot approach:
- Dedicated reserved GPU pool sized for Bridgecrest's expected QPS (Marcus to provide precise RU delta).
- Measured p95 target: <= 100ms for the agreed inference config (model X, max tokens 512) with the following caveats:
  - Commitment is for the pilot window and contingent on Bridgecrest accepting the operational runbook and fallback policy.
  - Fallback: automatic routing to a tuned L2 variant if p95 breaches the threshold during sustained windows; this is explicitly allowed in the addendum.

Operational and commercial asks (action items):
- Bridgecrest: review and sign the addendum (DocuSign link below) by Dec 15 to keep the launch schedule. The addendum will reference the runbook and the model pin.
- Redwood (Marcus / Grace): finalize RU estimates and runbook draft by EOD Dec 6 (Grace to lead runbook).
- Redwood (Vivek): SLA governance will perform a final review upon runbook submission; will notify if exec sign-off is needed.

Links / docs:
- Addendum (DocuSign): https://www.docusign.com/tx/BRG-4521-addendum
- Internal runbook draft: https://redwood.atlassian.net/l/c/BRG-4521-runbook
- HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/BRG-4521

Next sync: propose Dec 8 at 10am PT to align on commercial terms and confirm sign-off owners. Please confirm availability.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@shopfront.com
Date: 2026-12-05T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Hi Ava,

We're seeing a significant degradation in checkout performance this morning during our peak window (08:45–09:15 PST). Key symptoms: 
- p95 steady but p99 jumped from ~450ms to ~2.8s starting ~08:50.
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on checkout API for ~15 minutes.
- throughput (requests/sec) held roughly equal — not a traffic surge.

This is impacting conversions on the storefront. I've attached traces and a small set of sampled p99 payloads. Can you triage and advise whether this is on your side (model serving) or ours (request patterns)?

Attachments: [shopfront-checkout-traces.zip (1.2MB)], [p99-samples.txt]

Thanks — need a quick heads-up to share with our ops team.

— Liam
Head of Platform, ShopFront
liam.oneill@shopfront.com
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:26:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Marcus / Rishi — forwarding an urgent report from ShopFront (Deal SF-4721). They see p99 spikes and 5xxs during their checkout peak; traffic didn't jump. Attached traces from customer. Can you: 
1) Triage root cause (queueing/backpressure/concurrency)
2) Apply short-term mitigations we can push in the next 15–30m
3) Draft a <5 min customer update I can send to Liam

Evelyn I cc'd for visibility — might need exec update if this persists.

Forwarded message:
> From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
> Date: 2026-12-05 09:12 PST
> Subject: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent
> 
> [original body and attachments]

— Ava
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Liam — thanks for the data and flagging this. We're on it. Quick update for your ops: 
- We've started a limited mitigation: enabling an immediate fallback to the smaller model variant (redwood-lite-1b) for checkout route to reduce per-request CPU.
- We've also temporarily set a conservative concurrency cap for your account route to 50 concurrent requests to ease queueing.
- SRE is investigating whether backpressure from long-context KV cache churn caused worker stalls.

Expect a follow-up in ~30m with concrete metrics. If the mitigations reduce errors, we'll propose a short credit for the affected window while we complete the postmortem.

Action items I own: confirm fallbacks applied, monitor p99/p50/p95 and 5xx, propose credit amount.

— Marcus
Senior Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@shopfront.com>
Date: 2026-12-05T09:56:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Checkout latency and error spikes — urgent

Marcus/Ava — quick technical summary and what we changed (executed at 09:40 PST):
Findings (initial):
- Root cause appears to be KV cache churn on long sequences for a subset of checkout requests; those requests incur heavy KV cache recompute and push worker threads into long-tail processing.
- Concurrency cap for ShopFront route was set higher than intended in a config merge last week (cap=250 vs intended 75). During a steady request rate this allowed many requests to queue behind long-running ops, inflating p99.
- Backpressure from workers wasn't being propagated cleanly to the edge router due to an internal circuit tolerance mismatch.

Immediate mitigations applied:
- Reduced concurrency cap to 50 (config pushed at 09:40).
- Enabled aggressive prefix/KV caching for the checkout route to reduce recompute for repeated context.
- Activated fallback routing to redwood-lite-1b for very long contexts (threshold 2k tokens).
- Throttled large-context non-checkout requests for 30m.

Result so far: p99 has dropped from the 2.8s peak down to ~620ms within 12 minutes; 5xx rate down to 0.9%. Monitoring continues.

Next steps (post-incident):
- Root-cause postmortem and fix for the config drift that set the cap to 250.
- Improve backpressure propagation in edge router (SRE epic).
- Add an automated alert when route cap diverges from policy.

Links: metrics dashboard: https://monitoring.redwood.ai/d/shopsf/SF-4721-latency

I'll draft the technical notes for the postmortem — Marcus/Ava please let me know wording for customer update.

— Rishi
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-05T17:44:00-08:00
Subject: Exec note — ShopFront incident and customer communication

Team — thanks for the fast containment and the clear runbook. A few asks before we close the loop with the customer tomorrow morning: 
1) Customer update: Marcus, send a concise update (what happened, what we changed, current impact) and an estimated timeline for postmortem. Keep it <200 words.
2) Credits: I pre-approved a goodwill credit up to 10% for the billing period pending Marcus's recommendation.
3) Postmortem owners: Rishi please lead the RCA; include a timeline and the config-drift remediation plan.
4) Comms: I'll notify Sales leadership and CS once the final text is ready.

Let's finalize the customer note by 09:00 PST tomorrow so ShopFront can communicate internally.

— Evelyn
VP, Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Standby→Warmup gap causing p95/p99 latency spikes
messages:
From: Ella James <ella.james@fintechco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: FintechCo Platform <platform@fintechco.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: sudden p99 spikes during batch runway (first seen 08:50 PST)

Hi Ben,

We're seeing a sharp increase in p95/p99 latency for the Redwood hosted endpoint across our peak batch windows starting ~08:50 PST. p95 jumped from ~210ms to 1.4s and p99 is hitting 3.6s. This started after a surge where new GPU instances were brought up. The customer-facing jobs are timing out intermittently (2% errors), and this is impacting reconciliation jobs.

Attached is a short trace bundle and the CloudFront logs (fintechco_trace_12052026.zip). Can you confirm whether this aligns with a scaling warm-up problem on your side? We need immediate mitigation and a playbook — our ops lead is standing by.

Thanks,
Ella
Ella James
Platform Reliability Lead, FintechCo

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ella James <ella.james@fintechco.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 09:24:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: sudden p99 spikes during batch runway (first seen 08:50 PST)

Ella — thanks for the heads up and for the traces. I’m escalating to SRE and CS. Naomi (CS) will coordinate with your ops team; Marcus and Evelyn from SRE/Infra are looped in to triage the warmup behavior.

Immediate asks:
- Marcus/Evelyn: can you confirm whether any pool autoscaler policies kicked a cold pool (standby -> warm) for FintechCo at ~08:48–08:52 PST and whether instance init times spiked?
- Naomi / Ella: can you confirm if retries/fanout pattern changed on your side in the last 24h so we can rule out client-side amplification?

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 09:42:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: sudden p99 spikes during batch runway (first seen 08:50 PST)

Ben / team — quick initial findings from infra telemetry:
- Autoscaler did trigger a standby->warm transition for FintechCo pool at 08:47:33 PST (scale activity id=scale-7784).
- Instance boot times: median 38s, but 12% of instances took 2.1–3.8 minutes due to a delayed NVMe firmware init on the current image (kernel 5.17 + vendor NVMe driver v2.4).
- When the pool is in the 'initializing' phase, tokenization and KV cache warming are deferred — requests are served by cold model processes that pay heavier attention memory setup, causing head latency to spike (seen in our trace).
- p95/p99 correlated to the proportion of requests routed to these slow nodes during the warmup window.

Action items I’m executing now:
1) Pulling the failing instance ids and marking them for forensic logs (collector running now).
2) Temporarily increasing routing weight to warm nodes with already-cached KV (to reduce surface area).
3) Rolling a short-lived config: lower max-boot-concurrency for the pool to avoid simultaneous NVMe init storms.

I’ll post updated telemetry in 20 minutes.

—Marcus

Senior SRE, Redwood Inference

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Ella James <ella.james@fintechco.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 11:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: sudden p99 spikes during batch runway (first seen 08:50 PST)

Update after running targeted queries against traces and the init logs:
- Confirmed NVMe init delay on image img-v5.17-202611 — kernel messages show repeated controller resets that line up with the 2–4 minute boots.
- When NVMe init stalls, the model process stays in a partial-start state: grpc thread accepts connection but backend tokenization / kv warm path is delayed → inflated head times and occasional 503s when request queue fills.
- Workaround applied at 10:45 PST: set routing to favor already-initialized shards and increase retry-window for user jobs. This reduced p95 from 1.4s → 320ms and p99 from 3.6s → 780ms on sampled traffic. Error rate dropped from 2% → 0.4%. (See sla-impact-summary.pdf attached.)

Request: Marcus, can infra schedule a conservative image rollback or NVMe firmware pin for the FintechCo pool and a constrained boot-concurrency change (<=2) so we don't re-trigger a bootstorm during their next batch window?

Platform Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 17:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Post-mortem summary & next steps — standby->warm gap (INC-2026-4512)

Instances taking 2–4 minutes to finish init redirected traffic to partially-initialized model processes, inflating p95/p99 and causing a ~2% error rate in recon jobs.

Immediate mitigations performed today:
- Routing weight change to prefer warmed shards (applied 10:45 PST) — measurable latency improvement (p95 and p99 reduced).

- Forensic logs collected and trace bundle uploaded (fintechco_trace_12052026.zip).

- Marcus: schedule image rollback to v5.16 kernel and pin NVMe firmware for FintechCo pools (ETA: 48 hrs).

We’ll treat this as a priority post-mortem and add a specific warmup-runbook to the hosted API playbooks to prevent regression across other customers.

If you need anything else right now, call me directly.

source.subject: Proposal: Redwood estimate and priority check-in for Northbridge
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Carla Muñoz <carla@northbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal: Redwood estimate and next steps (Northbridge)

Hi Carla —

Thanks again for taking the time today. As promised, attached is the short proposal and pricing estimate we discussed for Northbridge’s inference workload. Quick summary: 

• Baseline: Hosted API for dev/test (short-term PoC)
• Production: Dedicated capacity (reserved nodes) — baseline throughput for 5M tokens/day
• Cost drivers: token volume, sequence length distribution, optional quantized model tier

Attachments: Redwood-Proposal-Northbridge-Estimate-20261130.pdf (PDF, 1.1MB)

Next steps I suggested on the call:
1) Confirm receipt of the deck and whether the dedicated option is required for your procurement path
2) If helpful, we can do a 15-minute walkthrough (I’ll run the pricing math and answer any questions)
3) If procurement needs an exec summary, I can prepare one tailored to your cloud/compliance notes

Proposed times for a quick walkthrough (PT): Tue 12/1 10:00–10:15, Wed 12/2 11:30–11:45, Thu 12/3 09:00–09:15. If none of these work, tell me a couple slots that do.

If you’d like me to loop in our solutions lead (Neha) to cover the dedicated capacity sizing or Marcus on commercial terms, happy to include them — I’ve cc’d both.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
<aisha@redwoodinference.com>
(415) 555-0187

--
Attachment: Redwood-Proposal-Northbridge-Estimate-20261130.pdf (PDF)
From: Carla Muñoz <carla@northbridgehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Redwood estimate and next steps (Northbridge)

Hi Aisha —

Thanks, I got the proposal and I’ll circulate with the infra and finance teams. A couple quick clarifying questions while we review:

• Does the estimate assume dedicated capacity by default for production or is that optional?
• Any lead times for reserved capacity commits (we have a Jan 15 target for procurement decisions)?

I’ll aim to come back early next week with availability for a quick walkthrough — appreciate the options.

Best,
Carla Munoz
Director, Platform Engineering
Northbridge Health
carla@northbridgehealth.com

> Hi Carla —
> 
> Thanks again for taking the time today. As promised, attached is the short proposal and pricing estimate we discussed for Northbridge’s inference workload. Quick summary: ...
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Carla Muñoz <carla@northbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Dec 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Checking in — proposal landed and 15m walkthrough offer

Hi Carla —

Just following up on the proposal I sent last week. Wanted to check whether this is still a priority for your team given your Jan 15 procurement window. If it helps move things along, I can:

• Do a focused 15-minute pricing walkthrough to highlight the committed vs burst cost tradeoffs
• Share a one-page exec summary for procurement (I’ve attached a draft)
• Schedule Neha for a 20-minute sizing call if the infra team wants to validate throughput assumptions

Here are a few slots (PT) that are open on my end: Mon 12/6 09:30–09:45, Tue 12/7 11:00–11:15, Wed 12/8 14:00–14:15. If you prefer, pick a time and I’ll send a calendar invite.

Thanks — I don’t want this to sit on your backlog if the timing changed. Quick reply on priority is totally fine.

Best,
Aisha

--
Attachment: One-pager-Dedicated-vs-Hosted.pdf (PDF)

On Tue, Dec 1, 2026 at 9:03 AM Carla Muñoz <carla@northbridgehealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Aisha —
> 
> Thanks, I got the proposal and I’ll circulate with the infra and finance teams. A couple quick clarifying questions while we review: ...
From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwoodinference.com>
To: Carla Muñoz <carla@northbridgehealth.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Dec 2026 09:43:00 -0800
Subject: Quick note on dedicated capacity (happy to join a short walkthrough)

Hi Carla —

Looping in briefly from the solutions side — I’m the SE that would join the sizing conversation if helpful. Two quick clarifications on the estimate you asked about: 

• The proposal includes a recommended baseline of dedicated nodes to guarantee latency and KV-cache persistence for your 24/7 inference patterns. That baseline is what we modeled as the reserved capacity line item in the PDF.
• Burst capacity is optional and billed separately; it’s there to handle rare spikes without over-provisioning baseline.
• Typical lead time for reserved capacity is ~3–4 weeks from purchase to full provisioning in our cloud regions; we can accelerate for enterprise-only customers with an implementation sprint.

If you’d like, I can join a 15–20 minute call with Aisha next week to run the sizing assumptions and answer any infra-level questions. Suggested times: Tue 12/7 11:00–11:20 PT or Wed 12/8 14:00–14:20 PT.

Thanks and looking forward to connecting,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
neha@redwoodinference.com

>

• Baseline: Hosted API for dev/test (short-term PoC)
• Production: Dedicated capacity (reserved nodes) — baseline throughput for 5M tokens/day

source.subject: Billing allocation and ASC 606 checklist for multi-year prepay
messages:
From: Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Britech Procurement <procure@britech.com>
Cc: Tom Harris <tharris@britech.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Hi Ben / team,

We just received the countersigned SOW for the 3-year prepay. Our auditors are asking for a few items before we book the commitment: a clear invoice schedule/wording they can match to our ledger, and a revenue recognition memo that explains how Redwood will allocate revenue across the three years under ASC 606.

Two specific asks from finance:
- Can Redwood issue invoices split by fiscal year (we prefer a separate invoice for each contract year), or will you issue one upfront invoice and provide an allocation schedule?
- We need sample invoice wording and GL line detail (service start date, term, applied discounts) so it aligns with our SAP mapping.

I've attached the signed SOW (britech-signed-sow.pdf). Can you confirm next steps and timeline — our month-end close is coming up.

Thanks,
Elena Campos
Director, Finance — Britech
Attachment: britech-signed-sow.pdf
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 12:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Elena — thanks for the note and congrats on getting the SOW signed. TL;DR: we can support both approaches and will provide the auditor materials you need. Details below and an attached allocation workbook.

Options we support:
1) Separate fiscal-year invoices: Redwood can generate three invoices that correspond to each contract year (Invoice YYYY-01, YYYY-02, YYYY-03). Customer pays per invoice when issued; this is simplest for your AR/GL mapping.
2) One upfront invoice with split allocation: Redwood invoices the full prepay now and supplies an allocation schedule and an ASC 606 memo for your auditors. We then recognize revenue over the contract period in our ledger — for you the payment is a prepayment.

What we included in the attached proposed-allocation.xlsx: a simple year-by-year breakdown (amounts, discount allocation, suggested invoice descriptions). We modeled two allocation methods: straight-line over term, and usage-weighted (if you expect ramp).

Next steps I suggest:
- Marcus (finance) to confirm preferred invoice flow and any required invoice fields.
- We will produce a short ASC 606 memo + recognition schedule for your audit team.
- If you want separate invoices, confirm invoice recipients and PO number(s).

I copied Marcus and Neha from our finance/ops team to answer the accounting specifics. If it's helpful I can also prepare draft invoice PDFs for review.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-allocation.xlsx
From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 08:50:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Hi Elena / Ben — Marcus from Redwood finance here. A few clarifying questions so we can produce auditor-friendly materials: 

1) Contract start date: please confirm the service start (we typically recognize from that date).
2) Are any delivery obligations front-loaded (e.g., professional services, onboarding credits) that need separate contract line items?
3) Is the 3-year discount applied up front or as an effective rate across each year? Our allocation workbook assumes the discount is applied to the total prepay and then prorated per the chosen allocation method.

Notes on invoice formatting: see attached redwood-ds-invoice-template.pdf for the fields we can include (PO#, start/end dates, line-level description, discount line). If you require different GL codes or vendor IDs in the invoice text, list them here and we'll add.

If you prefer separate invoices but one payment, we can (a) issue three invoices and accept one payment against Invoice 1 with references to the others, or (b) issue one upfront invoice and provide the year split. Both are auditor-friendly if documented.

I'll prepare a one-page ASC 606 memo describing revenue recognition approach once we have start date and confirmation on discount handling.

Attachment: redwood-ds-invoice-template.pdf
Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Accounting Manager, Redwood Inference
From: Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Britech Procurement <procure@britech.com>, Tom Harris <tharris@britech.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Thanks Marcus — answers below.

- Contract start date: 2027-01-01.
- No separate professional services in the SOW; onboarding is included in the annual service line.
- Discount: 12% term discount applied to the total prepay at signature (we'd like the economics to reflect a 12% off list across the 3-year commitment).

Usage expectations (for your usage-weighted allocation model): Year 1: ~30% of total consumption, Year 2: ~50%, Year 3: ~20%. We understand you may still spread recognition straight-line, but we want the option to match usage for our internal reporting if possible.

Regarding invoices: our preference is separate invoices per contract year (for our fiscal reporting) but we can accept a single payment if Redwood requires it operationally. If you issue three invoices, please make sure the invoice description includes: "3-yr prepay — year X of 3, service start 2027-01-01" and the PO number on each invoice.

Also request: a DocuSign-ready invoice/SOW package (we often need the invoice wording in the box on the SOW for our AR team). Can you produce the DocuSign link and sample invoice PDFs by Friday?

Best,
Elena
Director, Finance — Britech
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Dec 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Elena — great, thanks for the quick clarifications. Action items and commitments from Redwood below: 

1) Invoices: we'll generate three invoice PDFs (2027-Y1, 2028-Y2, 2029-Y3) with the exact description you requested and the PO block populated. We'll also provide an alternative single-upfront invoice + allocation pack if you prefer one payment.
2) Allocation spreadsheet: updated and attached (proposed-allocation.xlsx) using both straight-line and your usage-weighted split.
3) ASC 606 memo: Marcus will deliver a concise memo and recognition schedule (one page + table) that you can share with auditors.
4) DocuSign: I'll send a DocuSign bundle with the signed SOW + invoice cover page and a field for your signature. DocuSign preview link: https://www.docusign.com/placeholder-sign?envelopeId=ABC12345

Please confirm the billing contact and final PO number and we'll issue the first invoice (2027-Y1) by Monday with the other two invoices dated 2028-01-01 and 2029-01-01 respectively. If you'd like the invoices backdated to your fiscal year boundaries instead, let us know.

Thanks — looping Neha and Marissa to track the DocuSign and invoice delivery.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-allocation.xlsx

- If you want separate invoices, confirm invoice recipients and PO number(s).

I copied Marcus and Neha from our finance/ops team to answer the accounting specifics. If it's helpful I can also prepare draft invoice PDFs for review.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-allocation.xlsx


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 08:50:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Hi Elena / Ben — Marcus from Redwood finance here. A few clarifying questions so we can produce auditor-friendly materials: 

1) Contract start date: please confirm the service start (we typically recognize from that date).
2) Are any delivery obligations front-loaded (e.g., professional services, onboarding credits) that need separate contract line items?
3) Is the 3-year discount applied up front or as an effective rate across each year? Our allocation workbook assumes the discount is applied to the total prepay and then prorated per the chosen allocation method.

Notes on invoice formatting: see attached redwood-ds-invoice-template.pdf for the fields we can include (PO#, start/end dates, line-level description, discount line). If you require different GL codes or vendor IDs in the invoice text, list them here and we'll add.

If you prefer separate invoices but one payment, we can (a) issue three invoices and accept one payment against Invoice 1 with references to the others, or (b) issue one upfront invoice and provide the year split. Both are auditor-friendly if documented.

I'll prepare a one-page ASC 606 memo describing revenue recognition approach once we have start date and confirmation on discount handling.

Attachment: redwood-ds-invoice-template.pdf
Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Accounting Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Britech Procurement <procure@britech.com>, Tom Harris <tharris@britech.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Thanks Marcus — answers below.

- Contract start date: 2027-01-01.
- No separate professional services in the SOW; onboarding is included in the annual service line.
- Discount: 12% term discount applied to the total prepay at signature (we'd like the economics to reflect a 12% off list across the 3-year commitment).

Usage expectations (for your usage-weighted allocation model): Year 1: ~30% of total consumption, Year 2: ~50%, Year 3: ~20%. We understand you may still spread recognition straight-line, but we want the option to match usage for our internal reporting if possible.

Regarding invoices: our preference is separate invoices per contract year (for our fiscal reporting) but we can accept a single payment if Redwood requires it operationally. If you issue three invoices, please make sure the invoice description includes: "3-yr prepay — year X of 3, service start 2027-01-01" and the PO number on each invoice.

Also request: a DocuSign-ready invoice/SOW package (we often need the invoice wording in the box on the SOW for our AR team). Can you produce the DocuSign link and sample invoice PDFs by Friday?

Best,
Elena
Director, Finance — Britech


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Campos <elena@britech.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Dec 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Billing approach for 3-year prepay (ASC 606 questions)

Elena — great, thanks for the quick clarifications. Action items and commitments from Redwood below: 

1) Invoices: we'll generate three invoice PDFs (2027-Y1, 2028-Y2, 2029-Y3) with the exact description you requested and the PO block populated. We'll also provide an alternative single-upfront invoice + allocation pack if you prefer one payment.
2) Allocation spreadsheet: updated and attached (proposed-allocation.xlsx) using both straight-line and your usage-weighted split.
3) ASC 606 memo: Marcus will deliver a concise memo and recognition schedule (one page + table) that you can share with auditors.
4) DocuSign: I'll send a DocuSign bundle with the signed SOW + invoice cover page and a field for your signature. DocuSign preview link: https://www.docusign.com/placeholder-sign?envelopeId=ABC12345

Please confirm the billing contact and final PO number and we'll issue the first invoice (2027-Y1) by Monday with the other two invoices dated 2028-01-01 and 2029-01-01 respectively. If you'd like the invoices backdated to your fiscal year boundaries instead, let us know.

Thanks — looping Neha and Marissa to track the DocuSign and invoice delivery.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed-allocation.xlsx

messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@halcyonhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap + MAP draft (vendor onboarding milestones)

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call today. Quick recap and the first-pass MAP attached. I tried to capture milestones we discussed and who we expect to own each step so procurement has clear inputs. Please confirm owners and target dates so we can put this into your Ariba/Coupa intake.

Key milestones (draft):
- Procurement vendor setup (Ariba/Coupa) — Owner: Halcyon procurement (please provide intake contact) — Target: 12/10/2026
- Submit W-9 & tax information — Owner: Halcyon vendor — Target: 12/07/2026
- Banking/ACH details (ACH form) — Owner: Halcyon vendor + Halcyon finance — Target: 12/10/2026
- PO issuance & payment terms agreement — Owner: Halcyon procurement / Redwood finance — Target: 12/15/2026
- SOW / signature (DocuSign) — Owner: Redwood legal + Halcyon business owner — Target: 12/18/2026

Dependencies: Ariba vendor ID required before PO; banking docs need direct contact at Halcyon finance for ACH.

Next steps I propose:
1) Elena to confirm procurement intake contact and vendor-assignment (who will complete Ariba).
2) Halcyon to share W-9 + ACH form by 12/07.
3) Redwood to prepare final MAP and DocuSign envelope once vendor ID is created.

I've attached MAP_Halcyon_v1.xlsx (simple milestone tracker) and a short vendor onboarding checklist for your procurement team to review.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 15-min sync if that helps align owners.

— Avery
Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.ai
Attachments: MAP_Halcyon_v1.xlsx (xlsx, 34 KB); VendorSetupChecklist.pdf (pdf, 48 KB)

From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@halcyonhealth.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, procurement@halcyonhealth.com
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 10:14:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + MAP draft (vendor onboarding milestones)

Hi Avery — thanks for sending this. Halcyon procurement will own the Ariba/Coupa submission. I've assigned Kyle Reynolds (copied) to be the intake lead — he will complete the Ariba vendor record and confirm vendor ID back to you.

A couple notes from procurement:
- Preferred intake: vendor completes our online form + W-9 upload. Kyle will email the intake link to the vendor contact.
- Banking: Halcyon requires an ACH authorization form plus a copy of a voided check for domestic payments. We do accept SWIFT/CIBC for international but there are extra steps.
- Estimated timeline: Ariba vendor creation usually takes 3-5 business days once we have the W-9 and bank info.

I attached our standard VendorSetupChecklist to this reply — it lists the exact files we need.

I'll aim to have Kyle reach out to your vendor (or you can provide a vendor contact) by EOD tomorrow. For timing, moving the MAP close from 12/18 to 12/20 is OK if procurement needs the buffer.

Thanks — Elena
Elena Morales
Director, Procurement — Halcyon Health
elena.morales@halcyonhealth.com
Attachments: VendorSetupChecklist.pdf (pdf, 22 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@halcyonhealth.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + MAP draft (vendor onboarding milestones)

Elena / team — looping in Redwood finance/legal notes so we have clear asks for vendor paperwork. Two small requests from our side:

1) Please confirm the exact W-9 fields you require (we typically provide standard W-9). If you need additional vendor tax paperwork (foreign entity forms), call it out and we'll coordinate.
2) For ACH we need the vendor's bank name, account number, routing number, and a voided check or bank letter. If Halcyon requires a vendor-signed ACH authorization with additional attestations, please share the template so the vendor can complete it correctly.

Payment terms: Redwood is comfortable with net-30 standard on initial pilots. If Halcyon requires different terms for onboarding, flag that and we can align.

Marcus — can you confirm if Redwood needs to pre-register any vendor legal information in our internal system before we accept PO?

— Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Counsel, Contracts — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@halcyonhealth.com>, Kyle Reynolds <kyle.reynolds@halcyonhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 03 Dec 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + MAP draft (vendor onboarding milestones)

Thanks all — quick update and an edited MAP attached. I updated the milestone owners per Elena and added the procurement intake contact. Proposed revised target close: 12/22/2026 to leave a 2-business-day buffer for PO processing.

Updated milestones (v1.1):
- 12/07: Halcyon procurement to send vendor intake link to vendor (Owner: Kyle Reynolds)
- 12/07: Vendor to upload W-9 + ACH docs (Owner: Vendor)
- 12/10: Halcyon to create Ariba vendor ID (Owner: Halcyon procurement)
- 12/15: Halcyon to issue PO (Owner: Halcyon procurement)
- 12/18: DocuSign SOW executed (Owner: Redwood + Halcyon business owner)
- 12/22: Production access & billing enabled (Owner: Redwood ops + Halcyon finance)

Action items for this week:
- Elena/Kyle: confirm the intake link and expected point of contact for the vendor by 12/01 EOD (noted — Kyle to reach out).
- Vendor: upload W-9 and ACH docs by 12/07.
- Redwood: prepare DocuSign envelope and final SOW once PO number provided.

Related links: HubSpot deal RDW-1392 (link below) and a draft DocuSign envelope. I'll attach MAP_Halcyon_v1.1 and the ACH instructions we've prepared to speed vendor completion.

If we need a brief 15-minute call between procurement + vendor + Redwood finance to clear the ACH template, I can schedule for Friday.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — I'll mark the MAP as 'awaiting vendor docs' in the tracker once Kyle confirms outreach.

Best,
Avery

Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Data residency & retention: hosted API — Q&A
messages:
From: Gina Park <gina@acmetech.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Hosted API — residency & retention clarifications

Hi Aisha / Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week. Our security team is working through the vendor questionnaire and had a couple of targeted asks about Redwood's hosted API:

1) If we use the hosted API, can we ensure all customer data (requests & generated content) remains within US regions only?
2) What is the default retention window for request/response logs and model telemetry? Where can we find a formal purge process and SLA?
3) For compliance, they also want to know whether backups/snapshots might replicate data outside the requested region and how long those are retained.

Can you point me to a short, plain-language summary and any attachments we can pass to InfoSec? We're on a tight timeline for our vendor review.

Appreciate it —
Gina
Security & Privacy, Acme Tech

---
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Gina Park <gina@acmetech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API — residency & retention clarifications

Hi Gina —

Thanks — great questions. Short answers up front, followed by a few operational details and attachments. I've CC'd Marcus (security) and Naomi (product ops) for technical backup.

1) Regional residency: Yes — when provisioning a hosted API key/account you can specify a US-only serving region (us-west-2 or us-east-1). All runtime request processing, ephemeral KV/prefix caches, and persistent logs for that tenant are stored in the selected region. We do not route request payloads to other regions for processing unless explicitly configured in routing policies.

2) Retention windows: The default retention for request/response logs and model telemetry is 30 days. Customers may request shorter windows (e.g., 7 days) as part of their onboarding. We also offer a paid retention-extension option (90/365 days) for audit needs. See attached retention-sla-summary.pdf for the table and options.

3) Backups & replication: Regular backups are taken for control-plane metadata and deployment state. Customer request/response content is excluded from cross-region backups by default for region-locked tenants. For Dedicated or Private deployments we provide stronger guarantees; hosted customers can opt into dedicated snapshots (US-only) if needed.

I've attached: data-residency-faq.pdf (plain-language summary), retention-sla-summary.pdf (SLA table + purge timelines), and a redwood-soc2-evidence.zip stub containing pointers to our evidence artifacts.

Action items:

title: Data residency & retention: hosted API — Q&A
preview:
From: Gina Park <gina@acmetech.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Hosted API — residency & retention clarifications

Hi Aisha / Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week. Our security team is working through the vendor questionnaire and had a couple of targeted asks about Redwood's hosted API:

1) If we use the hosted API, can we ensure all customer data (requests & generated content) remains within US regions only?
2) What is the default retention window for request/response logs and model telemetry? Where can we find a formal purge process and SLA?
3) For compliance, they also want to know whether backups/snapshots might replicate data outside the requested region and how long those are retained.

Can you point me to a short, plain-language summary and any attachments we can pass to InfoSec? We're on a tight timeline for our vendor review.

Appreciate it —
Gina
Security & Privacy, Acme Tech

---


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Gina Park <gina@acmetech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API — residency & retention clarifications

Hi Gina —

Thanks — great questions. Short answers up front, followed by a few operational details and attachments. I've CC'd Marcus (security) and Naomi (product ops) for technical backup.

1) Regional residency: Yes — when provisioning a hosted API key/account you can specify a US-only serving region (us-west-2 or us-east-1). All runtime request processing, ephemeral KV/prefix caches, and persistent logs for that tenant are stored in the selected region. We do not route request payloads to other regions for processing unless explicitly configured in routing policies.

2) Retention windows: The default retention for request/response logs and model telemetry is 30 days. Customers may request shorter windows (e.g., 7 days) as part of their onboarding. We also offer a paid retention-extension option (90/365 days) for audit needs. See attached retention-sla-summary.pdf for the table and options.

3) Backups & replication: Regular backups are taken for control-plane metadata and deployment state. Customer request/response content is excluded from cross-region backups by default for region-locked tenants. For Dedicated or Private deployments we provide stronger guarantees; hosted customers can opt into dedicated snapshots (US-only) if needed.

I've attached: data-residency-faq.pdf (plain-language summary), retention-sla-summary.pdf (SLA table + purge timelines), and a redwood-soc2-evidence.zip stub containing pointers to our evidence artifacts.

Action items:
- If you want an auditable statement we can provide a one-page attestation that we will keep Acme data in US regions for your tenant — Ben, can you pick this up and route a doc (simple letter) to Gina?
- Marcus/Naomi: can you confirm the backup snapshot cadence and retention for hosted US-only tenants so I can call it out explicitly?

If helpful we can also schedule a 30-min call with InfoSec next week.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: data-residency-faq.pdf (PDF), retention-sla-summary.pdf (PDF), redwood-soc2-evidence.zip (ZIP)

messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Turner <sophie.turner@brightwaveai.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Coordinating 30-min product briefing — stakeholder time pairs

Hi Sophie —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and next steps so we keep momentum:

- Recap: You’re exploring an inference platform to reduce per-token cost and guarantee sub-200ms median latency for your customer-facing chat assistant. Primary constraints: multi-tenant security model, on-prem/private deployment for regulated datasets, and predictable burst capacity.
- Ask: For the 30-minute intro/demo we’ll tailor the flow to the stakeholders you bring. To be most useful, please confirm whether you want a broad product tour or a focused look at the private deployment / security controls.

Proposed stakeholder time pairs (two options to make calendar alignment easier):

Option A — Product + Platform
- Tue Dec 6, 2026 — 09:00–09:30 PT (Avery + Ben)

Option B — Product + Security
- Wed Dec 7, 2026 — 11:30–12:00 PT (Avery + Ben + Marcus for a short security dig-in)

Fallback window: Dec 8–10, late morning PT preferred.

Attachments: Briefing-Agenda.pdf (short agenda), Pre-demo-questionnaire.docx (3 quick qual Qs we use to tailor the demo).

Quick qual for the demo prep — if you can reply with the bullets below, we’ll adjust the emphasis:
- Expected concurrent users / reqs per second (approx.)
- Data residency / retention concerns (PII, logs)
- Any blocked IP ranges or SSO/SAML notes we should know in advance

If you confirm stakeholders + pick A or B I’ll send an invite and prep doc.

Thanks —
Avery Johnson
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0129

Redwood Inference — Make inference predictable and economical
From: Sophie Turner <sophie.turner@brightwaveai.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Huang <mark.huang@brightwaveai.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Coordinating 30-min product briefing — stakeholder time pairs

On Nov 29, 2026, at 2:05 PM, Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and next steps so we keep momentum:
> ...

Thanks, Avery — this looks good. We’re most interested in a focused demo on private deployment + security controls (that’s the highest lift for us right now).

We can do Option B: Wed Dec 7, 11:30–12:00 PT. I’ve looped in Mark Huang (Head of Security) to the thread — he’ll join for the security portion. A few quick answers to your prep Qs:
- Expected throughput: target ~200 req/s during steady state, bursts to ~600 for short periods.
- Data residency: must retain audit logs for 12 months; PII must be redacted or tokenized at ingress.
- SSO: We use Okta; we’ll share SAML metadata ahead of time if we move to a Private deployment.

Please include any pre-demo checklist we should fill out — happy to get the questionnaire back to you today.

Best,
Sophie Turner
Head of ML Ops | BrightWave Analytics
Sophie.Turner@brightwaveai.com
From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>

Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>

Perfect — thanks Sophie. Option B is confirmed on our side for Wed Dec 7, 11:30–12:00 PT. I’ll send a calendar invite shortly (Meeting-invite.ics attached).

Ben (cc) will lead the product walkthrough and Marcus will touch on the security/Private deployment checklist for the first ~10 minutes.

Prep + attachments we’re dropping in the invite: Briefing-Agenda.pdf, Pre-demo-questionnaire.docx, Solutions-Prep-Sheet.xlsx. Please send the questionnaire back when you can — it helps us pre-load any demo data and prioritize the call.

A couple of action items for BrightWave before the call:
- Share Okta SAML metadata (if you have a staging tenant we can test SSO against, great).
- If you want us to run a quick throughput check, share a small anonymized test payload or example token sizes.

Drive link with pre-demo materials: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-redwood-brightwave-prep
HubSpot lead: HS-Lead-65321
Calendly (invite used to pick the slot): https://calendly.com/redwood/30min-product-briefing

I’ll follow up with the invite and meeting notes (short) after it’s on the calendar.

See you Nov 7 —
Avery

Cc: Mark Huang <mark.huang@brightwaveai.com>

Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Coordinating 30-min product briefing — stakeholder time pairs

Hi Sophie —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and next steps so we keep momentum:

- Recap: You’re exploring an inference platform to reduce per-token cost and guarantee sub-200ms median latency for your customer-facing chat assistant. Primary constraints: multi-tenant security model, on-prem/private deployment for regulated datasets, and predictable burst capacity.
- Ask: For the 30-minute intro/demo we’ll tailor the flow to the stakeholders you bring. To be most useful, please confirm whether you want a broad product tour or a focused look at the private deployment / security controls.

Proposed stakeholder time pairs (two options to make calendar alignment easier):

Option A — Product + Platform
- Tue Dec 6, 2026 — 09:00–09:30 PT (Avery + Ben)

Option B — Product + Security
- Wed Dec 7, 2026 — 11:30–12:00 PT (Avery + Ben + Marcus for a short security dig-in)

Fallback window: Dec 8–10, late morning PT preferred.

Attachments: Briefing-Agenda.pdf (short agenda), Pre-demo-questionnaire.docx (3 quick qual Qs we use to tailor the demo).

Quick qual for the demo prep — if you can reply with the bullets below, we’ll adjust the emphasis:
- Expected concurrent users / reqs per second (approx.)
- Data residency / retention concerns (PII, logs)
- Any blocked IP ranges or SSO/SAML notes we should know in advance

If you confirm stakeholders + pick A or B I’ll send an invite and prep doc.

Thanks —
Avery Johnson
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0129

Redwood Inference — Make inference predictable and economical

source.subject: Q4 performance narrative + scoring log for leadership review
messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Cc: board-ops@boardco.com, emma@luminate.ai
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Q4 performance narrative + scoring log for leadership review

Team —

Attached is my draft of the Q4 performance narrative (Q4_performance_narrative_v2.docx) plus the raw scoring sheet (Q4_scoring_sheet.csv) and the current slide deck for the QBR (QBR_slide_deck_v7.pdf). Key asks:

- Please review the narrative for tone and any factual corrections. I tried to keep the narrative tight (1.5 pages) and surfaced the three biggest cross-org levers: Dedicated revenue cadence, latency reliability program, and Redwood Optimize pilot.
- Update the scoring sheet with any adjustments to owner-level objective scores. I recommended a conservative approach in the Right column; if you want to be more aggressive mark in the Proposed column and add a one-line rationale.
- Call out any escalations or open evidence items for security/compliance we should surface in the QBR. I flagged two items in the doc (logs retention clarification and Dedicated SLA language).

Timing: I need final scores and any suggested sentence edits by EOD Monday 11/29 so I can fold into the slides and send to execs Tuesday morning.

Notable constraints: the Optimize pilot results are still incomplete — I included a single-slide summary with a recommendation to continue the pilot in Jan with an extended evaluation. If we want to accelerate a larger ask (headcount/credits) we should call it out explicitly.

Thanks — even short comments inline are super helpful.

— Avery
Avery Johnson
Director, Product Operations
Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0172

Attachments:
- Q4_performance_narrative_v2.docx (document)
- Q4_scoring_sheet.csv (spreadsheet)
- QBR_slide_deck_v7.pdf (slides)

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 11:17:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Q4 performance narrative + scoring log for leadership review

Avery — good draft, thanks for pulling this together. A couple of inline notes and a scoring suggestion below.

Inline:
- Paragraph 2 (market momentum): can we soften "outpaced" to "outpaced in selected benchmarks" — we'd want to avoid implying broad market leadership.
- Security callout: add a one-liner that SOC2 evidence will be provided in the appendix; Laura can confirm retention window.

Scoring: I updated the sheet with two changes:
- Dedicated capacity (OBJ-2): moved from 60 -> 70 (Proposed) because the new committed deals lined up in mid-November improved utilization — rationale in the notes column.
- Latency SLO (OBJ-1): kept at 85 since we still have the tail latency incidents that need the retry mechanism.

Big question: do we want to include the Optimize pilot as "progress" or mark it as "early stage risk"? I marked it as "early stage risk" but added a line recommending a pilot extension + prioritized instrumentation.

I'll attach a one-slide suggested phrasing for the narrative if you want to swap it in.

— Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
VP, Engineering
vivik_kulkarni@redwood.com

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 14:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Q4 performance narrative + scoring log for leadership review

Thanks Avery — this reads well. I confirmed with Security that the retention clarification can be handled; they asked to include the exact retention window (30 days) in the appendix.

Also: FYI to Marcus, we should call out the deferred hiring hit on throughput in the narrative (one sentence) — it explains some of the missed throughput targets and aligns the ask for Q1 headcount.

Short thumbs-up from me if you update the appendix line.

Laura
Laura Bennett
Head of Product Strategy
laura_bennett@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Q4 performance narrative + scoring log for leadership review

Adding my notes and suggested numeric tweaks. I updated the CSV with two cells:
- OBJ-3 (Retention & Cost): 55 -> 62 (Proposed). We made progress on cache improvements and the quantization experiments look promising, but cost per token still needs more ops-level automation. Rationale in the notes.
- OBJ-4 (Platform reliability): 72 -> 70 (kept conservative because incident count in Nov still shows variability).

I also flagged a small charting issue in the slide deck (slide 8, Y axis label wrong) — fixed version attached.

Marcus
Marcus Lin
Sr. Director, Infra & Ops
marcus_lin@redwood.com

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 15:23:00 -0800
Subject: Finalized narrative + consolidated scores (for send to execs)

Thanks all — incorporated Vivek and Marcus notes, added the retention window to the appendix per Laura, and fixed the slide label. I attached the final narrative and an updated scoring sheet (Q4_performance_narrative_v2.docx -> renamed in slides folder as Q4_performance_narrative_v3.docx; scoring sheet updated).

Plan: I will circulate to the exec list at 09:00 PT on Tue 12/01. If there are any late objections, reply-all with a clear proposed text change.

Outstanding items: security evidence file (Security to upload by Monday), and a one-line ask for a Q1 hire (headcount request) — I put the hiring ask as an appendix bullet so execs can greenlight or ask for details.

If no objections, I'll send the deck to board-ops and CC Emma at Luminate for the customer highlight slide.

Appended forwarded thread for context:

On Sun, 29 Nov 2026 at 10:08 AM Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com> wrote:
> Adding my notes and suggested numeric tweaks. I updated the CSV with two cells:
> - OBJ-3 (Retention & Cost): 55 -> 62 (Proposed). We made progress on cache improvements and the quantization experiments look promising, but cost per token still needs more ops-level automation. Rationale in the notes.
> - OBJ-4 (Platform reliability): 72 -> 70 (kept conservative because incident count in Nov still shows variability).
>
> I also flagged a small charting issue in the slide deck (slide 8, Y axis label wrong) — fixed version attached.
>
> Marcus

— Avery
Avery Johnson
Director, Product Operations
avery_johnson@redwood.com

Attachments (final):
- Q4_performance_narrative_v3.docx (document)
- Q4_scoring_sheet.csv (spreadsheet, updated)
- QBR_slide_deck_v7_fixed.pdf (slides)

Outstanding items: security evidence file (Security to upload by Monday), and a one-line ask for a Q1 hire (headcount request) — I put the hiring ask as an appendix bullet so execs can greenlight or ask for details.

If no objections, I'll send the deck to board-ops and CC Emma at Luminate for the customer highlight slide.

Appended forwarded thread for context:

On Sun, 29 Nov 2026 at 10:08 AM Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com> wrote:
> Adding my notes and suggested numeric tweaks. I updated the CSV with two cells:
> - OBJ-3 (Retention & Cost): 55 -> 62 (Proposed). We made progress on cache improvements and the quantization experiments look promising, but cost per token still needs more ops-level automation. Rationale in the notes.
> - OBJ-4 (Platform reliability): 72 -> 70 (kept conservative because incident count in Nov still shows variability).
>
> I also flagged a small charting issue in the slide deck (slide 8, Y axis label wrong) — fixed version attached.
>
> Marcus

— Avery
Avery Johnson
Director, Product Operations
avery_johnson@redwood.com

- Q4_performance_narrative_v3.docx (document)
- Q4_scoring_sheet.csv (spreadsheet, updated)

source.subject: Activation gating for AWS Private Offers: buyer banding & subscription tiers
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-11-25T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Team —

Following the call this morning with AcmeCo and Carlos from AWS, I sketched a lightweight banding approach we can use across private offers to accelerate buyer approvals and co-sell activation. Attached: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx and a short activation playbook draft.

Key proposal (high-level):
- Band A: <$100k ARR equivalent, auto-approval via Seller Ops + one finance item (invoice cadence).
- Band B: $100k–$500k, requires seller manager + finance quick review (24–48h) and AWS partner signoff for co-sell.
- Band C: >$500k, full procurement evidence pack + legal/finance signoff and DocuSign.

Questions I want us to decide on in the next 48 hours:
1) Exact thresholds (USD / ARR equivalent) — are the breakpoints above reasonable?
2) Minimal evidence per band (what must live in the HubSpot deal to trigger activation).
3) Owner for automated gating (PR/Partner Ops vs Seller Ops) and whether we route to finance first for Band B.

Action items: please review the attached matrix and the playbook. Vanessa, can you confirm AWS co-sell expectations? Marcus, can you own the gating automation checklist? Priya, can you draft the evidence template for finance?

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), aws_activation_playbook_draft.pdf (pdf)

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben — great framing. A few notes from AWS partner side after syncing with Carlos: 
- AWS wants a clear activation SLA for co-sell lead assignment — recommend 72h for Band A/B to assign partner rep and 5 business days for Band C.
- For Band B, a one-pager (commercial summary + contact list) + confirmation of buyer identity is usually sufficient for initial co-sell allocation.
- Band C will need the procurement packet (SCA, billing contact, PO window) before we push co-sell resources.

I'll loop Carlos into the next doc review. I like the automated gating idea; we should be careful not to increase friction for high-velocity SMB deals.

Vanessa Ortiz
Global Marketplace Lead | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T13:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Thanks — reviewed the matrix. Technical notes for gating automation:
- HubSpot deal properties we can use as triggers: "Estimated ARR", "PO Expected Value", "Primary Billing Contact", "Procurement Contact Email".
- For Band A, implement an automated workflow that sets "PO Activation Ready" when Estimated ARR < 100000 and Billing Contact verified.
- For Band B, add a two-step: auto-notify finance with one-click "Approve" in HubSpot that sets a 48h timer. If finance doesn't approve, escalate to Seller Ops.

I'll draft the checklist in Confluence and link it in the playbook — will aim to have the automation spec by Wednesday (2026-11-28).

Marcus Lin
Head of Seller Ops | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

From: Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@acmeco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T09:07:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben / Vanessa — thanks for the clear summary. A couple of quick points from AcmeCo procurement: 
- We prefer Band B behavior: we can move to a 12-month usage commitment with quarterly invoicing; finance will need a short evidence pack (signed SOW or summary email + procurement contact).
- For Band C (if we expand later) procurement will request our legal team to review SLA and data retention.

Happy to review the one-pager once you have it.

—Lisa
Lisa Tran
Head of Procurement | AcmeCo
lisa.tran@acmeco.com

-----Original Message-----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Sent: Wednesday, November 25, 2026 9:12 AM
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>; Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Subject: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

(Quoted above)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Thanks, all. Draft evidence template for finance attached (I pasted it into the activation playbook and updated the spreadsheet):
- Required for Band A: billing contact + signed order email.
- Required for Band B: one-pager, procurement contact, billing cadence, summary SOW or summary email, CFO name.
- Required for Band C: standard evidence pack (SCA, legal review summary, PO term sheet).

I'll put the template in HubSpot as a required checklist item so workflow can verify presence of fields before flagging "PO Activation Ready".

Priya Natarajan
Finance Ops Manager | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@acmeco.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@aws-partner.amazon.com>
Date: 2026-11-27T15:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Quick status update and next steps: 
- Marcus will publish the gating automation spec by 2026-11-28.
- Priya has added the finance checklist to the playbook (see attached pdf).
- Vanessa / Carlos: please confirm the 72h SLA for Band A/B assignment; if OK we will add it to the HubSpot SLA automation and communicate to AWS.
- Lisa: when you have a minute, would you confirm that a one-pager + confirmation email will be sufficient for your procurement team for Band B?

If we align on these items, I'll push the HubSpot workflow and create the internal Seller Ops runbook for onboarding private offers.

Ben

source.subject: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop
messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>, Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Hi Kara and Tom,

Quick kickoff for the Acme exec day on Dec 7 — two priority items I want to lock before vendor confirmations:

1) Guest flow and mic coverage: Acme wants unobtrusive table mics in the boardroom and a plex mic grid in the foyer for registration announcements. Attached is the latest floorplan (acme_floorplan_v3.pdf) and a proposed mic_grid_v2.png. Can you confirm:
- recommended number of lavs vs table mics for the 28-person boardroom
- power/snake runs we should reserve on load-in

2) Bridge fallback and SLA: Acme requires a redundant conferencing bridge with a warm failover (15s switchover) and call-recording retained for 30 days. Tom — can you share the SOW/quote for the fallback bridge and the exact latency we should expect?

Timing: we need vendor confirmation by EOD Wednesday (Nov 24) so procurement can start.

Attachments: acme_floorplan_v3.pdf, mic_grid_v2.png

Thanks,

Alex Martinez
Event Ops Lead, Customer Events
Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0143

From: Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Alex — thanks for the clear brief. High level answers below; will follow up with a short SOW PDF and quote by Monday.

- Boardroom mic guidance: For a 28-person boardroom with a single presenter setup + Q&A, we recommend:
  * 6 cardioid boundary table mics (distributed every 4–5 seats) for best pickup and minimal cabling
  * 2 lavs for presenter + backup
  * One ambient pair for room audio feed into the bridge
  Power: we need two clean AC drops at the head table and one distro on the east wall — snake runs noted on your floorplan are sufficient.

- Foyer/registration: a compact plex mic grid (4 mics) will cover announcements and will route to a front-of-house mixer. We'll provide a small DSP for gating.

- Bridge fallback: We can deliver a geo-redundant bridge with warm failover (target 10–12s for SIP/TLS sessions). Recording retention 30 days available as an add-on; will include a redundant egress path and monitoring.

Attachment: preliminary_quote_bridge_fallback.pdf (I'll send detailed SOW Monday).

Tom Rivera
Solutions Architect
AVBridge Co.
Office: +1 (206) 555-2233
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>, Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Thanks Tom — that lineup looks good. A couple of constraints from our side to keep in the SOW:

- Network: the boardroom VLAN is isolated (10.42.11.0/24). We need a list of IPs/services your bridge endpoints will call out to so our NetOps can pre-approve firewall rules.
- Latency and monitoring: Acme wants RTT < 120ms for trans-pacific calls; please include synthetic monitoring and alerting in the quote (pager escalation to our on-call if failover occurs).
- Recording storage: ensure recordings are encrypted at rest and accessible via a secure link for 30 days (audit logs required).

Also, Lauren — can you confirm room staging window (we requested 07:00–09:30 for load-in) and whether we have concierge to direct carriers to the loading dock?

Marcus Lin
Head of Site Reliability, Redwood Inference

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>, Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Jason Chen <jason@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Forwarding the notes and a slightly updated floorplan — small pod added near the east foyer for guest check-in (see acme_floorplan_v3.pdf).

Jason — looping you in to confirm guest arrival flow and whether two concierge staff are available to manage registration between 07:30–09:30. If Acme can provide badge lists via the shared Drive link, we'll pre-print badges and reserve the east doorway for a covered canopy (weather contingency).

Subject: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Hi Kara and Tom,

Quick kickoff for the Acme exec day on Dec 7 — two priority items I want to lock before vendor confirmations:

1) Guest flow and mic coverage: Acme wants unobtrusive table mics in the boardroom and a plex mic grid in the foyer for registration announcements. Attached is the latest floorplan (acme_floorplan_v3.pdf) and a proposed mic_grid_v2.png. Can you confirm:
- recommended number of lavs vs table mics for the 28-person boardroom
- power/snake runs we should reserve on load-in

2) Bridge fallback and SLA: Acme requires a redundant conferencing bridge with a warm failover (15s switchover) and call-recording retained for 30 days. Tom — can you share the SOW/quote for the fallback bridge and the exact latency we should expect?

Timing: we need vendor confirmation by EOD Wednesday (Nov 24) so procurement can start.

Attachments: acme_floorplan_v3.pdf, mic_grid_v2.png

Thanks,

Alex Martinez
Event Ops Lead, Customer Events
Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0143



From: Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@avbridge.co>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Kara Simmons <kara@starlightvenues.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Alex — thanks for the clear brief. High level answers below; will follow up with a short SOW PDF and quote by Monday.

- Boardroom mic guidance: For a 28-person boardroom with a single presenter setup + Q&A, we recommend:
  * 6 cardioid boundary table mics (distributed every 4–5 seats) for best pickup and minimal cabling
  * 2 lavs for presenter + backup
  * One ambient pair for room audio feed into the bridge
  Power: we need two clean AC drops at the head table and one distro on the east wall — snake runs noted on your floorplan are sufficient.

- Foyer/registration: a compact plex mic grid (4 mics) will cover announcements and will route to a front-of-house mixer. We'll provide a small DSP for gating.

- Bridge fallback: We can deliver a geo-redundant bridge with warm failover (target 10–12s for SIP/TLS sessions). Recording retention 30 days available as an add-on; will include a redundant egress path and monitoring.

Attachment: preliminary_quote_bridge_fallback.pdf (I'll send detailed SOW Monday).

Tom Rivera
Solutions Architect
AVBridge Co.
Office: +1 (206) 555-2233


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>

Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Guest flow, mic grid & bridge fallback for Acme exec workshop

Thanks Tom — that lineup looks good. A couple of constraints from our side to keep in the SOW:

- Network: the boardroom VLAN is isolated (10.42.11.0/24). We need a list of IPs/services your bridge endpoints will call out to so our NetOps can pre-approve firewall rules.
- Latency and monitoring: Acme wants RTT < 120ms for trans-pacific calls; please include synthetic monitoring and alerting in the quote (pager escalation to our on-call if failover occurs).
- Recording storage: ensure recordings are encrypted at rest and accessible via a secure link for 30 days (audit logs required).

Also, Lauren — can you confirm room staging window (we requested 07:00–09:30 for load-in) and whether we have concierge to direct carriers to the loading dock?

Marcus Lin
