source.subject: Request: trial extension and promo credits — Bridgeway SSO blocker
messages:
From: Jordan Pratt <jordan.pratt@bridgeway.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Request: trial extension and promo credits — SSO blocker

Ben —

Thanks for the product walkthrough last week. We started wiring up Redwood in our staging environment this morning and hit an SSO integration blocker: the partner SDK we rely on is returning a token exchange error during large-batch authentication (see attached error_log.txt). Our eng lead says it will take at least 2–3 sprints to get a proper fix into our release branch because the partner is prioritizing a different customer.

We’re at a hard deadline for a pilot demo to the executive committee on Dec 15 — if we can’t extend the sandbox window and get a small promo credit pool to cover continued testing, we’ll likely have to push the demo. That would push our go/no-go and (unfortunately) delay our purchase decision.

Request: 30-day trial extension + a temporary promo credit of 10k tokens for staging replay tests. Happy to provide more logs or jump on a call.

Appreciate any help —

Jordan Pratt
Senior Platform Engineer, Bridgeway AI
jordan.pratt@bridgeway.ai
Attachments: error_log.txt (text)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Pratt <jordan.pratt@bridgeway.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T10:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: trial extension and promo credits — SSO blocker

Jordan —

Thanks for the quick note and for attaching the log. Sorry you’re blocked — that’s exactly the kind of integration friction we want to avoid. I’m copying Laura (CS) so we can coordinate the extension and Marcus from eng for a quick technical triage.

Operationally, I can request a 30-day sandbox extension and propose a one-time 10k token promo to keep your team running through the demo window. I’ll escalate for approval now but flag that if engineering indicates this is a long-running effort we may want to pair that with a targeted sandbox environment (short-term) rather than additional production tokens.

Can you share the staging project ID and a link to the partner SDK issue tracker? If you have a short sequence to reproduce the token error that would speed our triage.

Best,

Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-11-20T11:30:00-08:00
Subject: Approval request: Bridgeway trial extension & credits

Laura / Marcus —

Bridgeway (DW-1387) hit an SSO adapter issue in staging this morning and asked for a 30-day trial extension + 10k tokens to keep their pilot demo on track (external note pasted below). They’re committed to the Dec 15 exec demo and closing this quarter — losing momentum here risks pushing the deal.

Summary:
- Customer: Bridgeway AI (platforming ML workloads for enterprise search)
- Problem: Partner SDK token exchange error in batched auth (error_log.txt attached by customer)
- Asks: 30-day extension; 10k token promo for staging tests
- Risk: If we don’t provide runway, they’ll postpone demonstration & delay decisioning

Options I see:
1) Approve 30-day extension + 10k promo (one-time exception) — fastest path to keep momentum
2) Offer 14-day extension + 5k promo + temporary sandbox (isolated env we can spin up) — lower spend, but might be insufficient for their testing cadence

Marcus — any quick read on how long this will take from an eng perspective and whether a temporary sandbox (limited endpoints + quota) is feasible this week?

If approved, I’ll log the promo in HubSpot and raise an internal promo approval (legal/compliance link below).

Thanks —
Ben
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T14:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Approval request: Bridgeway trial extension & credits

Ben / Laura —

Quick tech read: the stack trace points to an edge case in their partner SDK where the token refresh loop fails under concurrent exchange load. Fix is non-trivial on their side; for us the short-term mitigation is either a) narrower replay tests (single-threaded) or b) provision a small, sandboxed environment with an adjusted rate limit to avoid concurrent failure.

Effort & risk:
- Engineering time (our side): low (sandbox provisioning and quota config ~ 3–5 hours)
- Customer fix (their side): unknown, likely 2 sprints if they need partner changes

Recommendation: Approve a 30-day extension, but give 7k tokens instead of 10k and offer sandbox provisioning (we can have it ready in 24 hours). That reduces promo spend while keeping them moving for the demo. I’ve attached a proposed provisioning timeline (integration_timeline.pdf).

—Marcus
Senior Software Engineer, Runtime
marcus.lin@redwood.com
Attachments: integration_timeline.pdf
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T15:25:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Approval request: Bridgeway trial extension & credits

Team —

Thanks for the context. I approve a one-time 30-day extension and a 10k token promo for Bridgeway AI (deal DW-1387) as a sales-led exception — we’ll treat this as a win to preserve momentum into the quarter close.

Please log the promo in HubSpot (link below), mark the promo reason as "integration blocker — partner SDK", and include the sandbox offer in the email to the customer. Marcus, thanks — if you can stand up the sandbox tomorrow that’s ideal. Ben, get a quick DocuSign cover if legal requires it (see PR-7882).

Laura Bennett
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com
Related: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/company/Bridgeway-AI
Approval doc: https://redwood.sharepoint.com/sites/Legal/Forms/PromoApprovals/PR-7882
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Pratt <jordan.pratt@bridgeway.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T16:00:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: trial extension and promo credits — SSO blocker

Jordan —

Good news: Laura approved a one-time 30-day extension and we’ve issued a 10k token promo voucher for your staging project. Marcus will provision a sandbox environment with adjusted rate limits (ETA: 24 hours) to avoid the concurrent token exchange failure. I’ve attached a short voucher stub (promo_voucher.pdf) — we’ll also log the promo in HubSpot under DW-1387 and follow up with a DocuSign cover if legal wants a quick signature.

Next steps:
- Marcus to provision sandbox in next 24 hours and share access
- Jordan to confirm reproduction steps and staging project ID so we can validate
- Ben to log the promo and confirm HubSpot/DocuSign links

Appreciate the quick collaboration — let me know if the sandbox timing works for you.

Best,

Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Attachments: promo_voucher.pdf (stub)

From: Jordan Pratt <jordan.pratt@bridgeway.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Request: trial extension and promo credits — SSO blocker

Ben —

Thanks for the product walkthrough last week. We started wiring up Redwood in our staging environment this morning and hit an SSO integration blocker: the partner SDK we rely on is returning a token exchange error during large-batch authentication (see attached error_log.txt). Our eng lead says it will take at least 2–3 sprints to get a proper fix into our release branch because the partner is prioritizing a different customer.

We’re at a hard deadline for a pilot demo to the executive committee on Dec 15 — if we can’t extend the sandbox window and get a small promo credit pool to cover continued testing, we’ll likely have to push the demo. That would push our go/no-go and (unfortunately) delay our purchase decision.

Request: 30-day trial extension + a temporary promo credit of 10k tokens for staging replay tests. Happy to provide more logs or jump on a call.

Appreciate any help —

Jordan Pratt
Senior Platform Engineer, Bridgeway AI
jordan.pratt@bridgeway.ai
Attachments: error_log.txt (text)

Summary:
- Customer: Bridgeway AI (platforming ML workloads for enterprise search)
- Problem: Partner SDK token exchange error in batched auth (error_log.txt attached by customer)
- Asks: 30-day extension; 10k token promo for staging tests
- Risk: If we don’t provide runway, they’ll postpone demonstration & delay decisioning

Options I see:
1) Approve 30-day extension + 10k promo (one-time exception) — fastest path to keep momentum
2) Offer 14-day extension + 5k promo + temporary sandbox (isolated env we can spin up) — lower spend, but might be insufficient for their testing cadence

Marcus — any quick read on how long this will take from an eng perspective and whether a temporary sandbox (limited endpoints + quota) is feasible this week?

Ben

Effort & risk:
- Engineering time (our side): low (sandbox provisioning and quota config ~ 3–5 hours)
- Customer fix (their side): unknown, likely 2 sprints if they need partner changes

Recommendation: Approve a 30-day extension, but give 7k tokens instead of 10k and offer sandbox provisioning (we can have it ready in 24 hours). That reduces promo spend while keeping them moving for the demo. I’ve attached a proposed provisioning timeline (integration_timeline.pdf).

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@northridge.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Supplier activation: PO formatting & issuance timeline

Hi Aisha —

Thanks for agreeing to kick off vendor activation for Northridge. To get procurement & finance ready on our side, please can you confirm the following items so we can generate the initial PO and complete vendor setup quickly:

1) Vendor profile & tax docs — please attach your W-9 (or relevant tax form) and primary vendor legal name/personal tax ID to match invoices.
2) Remit address and preferred invoice method (PDF via email, AP portal, EDI).
3) Billing contact (name + email) for PO-to-invoice routing.
4) Preferred PO line-item granularity — do you need SKU-level descriptions or is an aggregated license + support split acceptable? Examples below.
5) Payment terms you expect (Net 30/45/60).

I've attached our vendor_onboarding_form and a blank PO template to show the fields Finance requires (redwood_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf, redwood_po_template.xlsx). Our internal SLAs are: once vendor profile + tax docs are in the portal we typically issue the first PO within 3 business days (exceptions for new legal entities or custom contract terms).

Can you return the W-9 and confirm the remit address and preferred line-item format? If you can fill the onboarding form and return by EOD Thursday, we aim to have the initial PO out by Monday.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this live.

Best,
Ben Carter
Sr. Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0183

Attachments: redwood_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf, redwood_po_template.xlsx

From: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@northridge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@northridge.ai
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 13:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Supplier activation: PO formatting & issuance timeline

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the clear checklist. We're ready to proceed. Quick answers below:  

• Attaching our W-9 and vendor ID export (northridge_w9.pdf, northridge_vendor_ids.csv).
• Remit address / invoice method: we prefer PDF invoices via email to ap@northridge.ai; remit address is listed on the W-9.
• Billing contact: Priya Mehta (priya.mehta@northridge.ai).
• Payment terms: Net 45 is our standard for new vendors; we can accept Net 30 for volume-based agreements.
• Line-item granularity: For software we prefer two lines — (1) Product license (SKU + qty + unit price) and (2) Annual support (separate line). Implementation/one-time services we prefer as separate line items. We can accept consolidated license lines if you pass through SKU metadata in the invoice.

We have no issue with your 3 business day issuance target once the vendor record is active. I've attached the W-9 and a CSV with our internal vendor IDs. Let me know if you need the legal entity name spelled out differently.

Best,
Aisha Kapoor
Head of Procurement, Northridge Analytics

>

> 5) Payment terms you expect (Net 30/45/60).
>

Our internal SLAs are: once vendor profile + tax docs are in the portal we typically issue the first PO within 3 business days (exceptions for new legal entities or custom contract terms).
> 
> Can you return the W-9 and confirm the remit address and preferred line-item format? If you can fill the onboarding form and return by EOD Thursday, we aim to have the initial PO out by Monday.
> 
> Thanks — looking forward to getting this live.
> 
> Best,
> Ben
>

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@northridge.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 10:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Supplier activation: PO formatting & issuance timeline

Aisha —

Perfect, thanks for the quick turnaround and the attachments. A few confirmations from our side so there are no surprises during invoicing:  

• Billing contact to use for PO/invoice routing: Finance AP team <ap@redwood.ai>. Please also copy priya.mehta@northridge.ai on initial invoices if possible.
• Remit / legal name to use on the PO: Please confirm the full legal entity as it appears on the W-9; if it's "Northridge Analytics, Inc." we'll use that verbatim.
• Payment terms we will annotate on the PO as Net 45 per your standard. If you confirm Net 30 preference for this deal, call it out and we'll request approval from Finance.
• PO issuance & timeline: internal process is vendor record creation -> validation of tax docs -> PO generation. Expected timing: 3 business days after we receive completed vendor intake + tax docs. For urgency we can expedite to same-week if necessary but will need a signed SOW and exec approval.

Attached is a filled example of our PO template showing how we'll populate fields (redwood_po_template_filled.xlsx). Please confirm whether the two-line approach you suggested (product license + annual support) is sufficient for your AR team, or if you need SKU-level breakdowns per invoice.

Next steps: can you (1) confirm legal entity name exactly as on W-9, (2) confirm Priya Mehta as billing contact, and (3) tell us whether Net 45 is fine. Once we have those three items we'll kick vendor setup and issue the PO.

Thanks,
Ben

On Tue, Nov 17, 2026 at 01:46 PM Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@northridge.ai> wrote:
> Hi Ben —
> 
> Thanks for the clear checklist. We're ready to proceed. Quick answers below:  
> 
> • Attaching our W-9 and vendor ID export (northridge_w9.pdf, northridge_vendor_ids.csv).
> • Remit address / invoice method: we prefer PDF invoices via email to ap@northridge.ai; remit address is listed on the W-9.
> • Billing contact: Priya Mehta (priya.mehta@northridge.ai).
> • Payment terms: Net 45 is our standard for new vendors; we can accept Net 30 for volume-based agreements.
> • Line-item granularity: For software we prefer two lines — (1) Product license (SKU + qty + unit price) and (2) Annual support (separate line). Implementation/one-time services we prefer as separate line items. We can accept consolidated license lines if you pass through SKU metadata in the invoice.
> 
> We have no issue with your 3 business day issuance target once the vendor record is active. I've attached the W-9 and a CSV with our internal vendor IDs. Let me know if you need the legal entity name spelled out differently.
> 
> Best,
> Aisha

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 14:02:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: PO line-item structure guidance for vendor onboarding (Northridge)

Ben / Marcus —

Following Ben's thread with Northridge, a few internal notes for Finance & AP to follow when we create the PO and instruct the vendor on invoice formatting:  

Required line-item fields (must appear in the PO and be mirrored on invoices):
- item_code (SKU or internal code)
- description (clear text, include product name + any tier)
- quantity
- unit_price (USD)
- total_line_value
- start_date / end_date (for subscription lines)
- account_code (GL mapping)
- cost_center (if specific to the deal)
- tax_exempt flag (Y/N)

We should prefer the two-line approach Aisha recommended for product license + annual support. For implementation/one-time services, use a separate, clearly dated line with service period and attach the SOW number in the description. Invoice must include our PO number in the header; otherwise routing will be delayed.

If Northridge can provide SKU metadata on the invoice, AP will accept an aggregated license line as long as invoice attachments include the SKU breakout CSV. If they cannot, require separate SKU lines for items > $5,000.

I'm attaching the vendor intake checklist we use when handing to AP for new suppliers (vendor_intake_checklist.pdf). Ben — after Northridge confirms legal entity name and billing contact (Priya), Finance will kick off vendor creation and you can expect the PO within the 3 business day SLA. If PR/Legal raises any contract exceptions we'll escalate.

Laura Bennett
Head of Procurement Ops, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

Attachments: vendor_intake_checklist.pdf

Thanks — looking forward to getting this live.

Best,
Ben Carter
Sr. Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0183

Attachments: redwood_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf, redwood_po_template.xlsx

Subject: Re: Supplier activation: PO formatting & issuance timeline

Thanks for the clear checklist. We're ready to proceed. Quick answers below:  

• Attaching our W-9 and vendor ID export (northridge_w9.pdf, northridge_vendor_ids.csv).
• Remit address / invoice method: we prefer PDF invoices via email to ap@northridge.ai; remit address is listed on the W-9.
• Billing contact: Priya Mehta (priya.mehta@northridge.ai).
• Payment terms: Net 45 is our standard for new vendors; we can accept Net 30 for volume-based agreements.
• Line-item granularity: For software we prefer two lines — (1) Product license (SKU + qty + unit price) and (2) Annual support (separate line). Implementation/one-time services we prefer as separate line items. We can accept consolidated license lines if you pass through SKU metadata in the invoice.

We have no issue with your 3 business day issuance target once the vendor record is active. I've attached the W-9 and a CSV with our internal vendor IDs. Let me know if you need the legal entity name spelled out differently.

Best,
Aisha Kapoor

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 14:02:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: PO line-item structure guidance for vendor onboarding (Northridge)

Ben / Marcus —

Following Ben's thread with Northridge, a few internal notes for Finance & AP to follow when we create the PO and instruct the vendor on invoice formatting:  

Required line-item fields (must appear in the PO and be mirrored on invoices):
- item_code (SKU or internal code)
- description (clear text, include product name + any tier)
- quantity
- unit_price (USD)
- total_line_value
- start_date / end_date (for subscription lines)
- account_code (GL mapping)
- cost_center (if specific to the deal)
- tax_exempt flag (Y/N)

We should prefer the two-line approach Aisha recommended for product license + annual support. For implementation/one-time services, use a separate, clearly dated line with service period and attach the SOW number in the description. Invoice must include our PO number in the header; otherwise routing will be delayed.

If Northridge can provide SKU metadata on the invoice, AP will accept an aggregated license line as long as invoice attachments include the SKU breakout CSV. If they cannot, require separate SKU lines for items > $5,000.

I'm attaching the vendor intake checklist we use when handing to AP for new suppliers (vendor_intake_checklist.pdf). Ben — after Northridge confirms legal entity name and billing contact (Priya), Finance will kick off vendor creation and you can expect the PO within the 3 business day SLA. If PR/Legal raises any contract exceptions we'll escalate.

Laura Bennett
Head of Procurement Ops, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

Attachments: vendor_intake_checklist.pdf

source.subject: Private deployment residency — escrow, regional topology & staged failover proposal
messages:
From: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
To: aditya_rao@redwood.example.com
Cc: Satoshi Ito <ops+cloud@tokyo-retail.jp>
Date: 2026-11-12T09:03:00-05:00
Subject: Private deployment residency question

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for taking this on. We're lining up a pilot with Redwood and have two hard constraints from legal/compliance: EU-residency of all customer data (including logs/metrics/audit trails) and a desire to have APAC presence for latency/continuity — but only as a warm standby.

Two specific asks:
1) Can Redwood host a control plane and primary data stores in an EU-only VPC (customer-managed) while allowing a warm-standby in APAC that cannot be used for reads/writes except during an approved failover?
2) What does an "escrow" or escrow-like approach look like for metadata and audit logs (legal wants an auditable copy retained in EU and a separate encrypted mirror in APAC)?

I've attached our initial compliance questionnaire and a sketch of our topology. We also need rough RTO/RPO numbers for a warm failover.

Thanks — can we have an architecture review this week? I'm flexible.

Best,
Anna Müller
Head of Cloud Ops, EU-Tech
[Attachment: compliance-questionnaire.pdf (PDF)]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>
To: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>, Satoshi Ito <ops+cloud@tokyo-retail.jp>
Date: 2026-11-12T11:20:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Private deployment residency question

Anna — thanks, this is helpful context. Looping in Laura (solutions) and Marcus (infra) to validate an initial approach.

Short answers up front:
- Yes: we can run a customer-managed EU VPC for the control plane and primary stores.
- Warm standby in APAC is supported: cold-read-only replicas + KV cache warmers; failover gate controlled by your SRE + legal checklist.
- Escrow: we typically ship an encrypted, signed metadata bundle to an EU-resident key management container (KMS/HSM) and replicate a sealed copy to APAC; actual decryption keys remain EU-only unless your team authorizes key release.

I've attached a first-pass topology (eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf) with two candidate models (split-control vs mirrored-control). Laura/Marcus — could you review the attached and call out network prerequisites (peering, allowed egress destinations, policy for cross-border logging)? Also: legal asked about log retention/immutable copies — Laura, can you propose where we'd store immutable audit blobs (S3 in EU vs on-prem vault)?

Proposed next step: 30-min architecture sync (we can do Mon/Tue next week).

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf (PDF)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>
Cc: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Date: 2026-11-13T09:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Private deployment residency question

On Fri, Nov 12, 2026 at 11:20 AM Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com> wrote:
> I've attached a first-pass topology (eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf) with two candidate models (split-control vs mirrored-control).

Thanks — reviewed the sketch and sketched a third hybrid approach in the attached draft. High level recommendation (short):

- Control plane & primary data: EU-only VPC (customer-managed). All sensitive artifacts (PII, logs, audit trails, KMS keys) live in EU accounts.
- APAC presence: warm-standby clusters with encrypted replicas. Replicas run with keys sealed (cannot be unlocked/decrypted without an EU-based key release event).
- Escrow pattern: produce periodic signed metadata bundles (shards + manifest + sig) stored in EU immutable store (S3 object lock + separate retention bucket). A second sealed copy is replicated to APAC for resilience but remains cryptographically sealed.
- Network: no direct cross-border writes. Use one-way replication channels (push only) from EU to APAC using TLS mutual auth. BGP/eBGP for failover is risky across providers — prefer DNS-based weighted failover + health checks with feature flags in app layer.
- Failover model: staged failover: (1) detection + verification, (2) legal approval & key unseal, (3) promotion of APAC replicas to primary for a bounded window, (4) controlled rollback plan. Estimated RPO: ~15 minutes for state replication (depends on topology), RTO: 30-90 minutes depending on manual approvals.

Questions for Marcus: can we ensure stable egress IPs from EU VPCs for compliance allowlisting? Also confirm whether we can run S3-compatible immutable buckets in EU with object-lock in your proposed infra.

I've attached the draft runbook and the network ACL matrix. If this looks good, Aditya — let's present an option matrix to Anna and pick the legal-approved flow.

--
Laura Bennett
Principal Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: regional-failover-runbook-draft.md (MD)]
[Attachment: network-acls-and-egress-table.csv (CSV)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>
Cc: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Date: 2026-11-15T14:00:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Private deployment residency question — infra confirmation

Thanks — short confirmation and a few specifics: 

- Stable egress: yes, we can offer fixed egress IPs from customer-managed EU VPCs via NAT gateways or customer-managed egress appliances; will call out costs and HA topology in the final doc.
- Immutable storage: EU S3 with object-lock + separate retention account is feasible; we recommend a write-once retention bucket in the customer's EU account (we provide bootstrapping templates).
- Replication: we'll use a push model from EU -> APAC with mutual TLS, no cross-region write endpoints. Replication lag estimated < 2 minutes for metadata shards, ~15 minutes for larger payloads depending on bandwidth.
- Routing & failover: prefer DNS weighted routing + health checks; can automate partial failover via feature-flag/traffic-shift playbook. Full BGP failover is only for certain network partners and would add complexity and potential data egress risk.
- Security gating: key unseal requires a multi-signer approval (legal + ops + customer SRE). We can integrate with your KMS (customer-managed HSM) or provide an EU-resident managed HSM.

Runbook highlights (attached):
- Canary promotion steps + smoke tests (automatic): /health, token-generation, sample inference checks.
- Manual approval checklist: legal acknowledgment, incident ticket, timeframe, rollback window.
- Estimated RTO/RPO: target RPO=15m, RTO=45-90m (depends on approval speed).

Next steps I propose:
1) Lock topology option (split-control vs hybrid) — Laura to annotate which option meets legal.
2) Sign off on egress IP model and pull costs.
3) Schedule an architecture review with the customer and legal.

Marcus Lin
Staff Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: regional-failover-runbook-draft.md (updated)]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>
To: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>, legal-procure@nordicbank.se
Date: 2026-11-18T16:45:00-08:00
Subject: Proposed approach & next steps — EU primary / APAC warm-standby

Anna — thanks again for the initial ask and for looping legal. Laura and Marcus validated a practical approach that balances legal constraints and operational readiness. Summary for your review: 

- Topology (recommended): Hybrid — EU control plane + primary stores in your EU VPC; APAC warm-standby clusters with sealed encrypted replicas; no cross-border writes.
- Escrow / immutable logs: signed metadata bundles stored in EU object-lock buckets; sealed replica in APAC for resilience. Customer-managed KMS in EU or Redwood-managed EU HSM are both options.
- Failover: staged process requiring legal + ops approval to unseal keys; DNS-weighted traffic shift; canary promotion with smoke tests.
- RPO / RTO targets: RPO ~15 minutes, RTO ~45-90 minutes depending on approval cadence.

Attachments: eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf, regional-failover-runbook-draft.md, network-acls-and-egress-table.csv. Also adding links to our internal runbook (https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-2314) and the draft HubSpot mutual action plan (https://app.hubspot.com/deal/NB-PILOT-2026-11).

Would you be available for a 30-minute review on Monday 2026-11-22 or Tuesday 2026-11-23? If legal wants to be present, happy to include them.

If this looks good, I can convert Laura's recommendation into a formal options matrix and a one-page compliance summary for your legal team.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf (PDF)]
[Attachment: regional-failover-runbook-draft.md (MD)]
[Attachment: network-acls-and-egress-table.csv (CSV)]

Thanks — reviewed the sketch and sketched a third hybrid approach in the attached draft. High level recommendation (short):

- Control plane & primary data: EU-only VPC (customer-managed). All sensitive artifacts (PII, logs, audit trails, KMS keys) live in EU accounts.
- APAC presence: warm-standby clusters with encrypted replicas. Replicas run with keys sealed (cannot be unlocked/decrypted without an EU-based key release event).
- Escrow pattern: produce periodic signed metadata bundles (shards + manifest + sig) stored in EU immutable store (S3 object lock + separate retention bucket). A second sealed copy is replicated to APAC for resilience but remains cryptographically sealed.
- Network: no direct cross-border writes. Use one-way replication channels (push only) from EU to APAC using TLS mutual auth. BGP/eBGP for failover is risky across providers — prefer DNS-based weighted failover + health checks with feature flags in app layer.
- Failover model: staged failover: (1) detection + verification, (2) legal approval & key unseal, (3) promotion of APAC replicas to primary for a bounded window, (4) controlled rollback plan. Estimated RPO: ~15 minutes for state replication (depends on topology), RTO: 30-90 minutes depending on manual approvals.

Questions for Marcus: can we ensure stable egress IPs from EU VPCs for compliance allowlisting? Also confirm whether we can run S3-compatible immutable buckets in EU with object-lock in your proposed infra.

I've attached the draft runbook and the network ACL matrix. If this looks good, Aditya — let's present an option matrix to Anna and pick the legal-approved flow.

Thanks — short confirmation and a few specifics: 

- Stable egress: yes, we can offer fixed egress IPs from customer-managed EU VPCs via NAT gateways or customer-managed egress appliances; will call out costs and HA topology in the final doc.
- Immutable storage: EU S3 with object-lock + separate retention account is feasible; we recommend a write-once retention bucket in the customer's EU account (we provide bootstrapping templates).
- Replication: we'll use a push model from EU -> APAC with mutual TLS, no cross-region write endpoints. Replication lag estimated < 2 minutes for metadata shards, ~15 minutes for larger payloads depending on bandwidth.
- Routing & failover: prefer DNS weighted routing + health checks; can automate partial failover via feature-flag/traffic-shift playbook. Full BGP failover is only for certain network partners and would add complexity and potential data egress risk.
- Security gating: key unseal requires a multi-signer approval (legal + ops + customer SRE). We can integrate with your KMS (customer-managed HSM) or provide an EU-resident managed HSM.
